ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pro.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_spider_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_spider_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_incline_.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_good_morning.description: Sit tall on a bench with dumbbells held at the shoulders or against the upper back. Brace, hinge forward from the hips, then return to tall sitting. Keep the spine long and avoid rounding the low back to get lower.

ff_bodyweight_hip_thrust.description: Set the upper back on a bench with both feet planted about hip width. Tuck the ribs, drive through the heel, and lift the hips until the torso is level. Lower under control and avoid arching the low back at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_box_pist.description: Sit to a box on one leg with the kettlebells held in the front rack. Keep the free leg forward, tap the box with control, then stand through the whole working foot. Avoid dropping to the box or letting the knee cave inward.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_incl.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_incline.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_incline_bench_p.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_ring_side_plank_reach_through.description: Place the lower forearm in a ring and stack the feet or stagger them for balance in a side plank. Reach the top arm under the ribs, then rotate back open while keeping the hips lifted. Move through the upper back and avoid letting the shoulder collapse into the ring.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the side opposite the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

Set a split stance and hold the load overhead. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_ipsila.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the bottoms-up front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the bottoms-up front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

Stand in front of a box with the weight in the bottoms-up front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_kettlebell_half_kneeling_low_to_high_.description: Kneel with one knee down and hold the kettlebell near the outside hip of the down-knee side. Brace and guide the weight diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder. Let the torso rotate only as far as you can control, avoiding a loose lower-back twist.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_windmill.description: Press the kettlebell overhead and turn the feet slightly away from the loaded arm. Keep eyes on the bell as you push the hips back and reach the free hand toward the inside foot. Stand back up with the bell stacked over the shoulder, avoiding a bent elbow or rounded back.

ff_kettlebell_russian_twist.description: Sit with knees bent and hold the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_pla.description: Anchor the band low behind you and stand facing away with the band between the legs. Hinge back until the hips reach behind you, then drive the hips forward to stand. Keep the arms as hooks and avoid turning it into a squat.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_single_leg_stan.description: Stand on one leg with a soft knee and hold the kettlebell in one hand. Pass or circle the bell around the body while the stance knee stays bent and the torso stays tall. Move slowly and avoid letting the hip drop or the foot wobble wildly.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_swing.description: Stand with one kettlebell just in front of you and grip it with one hand. Hike the load back, snap the hips forward, and let it float to about chest height. Keep the arm relaxed and avoid squatting or lifting with the shoulder.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_feet_eleva.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_spider_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_bench_pr.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_deadli.description: Stand beside the kettlebell with feet hip width and the bell just outside one foot. Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle, then stand tall by driving through the floor. Keep the shoulders level and avoid twisting toward the loaded side.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean.description: Set the kettlebell between the feet and hinge with the chest over the load. Drive the hips forward and guide the bell close into the front rack. Keep the elbow path tight and avoid flipping the bell hard over the wrist.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_overhe.description: Start with the kettlebell between the feet and hinge to grip it. Drive the hips, clean the bell to the front rack, then press it overhead after the body is still. Lower to the rack with control and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_pendl.description: Hinge until the torso is nearly parallel to the floor and let the dumbbell start from the floor each rep. Brace, pull one side at a time toward the lower ribs, then return it to the floor. Keep the back set and avoid bouncing the weight with the hips.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_snatch.description: Set the kettlebell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the kettlebell close to the body into an overhead lockout. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_kettlebell_sumo_deadlift.description: Stand wide with toes turned slightly out and the kettlebell between the feet. Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle, then stand tall by driving through the floor. Keep the shoulders level and avoid twisting toward the loaded side.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_turkish_get_up.description: Lie on your back with the kettlebell pressed over one shoulder and the same-side knee bent. Roll to the elbow, come to the hand, sweep the leg under, then stand while keeping the bell vertical. Reverse the same path and avoid taking your eyes off the bell during transitions.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_squat.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height with feet about shoulder width. Sit the hips down between the heels, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_squat.description: Hold the kettlebell overhead with the arm locked and feet about shoulder width. Brace, squat as low as you can while the bell stays stacked over the shoulder, then stand. Keep eyes forward and avoid letting the weight drift behind you.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_benc.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_turkish_sit_up.description: Lie on your back with one kettlebell pressed over the shoulder and the same-side knee bent. Brace and sit up until the torso is tall while keeping the bell or bells stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the weight drift behind the shoulder.

ff_double_kettlebell_turkish_sit_up.description: Lie on your back with both kettlebells pressed over the chest and knees slightly bent. Brace and sit up until the torso is tall while keeping the bell or bells stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the weight drift behind the shoulder.

ff_double_kettlebell_turkish_sit_up_to_z.description: Sit tall with legs straight and two kettlebells held in the front rack. Roll down only as far as you can control, sit back up, then press both bells overhead from the Z-sit. Keep the ribs down during the press and avoid using leg momentum.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_swing.description: Stand with the kettlebell or kettlebells just in front of you. Hike the load back, snap the hips forward, and let it float to about chest height. Keep the arm relaxed and avoid squatting or lifting with the shoulder.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_squat.description: Hold the load in the front rack with feet about shoulder width. Brace, squat between the heels, and stand through the whole foot. Keep the elbows lifted and avoid folding forward as you come out of the bottom.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_squa.description: Hold the load in the front rack with feet about shoulder width. Brace, squat between the heels, and stand through the whole foot. Keep the elbows lifted and avoid folding forward as you come out of the bottom.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_carr.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_squat_clean.description: Set the kettlebell between the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the floor, guide the bell close, and receive in the front rack as you drop into a squat. Stand tall before resetting, avoiding a hard forearm slap.

ff_double_kettlebell_prone_row.description: Lie chest-down on a flat or slightly inclined bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold the kettlebells locked out overhead. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the kettlebell in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_prone_r.description: Lie chest-down on a flat or slightly inclined bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_single_leg_romanian.description: Stand on one leg holding the kettlebells with a soft knee. Hinge by sending the free leg back and lowering the load toward the floor. Keep the hips square and avoid opening the pelvis to chase more depth.

ff_double_kettlebell_romanian_deadlift.description: Stand tall with the kettlebells in front of the thighs. Soften the knees, push the hips back, and lower until the hamstrings are loaded. Stand by driving the hips forward and avoid rounding the back for extra range.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_contralateral_s.description: Stand on one leg holding the kettlebell in the hand opposite the stance leg with a soft knee. Hinge by sending the free leg back and lowering the load toward the floor. Keep the hips square and avoid opening the pelvis to chase more depth.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Hold the kettlebell bottoms-up overhead with the arm locked and feet about shoulder width. Brace, squat as low as you can while the bell stays stacked over the shoulder, then stand. Keep eyes forward and avoid letting the weight drift behind you.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_prone_row.description: Lie chest-down on a flat or slightly inclined bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_parallette_tuck_planche.description: Grip the parallettes and lean the shoulders forward past the hands with elbows locked. Tuck the knees toward the chest and lift the feet as you press the floor away. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid jumping into a bent-elbow hold.

ff_macebell_offset_bicep_curl.description: Stand tall holding the weight with the working hand down by the thighs. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid leaning back or swinging the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_decline_.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_tuck_planche.description: Start in an L-sit on parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean the shoulders forward, and shift into a tuck planche before returning with control. Keep the elbows locked and avoid dropping the feet between positions.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_landmine_standing_oblique_twist.description: Stand holding the end of a landmine bar with both hands at chest height. Brace, then arc the bar from one hip to the other by rotating the ribs and shoulders. Pivot the feet as needed and avoid letting the bar pull the low back into a twist.

ff_cable_rope_tall_kneeling_high_to_low_.description: Stand tall and hold the kettlebell near one hip. Brace and guide the weight diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder. Let the torso rotate only as far as you can control, avoiding a loose lower-back twist.

ff_single_arm_parallette_plank.description: Set one hand on a parallette and the other hand on the floor, then step back to a high plank. Brace the abs and squeeze the glutes as you hold the body in one line. Keep the hips level instead of leaning or twisting toward the supported side.

ff_single_arm_suspension_plank.description: Set one hand in the suspension handle and the other hand on the floor, then step back to a high plank. Brace the abs and squeeze the glutes as you hold the body in one line. Keep the hips level instead of leaning or twisting toward the supported side.

ff_battle_rope_russian_twist.description: Sit tall with knees bent and both battle rope ends held together near the chest. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the ribs and shoulders to move the ropes from side to side. Keep the chest lifted and avoid yanking with only the arms or rounding the low back.

ff_bodyweight_bird_dog_knee_to_elbow.description: Start on hands and knees with hands under shoulders and knees under hips. Reach one arm and the opposite leg long, then draw the elbow and knee toward each other under the body. Move slowly and keep the pelvis level instead of twisting or arching the back.

ff_slam_ball_feet_elevated_russian_twist.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_medicine_ball_feet_elevated_russian_t.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_prone_row.description: Lie chest-down on a flat or slightly inclined bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_bodyweight_forearm_plank_alternating_.description: Start in a forearm plank with elbows under shoulders and legs straight. Bring one knee toward the same-side elbow, return to plank, and alternate sides. Keep the hips low and quiet instead of rocking side to side with each knee drive.

ff_bodyweight_loaded_beast_to_alternatin.description: Start on hands and feet with knees hovering under the hips, then sit the hips slightly back into loaded beast. Shift forward, lift one hand and opposite foot, and thread the leg under into a kick-through. Return to center and alternate, keeping the shoulders packed instead of collapsing.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_prone_row.description: Lie chest-down on a flat or slightly inclined bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean.description: Set the kettlebells between the feet and hinge with the chest over the load. Drive the hips forward and guide the bells close into the front rack. Keep the elbow path tight and avoid flipping the bell hard over the wrist.

ff_double_kettlebell_snatch.description: Set the kettlebell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the kettlebell close to the body into an overhead lockout. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_battle_rope_feet_elevated_russian_twi.description: Sit with knees bent, heels lightly raised, and both battle rope ends held together near the chest. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the ribs and shoulders to move the ropes from side to side. Keep the chest lifted and avoid yanking with only the arms or rounding the low back.

ff_superband_dead_bug.description: Lie on your back holding a band anchored behind you with arms reaching toward the ceiling. Brace the ribs down, then slowly lower one leg while keeping tension on the band. Return and switch sides, keeping the low back from popping off the floor.

ff_parallette_v_sit.description: Sit between parallettes with hands gripping the handles beside the hips. Press down, lift the hips, and raise both legs into a V while keeping the chest tall. Keep elbows locked and avoid rounding the back to force the legs higher.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_pendlay_row.description: Hinge until the torso is nearly parallel to the floor and let the dumbbell start from the floor each rep. Brace, pull the load toward the lower ribs, then return it to the floor. Keep the back set and avoid bouncing the weight with the hips.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_decl.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_kettlebell_split_jerk.description: Clean the kettlebells to the front rack with feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive hard through the legs, and split the feet as the bells lock out overhead. Recover one foot at a time and avoid pressing the bells out slowly.

ff_double_kettlebell_high_pull.description: Start with the kettlebells between the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive the hips like a swing, then pull the elbows high as the bells rise close to the body. Keep the wrists neutral and avoid muscling the lift with rounded shoulders.

ff_superband_pallof_press.description: Set a band at chest height and stand side-on to the anchor. Hold the band at the sternum, brace, and press the hands straight forward. Pause without rotating, then return to the chest under control.

ff_superband_half_kneeling_pallof_press.description: Set a band at chest height and kneel with the inside knee down, side-on to the anchor. Hold the band at the sternum, brace, and press the hands straight forward. Pause without rotating, then return to the chest under control.

ff_superband_single_leg_standing_bent_kn.description: Stand on one leg with the band creating light resistance and the working knee bent. Keep the thigh lifted, rotate the hip outward, then return slowly. Stay tall through the stance leg and avoid twisting the trunk to move the band.

ff_macebell_reverse_grip_offset_bicep_cu.description: Stand tall holding the weight with the working hand down by the thighs. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid leaning back or swinging the load.

ff_ring_archer_row.description: Hold the rings and lean back with the body in a straight line. Pull mostly to one side while the other arm stays straighter, then lower and repeat or switch sides. Keep the hips lifted and avoid twisting the torso to reach the ring.

ff_single_arm_landmine_row_to_rotational.description: Stand in a staggered stance holding the end of the landmine bar in one hand. Row the bar toward the ribs, rotate through the hips, then press it up and forward. Keep the ribs braced and avoid turning the row into a low-back twist.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_skater_squat.description: Stand on one leg with the free knee bent behind you and use the hands lightly for balance. Sit the hips back and lower the back knee toward the floor, then drive through the standing foot to rise. Keep the pelvis level and avoid bouncing off the back knee.

ff_superband_pull_through.description: Anchor the band low behind you and stand facing away with the band between the legs. Hinge back until the hips reach behind you, then drive the hips forward to stand. Keep the arms as hooks and avoid turning it into a squat.

ff_miniband_quadruped_hip_extension.description: Start on hands and knees with a miniband around the thighs and one knee bent. Brace, then press the working foot up and back without changing the low-back position. Lower with control and avoid rotating the hips open.

ff_cable_hip_adduction.description: Attach the cable low to the inside ankle and stand side-on to the stack. Sweep the working leg across the body, then return slowly until you feel the cable pull. Keep the torso tall and avoid leaning to create range.

ff_superband_tall_kneeling_pallof_press.description: Set a band at chest height and kneel tall with both knees down, side-on to the anchor. Hold the band at the sternum, brace, and press the hands straight forward. Pause without rotating, then return to the chest under control.

ff_slider_eccentric_hamstring_curl.description: Lie on your back with both heels on sliders and hips lifted. Start with the knee bent, then slide the heel away slowly while keeping the hips up. Reset with help as needed and avoid letting the hips drop during the lowering phase.

ff_landmine_low_hold_lateral_lunge.description: Hold the landmine low at the chest or inside shoulder and stand tall. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, then push the floor away to return. Keep the opposite leg long and avoid letting the working knee dive inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_sumo_deadlift.description: Stand wide with toes turned slightly out and the kettlebell between the feet. Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle, then stand tall by driving through the floor. Keep the shoulders level and avoid twisting toward the loaded side.

ff_slider_kneeling_reach_push_up.description: Start in a kneeling push-up with one hand on a slider. As you bend the elbows, slide the slider hand forward into a reach, then pull it back as you press up. Keep the ribs braced and avoid letting the low back sag.

ff_slider_parallette_knee_tuck.description: Grip the parallettes and set the feet on sliders behind you in a high plank. Brace, slide the knees toward the chest, then extend the legs back out. Keep the shoulders over the hands and avoid piking the hips high.

ff_superband_shoulder_dislocates.description: Stand tall holding a light band wider than shoulder width. Keep the elbows straight and move the band in a wide arc from the thighs toward the back side. Use a wide enough grip that the ribs stay down and the shoulders do not pinch.

ff_superband_alternating_shoulder_disloc.description: Stand tall holding a light band wider than shoulder width. Keep the elbows straight and move the band in a wide arc from the thighs toward the back side, leading one arm at a time. Use a wide enough grip that the ribs stay down and the shoulders do not pinch.

ff_superband_prone_shoulder_dislocates.description: Lie face-down holding a light band wider than shoulder width. Keep the elbows straight and move the band in a wide arc from the thighs toward the back side. Use a wide enough grip that the ribs stay down and the shoulders do not pinch.

ff_ring_l_sit.description: Hold the rings at your sides and support your body with straight arms, then extend both legs forward. Push the shoulders down and keep the ribs stacked as you hold. Keep the rings close to your body and avoid bending the elbows or swinging.

ff_resistance_band_prone_shoulder_disloc.description: Lie face-down holding a light band wider than shoulder width. Keep the elbows straight and move the band in a wide arc from the thighs toward the back side. Use a wide enough grip that the ribs stay down and the shoulders do not pinch.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_shoulder_.description: Stand tall holding a light band wider than shoulder width. Keep the elbows straight and move the band in a wide arc from the thighs toward the back side, leading one arm at a time. Use a wide enough grip that the ribs stay down and the shoulders do not pinch.

ff_cable_straight_bar_spider_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench facing a low cable with the bar in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold the load in the front rack with feet about shoulder width. Brace, squat between the heels, and stand through the whole foot. Keep the elbows lifted and avoid folding forward as you come out of the bottom.

ff_barbell_back_rack_cyclist_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close, holding the kettlebell in the named position. Sit the knees forward and down while keeping the torso tall, then stand through the midfoot. Keep the knees tracking over the toes and avoid rocking onto the toes.

ff_gravity_boots_inverted_sit_up.description: Secure the gravity boots and hang inverted with hands across the chest or near the temples. Curl the ribs toward the pelvis to sit up, then lower under control. Keep the movement smooth and avoid pulling on the head or swinging.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_knee_ove.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_knee_over_.description: Set a split stance and hold the load overhead. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the back rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_barbell_front_rack_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_barbell_standing_rollout.description: Stand holding a barbell on the floor with hands about shoulder width. Brace hard and roll the bar forward only as far as you can keep the ribs down. Pull the bar back under the shoulders and avoid sagging the hips.

ff_stability_ball_cable_russian_twist.description: Sit with knees bent and hold the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_miniband_frog_pump.description: Lie on your back with knees open, soles of the feet together, and the miniband above the knees. Tuck the ribs, press the feet together, and lift the hips. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back instead of squeezing the glutes.

ff_miniband_knee_hover_quadruped_fire_hy.description: Start on hands and knees with the knees hovering an inch above the floor. Keeping the knee bent, lift one thigh out to the side, then return with control. Keep the pelvis square and avoid leaning away from the working hip.

ff_bodyweight_fire_hydrant.description: Start on hands and knees with the back flat. Keeping the knee bent, lift one thigh out to the side, then return with control. Keep the pelvis square and avoid leaning away from the working hip.

ff_bodyweight_knee_hover_quadruped_fire_.description: Start on hands and knees with the knees hovering an inch above the floor. Keeping the knee bent, lift one thigh out to the side, then return with control. Keep the pelvis square and avoid leaning away from the working hip.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_spider_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_spider_cu.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_bodyweight_bent_knee_copenhagen_plank.description: Place the top knee on a bench and support yourself on the lower forearm in a side plank. Lift the hips and bring the lower leg up toward the bench, then lower and raise the hips with control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_bodyweight_kneeling_side_plank.description: Set the body in the plank or hold shape named by the exercise and brace the ribs down. Hold steady while breathing quietly or add the limb motion listed. Avoid letting the hips or shoulders sag.

ff_bodyweight_wall_assisted_hip_airplane.description: Stand on one leg with fingertips on a wall for balance and hinge slightly at the hip. Rotate the pelvis open and closed over the standing leg while the spine stays long. Move slowly and avoid letting the knee collapse inward.

ff_superband_single_leg_tibialis_raise.description: Anchor the band in front of you and loop it over the forefoot while standing on one leg or sitting tall. Pull the toes toward the shin against the band, then lower slowly. Keep the knee still and avoid curling the toes to fake the motion.

ff_barbell_zercher_squat.description: Hold the bar in the crooks of the elbows with hands clasped and feet about shoulder width. Brace, squat between the heels, and stand while keeping the bar close to the torso. Keep the elbows lifted and avoid rounding forward under the bar.

ff_barbell_squat_snatch.description: Set the barbell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the barbell close to the body into an overhead lockout as you receive it in a squat. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_spider_.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_split_jerk.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull the bar close, receive it in a front squat, stand, then dip and drive into a split jerk overhead. Keep the bar close and recover the feet before lowering or resetting.

ff_slider_kneeling_alternating_reach_pus.description: Set up in the listed push-up position with hands on the floor or sliders. Lower the chest, press back up, and reach one hand forward or to the side without rotating the hips. Keep the body in one line through each reach.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_thruster.description: Hold the bar at mid-thigh and hinge slightly to start. Clean the bar to the front rack, receive in a squat, then stand directly into an overhead press. Keep the elbows high in the squat and avoid pressing before the legs finish driving.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_thruster.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Clean the bar to the front rack, receive in a squat, then stand directly into an overhead press. Keep the elbows high in the squat and avoid pressing before the legs finish driving.

ff_bodyweight_copenhagen_plank.description: Place the top foot or ankle on a bench and support yourself on the lower forearm in a side plank. Lift the hips and bring the lower leg up toward the bench. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_bodyweight_bent_knee_copenhagen_plank.description: Place the top knee on a bench and support yourself on the lower forearm in a side plank. Lift the hips and bring the lower leg up toward the bench. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_pullover.description: Stand tall holding the clubbell in one hand near the shoulder. Guide it in a controlled inside arc around the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_miniband_single_leg_hip_thrust.description: Set the upper back on a bench with one foot planted and the band around the thighs. Tuck the ribs, drive through the heel, and lift the hips until the torso is level. Lower under control and avoid arching the low back at the top.

ff_barbell_deficit_snatch_grip_deadlift.description: Stand on a small platform with a wide snatch grip on the bar. Soften the knees, push the hips back, and lower until the hamstrings are loaded. Stand by driving the hips forward and avoid rounding the back for extra range.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_outside_pullover.description: Stand tall holding the clubbell in one hand near the shoulder. Guide it in a controlled outside arc around the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_foot_elevate.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bodyweight_foot_elevated_knee_over_to.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_thruster.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and set the feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_double_kettlebell_cossack_squat_thrus.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and set the feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_double_kettlebell_alternating_cossack.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and set the feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_landmine_shrug.description: Stand beside the landmine and hold the sleeve in one hand with the arm straight. Let the shoulder blade lower, then shrug it up toward the ear without bending the elbow. Pause briefly and avoid leaning the torso to lift the bar.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_walking_.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_walking_lu.description: Hold the kettlebells locked out overhead. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_plate_seated_steering_wheel.description: Sit tall holding a plate in front of the chest with elbows slightly bent. Brace and rotate the plate like a steering wheel from side to side without leaning back. Keep the shoulders down and avoid rushing through a range you cannot control.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_handstand.description: Begin in an L-sit on parallettes with straight arms and shoulders pressed down. Tuck the knees, lean forward, and press the hips upward toward a handstand line. Move in small controlled ranges and avoid kicking past balance with bent elbows.

ff_ring_straddle_l_sit.description: Hold the rings at your sides and support your body with straight arms, then open the legs into a straddle. Push the shoulders down and keep the ribs stacked as you hold. Keep the rings close to your body and avoid bending the elbows or swinging.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_overhead_pre.description: Stand tall with two kettlebells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_kneeling_seesa.description: Kneel with one knee down and hold two kettlebells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_b.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_i.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_d.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_f.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_seesaw_.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_double_dumbbell_half_kneeling_seesaw_.description: Kneel with one knee down and hold two kettlebells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_z_press.description: Sit tall on the floor with legs straight and two dumbbells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_stability_ball_sit_up.description: Lie back over a stability ball with feet planted and hands across the chest. Curl the ribs toward the pelvis to sit up, then lower under control. Keep the movement smooth and avoid pulling on the head or swinging.

ff_ez_bar_wide_grip_strict_bicep_curl.description: Stand tall holding the EZ bar with the working hand down by the thighs. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid leaning back or swinging the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_squat_clean.description: Set the kettlebells between the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the floor, guide the bells close, and receive in the front rack as you drop into a squat. Stand tall before resetting, avoiding a hard forearm slap.

ff_double_kettlebell_hollow_body_hold.description: Lie on your back holding the kettlebells locked out over the chest. Curl the shoulders up and extend the legs until the body forms a shallow banana shape. Keep the low back heavy on the floor and raise the legs if the back starts to arch.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_bodyweight_side_lying_hip_adduction.description: Attach the cable low to the inside ankle and stand side-on to the stack. Sweep the working leg across the body, then return slowly until you feel the cable pull. Keep the torso tall and avoid leaning to create range.

ff_superband_hollow_body_hold.description: Lie on your back with the band tensioned and arms reaching overhead or toward the anchor. Curl the shoulders up and extend the legs until the body forms a shallow banana shape. Keep the low back heavy on the floor and raise the legs if the back starts to arch.

ff_parallette_freestanding_handstand_pus.description: Set the parallettes shoulder width and kick or press to a stable freestanding handstand. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the handles, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body stacked over the hands and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

ff_slider_single_leg_eccentric_hamstring.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a slider and the other leg held up. Start with the knee bent, then slide the heel away slowly while keeping the hips up. Reset with help as needed and avoid letting the hips drop during the lowering phase.

ff_bodyweight_freestanding_handstand_pus.description: Place the hands shoulder width on the floor and kick or press into a stacked handstand. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep ribs tucked and elbows tracking under the hands instead of flaring or arching the low back.

ff_single_arm_ring_grip_push_up.description: Hold the rings under the shoulders in a plank with straps vertical. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this single-arm-ring-grip-push-up version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_superband_side_plank_hanging_hip_addu.description: Anchor a superband above foot height and loop it around the top leg, then set up in a side plank on the forearm. Keep the body long, lift the banded leg slightly, and draw it down toward the lower leg before returning. Keep the hips stacked and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_ring_strap_bulgarian_handstand_push_u.description: Set the rings low enough to control them and kick or press into your handstand with the straps vertical. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep ribs tucked and elbows tracking under the hands instead of flaring or arching the low back. In this ring-strap-bulgarian-handstand-push-up version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_ring_freestanding_handstand_push_up.description: Set the rings low enough to control them and kick or press into your handstand with the straps vertical. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep ribs tucked and elbows tracking under the hands instead of flaring or arching the low back. In this ring-freestanding-handstand-push-up version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_stability_ball_feet_elevated_frog_pum.description: Lie on your back with soles of the feet together, knees opened out, and feet elevated on the stability ball. Tuck the ribs down, press the edges of the feet into the ball, and lift the hips until the body forms a short bridge. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back to gain height.

ff_double_kettlebell_hollow_body_flutter.description: Lie on your back and hold two kettlebells locked over the chest while the legs extend long. Lift the shoulders slightly, brace into a hollow position, and alternate small up-and-down kicks. Keep the low back heavy on the floor instead of letting the weight pull the ribs open.

ff_barbell_overhead_cyclist_squat.description: Set the heels on a wedge or plates with feet narrow, then press the barbell overhead before you descend. Keep the arms locked, ribs down, and knees tracking forward as you squat between the heels, then stand through the whole foot. Do not let the bar drift away from the midfoot or chase depth by arching the back.

ff_barbell_front_rack_cyclist_squat.description: Set the heels on a wedge or plates with feet narrow and the barbell in a front rack with elbows high. Bend the knees forward and squat between the heels, then stand tall without dropping the elbows. Keep the torso upright and avoid letting the heels roll off the support.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_push_.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor between or just inside the feet, then pull it close as the hips drive. Catch the bell softly in the rack. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_bodyweight_bent_arm_press_handstand.description: Start with hands planted, hips piked, and shoulders leaning forward so the hands can support your weight. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press. In this bodyweight-bent-arm-press-handstand version, use only the range you can control.

ff_barbell_cyclist_thruster.description: Stand narrow with heels elevated and the load in the rack position. Squat down, then drive up and continue directly into an overhead press. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip. In this barbell-cyclist-thruster version, keep the bar centered over the midfoot.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_bent_arm_press_ha.description: Start in a strong L-sit on the parallettes or rings with shoulders pushed down and legs lifted. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press. In this parallette-l-sit-to-bent-arm-press-handstand version, press evenly through both handles.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_thrus.description: Stand with the load in the rack position and feet about shoulder width. Lower into the squat or split squat, then drive out of the squat and press the landmine up along its angled path. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_cyclist_thruster.description: Stand narrow with heels elevated and the load in the rack position. Squat down, then drive up and continue directly into an overhead press. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip. In this single-arm-dumbbell-cyclist-thruster version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_cycli.description: Stand narrow with heels elevated and the load in the rack position. Lower into the squat or split squat, then drive out of the squat and press the landmine up along its angled path. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip.

ff_barbell_low_bar_cyclist_squat.description: Set the heels on a wedge or plates with feet narrow and pin the bar low across the rear shoulders. Bend the knees forward as the hips sit down, then drive through the midfoot to stand. Pull the bar into the back and keep the knees tracking over the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_split_sq.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_bulgaria.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this front-rack-bulgarian-split-squat version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_squat.description: Clean or snatch two kettlebells overhead, then stand with feet about shoulder width. Keep both arms vertical and ribs down as you squat, then stand with the bells stacked over the shoulders. Use a range where the bells stay over the midfoot instead of leaning forward to find depth.

ff_bodyweight_chest_roll_to_straight_bod.description: Start from a prone chest roll position, place the hands by the ribs, and tuck the legs as the hips rise. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this knee-over-toe-split-squat version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_ring_bent_arm_bent_body_press_handsta.description: Start with hands planted, hips piked, and shoulders leaning forward so the hands can support your weight. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press. In this ring-bent-arm-bent-body-press-handstand version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_sumo_deadlift.description: Place one kettlebell between wide-set feet with toes turned slightly out. Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle with one hand, then stand by driving through the whole foot. Keep the chest lifted and do not jerk the bell off the floor.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load in the front rack. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_ring_dip_to_handstand.description: Start in a stable ring support with elbows straight, shoulders down, and the rings close to the ribs. Lower into a ring dip, press up strongly, then continue driving the shoulders forward and hips upward toward a handstand. Keep the rings close and stop the rep if swinging replaces the press. In this ring-dip-to-handstand version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_ring_handstand_to_elbow_lever_to_hand.description: Start in a ring handstand or high inverted support with the straps steady. Bend the elbows and lower the torso into an elbow lever, then press the rings down to return to the handstand. Keep the body tight from shoulders to toes and avoid letting the rings drift wide.

ff_ring_dip_straight_body_press_to_hands.description: Start in a stable ring support with elbows straight, shoulders down, and the rings close to the ribs. Lower into a ring dip, press up strongly, then continue driving the shoulders forward and hips upward toward a handstand. Keep the rings close and stop the rep if swinging replaces the press. In this ring-dip-straight-body-press-to-handstand version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_kettlebell_swing.description: Stand with the kettlebell just in front of the feet and hinge to grip it. Hike the bell back with both hands, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. In this kettlebell-swing version, keep the variation controlled through the finish.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_swi.description: Stand with the kettlebell just in front of the feet and hinge to grip it. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Switch hands only when the bell floats near chest height. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Catch the bell softly in the rack. In this alternating-single-arm-kettlebell-clean version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sna.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. In this alternating-single-arm-kettlebell-snatch version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_ring_straight_arm_l_sit_straddle_pres.description: Start in a strong L-sit on the parallettes or rings with shoulders pushed down and legs lifted. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press. In this ring-straight-arm-l-sit-straddle-press-handstand version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this bottoms-up overhead split-squat version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_staggered_stanc.description: Set a staggered stance with the working-side foot slightly back and the bell just in front of you. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_alt.description: Set a staggered stance with the working-side foot slightly back and the bell just in front of you. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Switch hands only when the bell floats near chest height. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_s.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_double_kettlebell_outside_swing.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to hinge. Hike both bells back outside the legs, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_double_kettlebell_outside_half_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. In this double-kettlebell-outside-half-snatch version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_outside_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Catch the bell softly in the rack. In this double-kettlebell-outside-clean version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_side_swing.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. In this single-arm-kettlebell-side-swing version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_kettlebell_feet_elevated_russian_twis.description: Sit on the floor with knees bent, feet lifted or lightly elevated, and a kettlebell held by the horns. Lean back until the abs brace, then rotate the bell from one side of the hips to the other. Turn the ribs with the hands and avoid yanking the bell with rounded shoulders.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_w.description: Start half kneeling with the kettlebell pressed overhead and the loaded arm vertical. Hinge the hips back and reach the free hand toward the floor inside the front leg, then return by driving the hips under you. Keep your eyes on the bell and avoid rounding forward or letting the shoulder drift from its stacked position.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_skater_s.description: Stand on one leg with the load in the front rack and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a skater squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_skater_squat.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height and balance on one foot. Sweep the free leg back as you bend the standing knee, tap the rear knee lightly if needed, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_pistol_s.description: Stand on one leg with the load in the front rack and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_ring_straight_arm_pike_press_handstan.description: Start in a pike with hands fixed, hips high, and shoulders leaning forward over the hands. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_hollow_body_flu.description: Lie on your back and hold one kettlebell locked over the chest while the legs extend long. Lift the shoulders slightly, brace into a hollow position, and alternate small up-and-down kicks. Keep the low back heavy on the floor instead of letting the weight pull the ribs open.

ff_ring_back_lever_pullout.description: Hang from the rings with straight arms and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Keep the body tight as you move through the lever or German hang, then pull back under control. Use a tuck or bent knees before extending the legs, and avoid a fast swing into the end position. In this ring-back-lever-pullout version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_overhead_p.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Catch the bell softly in the rack. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this double-kettlebell-clean-to-overhead-press version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_kettlebell_alternating_halo_to_horn_g.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Circle the bell, catch it at chest height, then sit into the squat before standing. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_ring_german_hang_pullout.description: Hang from the rings with straight arms and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Keep the body tight as you move through the lever or German hang, then pull back under control. Use a tuck or bent knees before extending the legs, and avoid a fast swing into the end position. In this ring-german-hang-pullout version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_ring_dead_hang_to_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with active shoulders and legs in a tight lever. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull. In this ring-dead-hang-to-front-lever-pull-up version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_side_to_side_sw.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. In this single-arm-kettlebell-side-to-side-swing version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_turk.description: Lie on your back with the working-side knee bent and hold the kettlebell bottoms-up over the shoulder. Roll to the elbow, post to the hand, sweep the leg under, and stand while watching the weight. Reverse each step slowly, keeping the arm vertical instead of letting the load drift behind you.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_thruster.description: Stand with the load in the rack position and feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then drive up and continue directly into an overhead press. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip. In this single-arm-kettlebell-thruster version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_thruste.description: Stand with the load in the rack position and feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then drive up and continue directly into an overhead press. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip. In this alternating-double-kettlebell-thruster version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_dead.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor between or just inside the feet, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell into a bottoms-up rack with the handle vertical. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_snatch.description: Start with one kettlebell between the feet and prepare to switch hands at the backswing. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_snatch_to_overh.description: Start with the bell on the floor or in a hike position, then pull it close as the hips drive. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Then step into the listed reverse lunge while keeping the load stacked overhead. In this to-overhead-contralateral-reverse-lunge version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_snatch_to_overhead_.description: Start with the bell on the floor or in a hike position, then pull it close as the hips drive. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Then step into the listed reverse lunge while keeping the load stacked overhead. In this to-overhead-alternating-reverse-lunge version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_front_.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. In this front-rack-contralateral-reverse-lunge version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_front_rack.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. In this front-rack-alternating-reverse-lunge version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_ring_full_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with active shoulders and legs in a tight lever. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull. In this ring-full-front-lever-pull-up version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_split_jerk.description: Clean the kettlebell to one shoulder with the wrist straight and feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive the bell upward, and split one foot forward and one foot back as the arm locks out overhead. Recover the feet under you before lowering, keeping the torso tall instead of chasing the bell forward.

ff_parallette_stalder_press_to_handstand.description: Start with hands planted, hips piked, and shoulders leaning forward so the hands can support your weight. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press. In this parallette-stalder-press-to-handstand version, press evenly through both handles.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell into a bottoms-up rack with the handle vertical. From the rack, sit into the squat and stand tall. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_single_arm_ring_chin_up.description: Hang from the rings with palms facing you, arms long, and shoulders active. Pull the chest toward the rings by driving the elbows down, then lower until the arms are long again. Keep the body quiet and avoid kicking or shrugging through the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Clean two kettlebells bottoms-up, press them overhead, and stand with feet about shoulder width. Grip hard so the bells stay vertical while you squat between the heels, then stand without losing the overhead stack. Keep the ribs down and stop the rep if either bell tips or the elbows soften.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_thruster.description: Stand with the load in the rack position and feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then drive up and continue directly into an overhead press. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip. In this double-kettlebell-bottoms-up-thruster version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_ring_iron_cross.description: Begin in a tall ring support with elbows locked, shoulders depressed, and the rings turned slightly out. Slowly let the arms open to the sides only as far as you can hold tension, then pull the rings back under the shoulders. Keep the neck long and do not sink into the shoulders or bounce at the bottom.

ff_parallette_full_planche_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_parallette_straddle_planche_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs straddled to manage balance. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_bar_dead_hang_to_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull. In this bar-dead-hang-to-front-lever-pull-up version, keep the variation controlled through the finish.

ff_bar_full_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull. In this bar-full-front-lever-pull-up version, keep the variation controlled through the finish.

ff_barbell_overhead_bulgarian_split_squa.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.
