ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side and step back across into a curtsy lunge. Pass the bell under or beside the front thigh as you return, then alternate sides. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_low_bar_pause_back_squat.description: Set the bar low across the rear shoulders and brace before descending. Sit back and down, pause still at the bottom, then drive up over the midfoot. Keep the chest from folding and avoid bouncing out of the pause.

ff_barbell_front_rack_pause_squat.description: Hold the bar in a front rack with elbows high. Squat between the heels, pause at the bottom, then stand while keeping the bar over midfoot. Keep elbows lifted and avoid letting the bar roll forward.

ff_barbell_high_bar_pause_back_squat.description: Set the bar high on the traps and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat down with a tall chest, pause, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep knees tracking with toes and avoid relaxing in the bottom.

ff_barbell_overhead_pause_squat.description: Lock the bar overhead with hands wide and feet set for a squat. Descend, pause while the bar stays stacked over the midfoot, then stand. Keep elbows locked and avoid letting the bar drift forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_walkin.description: Hold one kettlebell suitcase-style and step forward into a lunge. Pass the bell under or beside the front thigh as you come through to the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid the load pulling you sideways.

ff_ring_hanging_knees_to_wrists.description: Hang from rings with arms long and shoulders active. Curl the pelvis and lift both knees toward the wrists, then lower slowly. Keep the rings steady and avoid swinging into the next rep.

ff_slider_suspension_lateral_lunge.description: Hold suspension handles with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the other knee bends, then pull back to center using the planted leg. Keep the straps light and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_macebell_10_to_2.description: Hold the mace with both hands and the head behind one shoulder. Swing it behind the head from the 10 oclock side to the 2 oclock side, then return. Keep elbows close and avoid arching the ribs as the mace passes behind you.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_cyclist_squat.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest and elevate the heels. Squat with knees traveling forward and torso upright, then press through the feet to stand. Keep the dumbbell close and avoid rocking off the wedge.

ff_slider_barbell_front_rack_lateral_lun.description: Hold a barbell in the front rack with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot to the side as the planted knee bends, then pull back to standing. Keep elbows high and avoid letting the planted knee collapse inward.

ff_slider_barbell_back_rack_lateral_lung.description: Set a barbell across the upper back and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out while sitting into the planted leg, then return to center. Keep the chest tall and avoid twisting the bar toward the slider.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_cossack_.description: Lock a barbell overhead and take a wide stance. Shift into a Cossack squat on one side, return through center, then alternate. Keep the bar over the midfoot and avoid letting the working heel lift.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_reverse_.description: Lock a barbell overhead with arms straight. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, return to standing, then alternate sides. Keep the bar stacked and avoid arching the back as the knee lowers.

ff_macebell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Stand on one softly bent leg and hold the mace with the head offset below the hand. Curl it briskly toward the shoulder, then control the drop without setting the free foot down. Keep the wrist firm and avoid swinging the torso.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold one kettlebell in each hand at the sides. Walk with tall posture, quiet shoulders, and even steps. Keep the bells from swinging and avoid shrugging them toward the ears.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side like a suitcase. Walk slowly while keeping shoulders and hips level. Brace the torso and avoid leaning away from the bell.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one side for a Cossack squat, then drive back to center. Keep handles vertical and avoid letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_kne.description: Set up on hands and toes with knees hovering just above the floor and a kettlebell outside one hand. Reach the opposite hand under the body to pull the bell across, then alternate. Keep hips low and avoid rocking side to side.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_high_pull.description: Start with one kettlebell in a hinge between the feet. Drive the hips, pull the elbow high and back, then let the bell float down into the next hinge. Keep the bell close and avoid muscling it up with a shrug.

ff_double_indian_club_outer_heart_shaped.description: Stand tall with one Indian club in each hand at the sides. Swing both clubs outward and up through a heart-shaped path, then guide them back down. Keep wrists relaxed and avoid speeding up before both clubs match.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_thr.description: Rack one kettlebell at the shoulder and stand with feet squat width. Squat, drive up into a press, then lower and switch sides on the next rep. Keep the elbow connected in the rack and avoid turning the press into a backbend.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_knee_over_.description: Hold one kettlebell in each hand and set a split stance. Let the front knee travel over the toes as you lower, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the heel planted and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_sumo_deadlift_to_pu.description: Stand over two kettlebells in a wide sumo stance and deadlift them to standing. Set the bells down, step or jump back to a plank, and perform a push-up on or between the handles. Keep the back long and avoid rushing the transition.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Clean one kettlebell to the rack, squat, and drive into an overhead press. Lower, switch sides, and repeat the clean-to-thruster sequence. Keep the bell close and avoid letting the rack collapse before the squat.

ff_suspension_alternating_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold suspension handles and stand tall. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, return to center, then alternate sides. Use the straps for balance and avoid pulling the front knee inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_single_leg_bala.description: Stand on one softly bent leg with a kettlebell in one hand. Pass the bell around the body or from hand to hand while staying balanced. Keep the stance hip level and avoid rushing the transfer.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_forwar.description: Hold the barbell in a front rack with elbows high. Step forward into a lunge, push back to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the bar roll down the shoulders.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_revers.description: Hold the bar in a front rack and stand tall. Step one leg back into a reverse lunge, drive through the front foot to stand, then alternate. Keep elbows high and avoid tipping forward as the back knee lowers.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_forward_.description: Lock the barbell overhead with arms straight. Step forward into a lunge, push back to standing, then alternate sides. Keep the bar over the shoulders and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_macebell_alternating_360.description: Hold the mace with both hands and cast it around one shoulder into a 360. Return to the front, then send the next rep around the other shoulder. Keep the path smooth and avoid twisting the knees with the mace.

ff_clubbell_shield_cast.description: Hold the clubbell in order position near the chest. Cast it over the shoulder and around behind the head, then pull it back to order. Keep shoulders packed and avoid flaring the ribs as the club travels.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_shield_cast.description: Hold one clubbell in order position at the shoulder. Cast it around the head and back to the start with a smooth elbow circle. Keep the wrist firm and avoid letting the club pull the torso backward.

ff_macebell_barbarian_squat.description: Hold the mace with both hands and the head behind the shoulders or upper back. Squat down with feet rooted, then stand while keeping the mace controlled. Keep elbows from flaring wide and avoid rounding the chest.

ff_single_arm_macebell_barbarian_squat.description: Hold the mace in one hand with the head behind the shoulder in a barbarian position. Squat under control and stand while the free arm balances. Keep the mace close and avoid side-bending away from the load.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_shield_ca.description: Hold two clubbells in order position. Cast one club around the shoulder and back, then repeat on the other side. Keep the resting club steady and avoid rotating the torso faster than the club path.

ff_double_clubbell_side_shoulder_cast.description: Hold two clubbells at the shoulders with the heavy ends controlled. Cast them to the side shoulder path and return to order. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the clubs pull the wrists open.

ff_macebell_isometric_split_squat_low_to.description: Hold a split squat with the mace near the outside hip. Chop diagonally upward toward the opposite shoulder, then return slowly. Keep the front knee steady and avoid rotating the pelvis with the mace.

ff_double_clubbell_side_shoulder_cast_to.description: Hold two clubbells in order position and perform a side shoulder cast. As they return, press into a side flag position before bringing them back. Keep wrists firm and avoid leaning back to move the clubs.

ff_clubbell_mill.description: Start with the clubbell in a guard position. Swing through the 180 arc, cast around the shoulder, and return to guard in one smooth mill. Lead with the elbow and avoid muscling the path with only the hand.

ff_clubbell_half_kneeling_shield_cast.description: Set a half-kneeling stance and hold the clubbell in order position. Cast it around the shoulder and behind the head, then return to order. Squeeze the back glute and avoid leaning away from the club.

ff_double_clubbell_iron_cross.description: Stand tall with one clubbell in each hand at the sides. Raise both arms out to an iron-cross position and hold or lower under control. Keep shoulders down and avoid arching the back to lift the clubs.

ff_double_clubbell_half_kneeling_iron_cr.description: Set a half-kneeling stance with one clubbell in each hand. Raise both arms out to an iron-cross position, pause, then lower with control. Keep ribs stacked and avoid shifting onto the front leg.

ff_clubbell_gamma_cast.description: Hold the clubbell in order position near the chest. Rotate the shoulders and thoracic spine as you cast the club through a gamma path, then decelerate back to order. Keep the hips quiet and avoid losing the handle angle.

ff_clubbell_half_kneeling_gamma_cast.description: Set half kneeling and hold the clubbell in order position. Cast through the gamma path with controlled torso rotation, then return to order. Keep the back glute tight and avoid swaying through the low back.

ff_double_clubbell_side_flag_press.description: Hold two clubbells at the chest with heavy ends forward. Press both clubs out into a side flag angle, then pull them back under control. Keep wrists straight and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_foot_e.description: Hold one kettlebell suitcase-style opposite the front leg and elevate the front foot slightly. Lower with the front knee traveling over the toes, then drive up through that foot. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the load pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Hold one kettlebell at the side opposite the front leg and set a split stance. Let the front knee travel over the toes as you lower, then stand through the front foot. Keep hips square and avoid leaning toward the kettlebell.

ff_double_indian_club_inner_heart_shaped.description: Stand tall with one Indian club in each hand. Swing both clubs inward through a heart-shaped path and guide them back to the sides. Keep elbows soft and avoid forcing the clubs with stiff wrists.

ff_alternating_double_indian_club_inner_.description: Hold one Indian club in each hand and start tall. Swing one club inward through the heart-shaped path, then alternate to the other side. Keep the rhythm even and avoid crossing the clubs too close to the face.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_side_flag.description: Hold two clubbells at the chest with heavy ends forward. Press one club into a side flag while the other stays parked, then alternate. Keep the torso square and avoid letting the resting club drift.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_flag_press.description: Hold one clubbell at the chest with the heavy end forward. Press it out to a side flag angle, pause, then pull it back under control. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid bending the wrist backward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle.description: Hold one clubbell near the shoulder in order position. Guide the elbow through an inside circle so the club arcs around and returns to the start. Keep the ribs down and avoid letting the club yank the wrist open.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_inside_ci.description: Hold two clubbells in order position. Make an inside circle with one club, return it, then alternate to the other side. Keep the nonmoving club stable and avoid rushing the hand switch.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_shoulder_cas.description: Hold one clubbell at the shoulder in order position. Cast it through the side shoulder path and return it to the start. Lead with the elbow and avoid leaning back as the club passes behind you.

ff_barbell_reverse_grip_bicep_curl.description: Stand holding a barbell with palms down and hands about shoulder width. Curl the bar toward the chest, then lower to straight arms. Keep elbows close and avoid bending the wrists back to finish.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_reverse_mill.description: Hold one clubbell in a guard position. Move through the reverse mill path with an outside elbow circle, then return to guard. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid letting the hand drag the club behind the elbow.

ff_clubbell_clean_to_barbarian_squat.description: Start with the clubbell near the floor or low guard. Clean it to the barbarian position behind the shoulders, then squat and stand under control. Keep the club close and avoid catching it with a loose wrist.

ff_clubbell_tall_kneeling_gamma_cast.description: Set both knees on the floor and hold the clubbell in order position. Cast through the gamma path and return to order without shifting the hips. Keep glutes tight and avoid leaning back as the club rotates.

ff_clubbell_inside_circle.description: Hold the clubbell in order position with both hands or the prescribed grip. Guide it through an inside circle and return to the start smoothly. Keep elbows soft and avoid letting the club pull the ribs open.

ff_clubbell_tall_kneeling_shield_cast.description: Kneel tall with the clubbell in order position. Cast it around the shoulder and behind the head, then return to order. Keep glutes squeezed and avoid arching the low back during the cast.

ff_clubbell_isometric_split_squat_shield.description: Hold the bottom of a split squat with a clubbell in order position. Perform a shield cast around the shoulder while the legs stay still. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid rising out of the hold.

ff_clubbell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Stand on one softly bent leg with the clubbell in order position. Cast it around the shoulder and return to the start while maintaining balance. Keep hips level and avoid hopping as the club moves.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_tall_kneeling_shi.description: Kneel tall and hold one clubbell in order position at the shoulder. Cast it around the head and return to order with a smooth elbow circle. Keep the wrist firm and avoid leaning away from the club.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_mill.description: Hold one clubbell in a guard position with feet planted. Swing through the mill arc, cast around the shoulder, and return to guard. Lead with the elbow and avoid muscling the club only with the hand.

ff_double_clubbell_isometric_split_squat.description: Start with two clubbells and drop into a split squat and hold the bottom position. Raise the clubbells out to an iron-cross line at shoulder height while the trunk stays tall. Keep the bells level and avoid letting the ribs flare or the standing knee cave in.

ff_double_clubbell_single_leg_standing_b.description: Start with two clubbells and stand on one leg with the free knee bent. Raise the clubbells out to an iron-cross line at shoulder height while the trunk stays tall. Keep the bells level and avoid letting the ribs flare or the standing knee cave in.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_deadlift.description: Stand between two kettlebells with the handles beside your feet like suitcases. Hinge and bend the knees just enough to grip them, then drive the floor away to stand tall. Keep both bells close to your legs and avoid twisting or letting one shoulder dip.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean.description: Hike two kettlebells from the hang or floor and snap the hips to bring both handles upward. Rotate into a bottoms-up rack with the bell inverted and the wrist vertical. Catch softly with elbows close, and avoid overgripping so hard that the shoulder rises toward the ear.

ff_double_clubbell_mill.description: Stand in an athletic stance with two clubbells in guard. Use the elbow to guide the inside circle, cast behind the shoulder, and move both clubbells through matching mill paths. Keep the wrist firm and avoid muscling the club with only the hand.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_mill.description: Stand in an athletic stance with one clubbell in one hand in guard. Use the elbow to guide the inside circle, cast behind the shoulder, and switch hands each rep after the club returns to guard. Keep the wrist firm and avoid muscling the club with only the hand.

ff_clubbell_isometric_split_squat_gamma_.description: Grip the clubbell in order and hold a split-squat stance. Cast the club around the shoulder in the gamma path, returning it to order without changing your lower-body position. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid whipping from the wrist.

ff_clubbell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Grip the clubbell in order and stand on one leg with the free knee bent. Cast the club around the shoulder in the gamma path, returning it to order without changing your lower-body position. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid whipping from the wrist.

ff_macebell_alternating_360_to_ballistic.description: Begin with the macebell in front rack and perform a 360 around the shoulders. As it returns forward, guide it into a ballistic curl and switch the lead side for the next rep. Keep the elbows close and avoid letting the mace crash into the chest.

ff_bar_chin_up.description: Hang from a pull-up bar with palms facing you and hands about shoulder width. Pull the shoulder blades down, drive elbows toward the ribs, and bring the chin over the bar. Lower to long arms without kicking or craning the neck to finish the rep.

ff_bar_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with overhand hands just outside shoulder width. Start by setting the shoulder blades, then pull the chest toward the bar until the chin clears. Lower under control and avoid swinging the legs for momentum.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_swing.description: Stand tall with one clubbell beside the hip and feet about shoulder width. Hinge slightly and swing the club side to side across the body, using the hips to absorb each change of direction. Keep the working hand connected through the whole arc, and do not let the shoulder chase the club forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_pendulum.description: Hold the clubbell in order and let the head swing inside the body line like a pendulum. Keep the elbow close as the club passes the ribs, and let it return to order with control. Stay on the same side until the rep is finished. Avoid shrugging or bending backward to clear the club.

ff_double_clubbell_swipe.description: Start with two clubbells in order and feet grounded. Sit slightly as the club swings down, then stand and and bring it back to order. Keep the club close enough to control and avoid rounding the back at the bottom of the swipe.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_swipe.description: Start with one clubbell in one hand in order and feet grounded. Sit slightly as the club swings down, then stand and and bring it back to order. Keep the club close enough to control and avoid rounding the back at the bottom of the swipe.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_cross_body_swipe.description: Start with one clubbell in one hand in order and feet grounded. Sit slightly as the club swings down, then stand and sweep the club across the body before the cast. Keep the club close enough to control and avoid rounding the back at the bottom of the swipe.

ff_bar_scapular_pull_up.description: Hang from the pull-up bar with straight arms and feet off the floor or lightly assisted. Without bending the elbows, pull the shoulder blades down and slightly back to raise the body a few inches. Pause, then return to a passive hang without turning it into a half pull-up.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle_to_.description: Start with one clubbell in order and feet about shoulder width. Draw an inside circle across the centerline, then continue into a shield cast around the shoulder. Keep the elbow path round and avoid letting the club pull your head forward.

ff_clubbell_barbarian_squat.description: Stand with one clubbell with both hands in front order and feet set to leave room for the swing. Cast the clubbell down as you sit into the squat, then stand and return it to order. Keep the chest proud and do not let the club pull you into a rounded bottom position.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle_to_.description: Hold the clubbell in order with one hand and stand in a steady athletic stance. Draw an inside circle across the centerline, then reverse into an outside circle away from the body. Keep the elbow leading the circle and avoid flicking the club with a loose wrist.

ff_clubbell_inside_circle_to_outside_cir.description: Hold the clubbell in order with both hands on the club and stand in a steady athletic stance. Draw an inside circle across the centerline, then reverse into an outside circle away from the body. Keep the elbow leading the circle and avoid flicking the club with a loose wrist.

ff_double_clubbell_barbarian_squat.description: Stand with two clubbells in front order and feet set to leave room for the swing. Cast the clubbell down as you sit into the squat, then stand and return it to order. Keep the chest proud and do not let the club pull you into a rounded bottom position.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_to_side_swin.description: Stand with one clubbell in one hand and the feet about shoulder width. Swing the club across the front of the body, then reverse it to the other side with a small hip hinge. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid letting the club pull the wrist open.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_side_.description: Stand tall with one clubbell beside the hip and feet about shoulder width. Hinge slightly and swing the club side to side across the body, using the hips to absorb each change of direction. Switch hands only as the club floats in front, and do not let the shoulder chase the club forward.

ff_clubbell_alternating_inside_pendulum_.description: Grip the clubbell in order and stand in an athletic stance. Cast the club around the shoulder in the gamma path, returning it to order without changing your lower-body position. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid whipping from the wrist.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_single_leg_standi.description: Set up with one clubbell in one hand and a braced athletic stance. Move through the named clubbell pattern with the elbow leading and the club close enough to control. Keep the wrist firm and reset if the club pulls your posture out of line.

ff_bar_skin_the_cat.description: Hang from the pull-up bar with an overhand grip and shoulders active. Tuck or pike the legs, roll them through the arms, and lower behind you only as far as you can control. Reverse the path slowly and avoid dropping into the end range with relaxed shoulders.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_tall.description: Set up tall-kneeling with knees under hips and rack one kettlebell bottoms-up with vertical wrists. Press overhead only after the bells stop wobbling, then lower back to the rack with control. Keep glutes and abs braced so the ribs do not flare to fake the lockout.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle_to_.description: Start with one clubbell in order at the shoulder. Draw an inside circle, catch the club in front, and press to a front flag with the arm long. Brace hard so the club does not drag the wrist down or twist the torso open.

ff_double_clubbell_order_bulgarian_split.description: Set the rear foot on a bench and hold two clubbells in order at the shoulders. Lower straight down until the front leg controls the split squat, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the order position quiet and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean.description: Stand with the barbell at mid-thigh and hands just outside the legs. Dip to the hang, extend the hips hard, then pull under and catch in a front squat. Stand to finish, keeping the bar close instead of looping it away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_fee.description: Set up with one kettlebell and use a hip snap to move the load close to the body. Finish the named kettlebell phase on one side with a stacked wrist and steady rack or lockout. Keep the bell path tight and reset if it bangs the forearm or pulls your ribs open.

ff_miniband_supine_alternating_hip_flexi.description: Lie on your back with a miniband around the feet and knees bent over the hips. Brace the ribs down, then pull one knee toward the chest as the other leg reaches away against the band. Move slowly and avoid arching the low back off the floor.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_tall_kne.description: Set up tall-kneeling with knees under hips and rack two kettlebells bottoms-up with vertical wrists. Press overhead only after the bells stop wobbling, then lower back to the rack with control. Keep glutes and abs braced so the ribs do not flare to fake the lockout.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Kneel tall with two kettlebells in a bottoms-up rack and glutes braced. Press one bell overhead while the other stays parked, then lower and alternate sides. Keep both wrists vertical and avoid leaning back to save a wobbling bell.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half_kne.description: Set a half-kneeling stance and rack two kettlebells bottoms-up with vertical wrists. Press both bells overhead after they stop wobbling, then lower to the rack with control. Keep the front foot rooted and do not arch the low back to finish the lockout.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Set a half-kneeling stance with two kettlebells bottoms-up in the rack. Press one bell overhead while the other stays steady, then lower and switch sides. Keep hips square and avoid letting the non-pressing bell drift away from vertical.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_barbarian_squat.description: Stand with one clubbell in one hand in front order and feet set to leave room for the swing. Cast the clubbell down as you sit into the squat, then stand and return it to order. Keep the chest proud and do not let the club pull you into a rounded bottom position.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_single_leg_standi.description: Stand on one leg with the free knee bent and hold the clubbell on the same side as the stance leg. Cast the club around the shoulder and return to order without setting the foot down. Keep the knee stacked over the toes and avoid leaning away from the club.

ff_clubbell_order_cyclist_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated, feet close, and hold the clubbell in order at the shoulder. Bend the knees forward as you squat tall, then drive through the balls of the feet to stand. Keep the clubbell order stacked and avoid folding at the hips like a hinge.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Set the rear foot on a bench and hold the clubbell in order at the shoulder on the side opposite the working leg. Lower straight down until the front leg controls the split squat, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the order position quiet and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_10_to.description: Stand tall with the macebell in rack and hands near the navel. Swing the head from the 10 o'clock side behind the shoulders to the 2 o'clock side, alternating the lead hand each rep. Keep the mace close and avoid flaring the ribs as it passes behind the head.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_tall_kne.description: Kneel tall with two kettlebells in a bottoms-up rack and ribs down. Press one bell as the other lowers in a seesaw rhythm, pausing whenever either wrist loses vertical. Keep the hips extended and avoid bouncing through the torso.

ff_clubbell_inside_pendulum_to_inside_pu.description: Hold the clubbell in order and let the head swing inside the body line like a pendulum. Keep the elbow close as the club passes the ribs, then pull it inside and over the shoulder before returning to order. Stay on the same side until the rep is finished. Avoid shrugging or bending backward to clear the club.

ff_clubbell_order_squat.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and hold the clubbell in order at the shoulder. Squat between the heels while the clubbell stays parked in order, then stand without bouncing out of the bottom. Keep the elbow close and avoid tipping toward the loaded side.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Set the rear foot on a bench and hold the clubbell in order at the shoulder on the same side as the working leg. Lower straight down until the front leg controls the split squat, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the order position quiet and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_squat.description: Hold one clubbell in order at the shoulder and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat between the heels, then stand while the club stays vertical. Keep the elbow close and avoid tipping toward the loaded side.

ff_double_clubbell_order_squat.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and hold two clubbells in order at the shoulders. Squat between the heels while the clubbell stays parked in order, then stand without bouncing out of the bottom. Keep the elbow close and avoid tipping toward the loaded side.

ff_clubbell_alternating_inside_pendulum_.description: Hold the clubbell in order and let the head swing inside the body line like a pendulum. Keep the elbow close as the club passes the ribs, then pull it inside and over the shoulder before returning to order. Alternate sides after each controlled catch. Avoid shrugging or bending backward to clear the club.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_arm_.description: Lie on your back and press one kettlebell bottoms-up so the bell sits above the handle. Roll toward the free side into an arm-bar position with the loaded arm vertical. Keep the wrist stacked and stop before the bell drifts or the shoulder loses its packed position.

Lie on your back and press one kettlebell bottoms-up over the shoulder, then roll carefully into the arm-bar position. Rotate the chest toward and away from the floor in a small range while the bell stays vertical. Move from the upper back, not by bending the wrist or letting the shoulder shrug.

ff_double_kettlebell_push_up.description: Set up with two kettlebells and use a hip snap to move the load close to the body. Finish the named kettlebell phase with both bells with a stacked wrist and steady rack or lockout. Keep the bell path tight and reset if it bangs the forearm or pulls your ribs open.

ff_bodyweight_pseudo_planche_lean.description: Set a push-up plank with hands near the hips or turned slightly out. Lean the shoulders forward past the wrists while keeping elbows straight and toes anchored. Hold the hollow body line and back off if the wrists lift or the hips pike.

ff_macebell_360_to_single_arm_360.description: Start with a two-hand macebell 360 from the front rack, circling the head behind the shoulders and back to center. After the two-hand rep, slide to one hand and repeat a smaller single-arm 360 on the same path. Keep the hands low in front and do not rush the transition if the head drifts away from you.

ff_tire_feet_elevated_decline_push_up.description: Place both feet on a tire and set the hands under the shoulders in a strong plank. Lower the chest toward the floor, then press back up without losing the straight body line. Keep the hips from sagging and do not let the head reach for the floor first.

ff_bodyweight_bench_feet_elevated_declin.description: Place both feet on a bench and set the hands under the shoulders in a strong plank. Lower the chest toward the floor, then press back up without losing the straight body line. Keep the hips from sagging and do not let the head reach for the floor first.

ff_bodyweight_box_feet_elevated_decline_.description: Place both feet on a box and set the hands under the shoulders in a strong plank. Lower the chest toward the floor, then press back up without losing the straight body line. Keep the hips from sagging and do not let the head reach for the floor first.

ff_bodyweight_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Place the feet on a sturdy platform and walk the hands back until the hips are high in a pike. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the hands, then press back up. Keep weight over the hands and avoid turning it into a flat decline push-up.

ff_tire_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Place the feet on a tire and walk the hands back until the hips are high in a pike. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the hands, then press back up. Keep weight over the hands and avoid turning it into a flat decline push-up.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_push_jer.description: Rack two kettlebells bottoms-up with forearms vertical and feet under the hips. Dip, drive, and drop slightly under the bells as the arms lock overhead. Stand to finish, keeping the handles stacked instead of chasing a wobble with your lower back.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Rack two kettlebells bottoms-up with forearms vertical. Dip, drive, and jerk one bell overhead while the other stays in the rack, then lower and alternate. Keep the catch quiet and do not chase a wobble by arching the back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Set up standing tall and rack one kettlebell bottoms-up with vertical wrists. Press overhead only after the bells stop wobbling, then lower back to the rack with control. Keep glutes and abs braced so the ribs do not flare to fake the lockout.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_push.description: Rack one kettlebell bottoms-up with the elbow close and wrist vertical. Use a small knee dip and leg drive to help press the inverted bell overhead. Lower to the rack under control and avoid turning the press into a backbend.

ff_bulgarian_bag_side_to_side_swing.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles and stand with feet slightly wider than the shoulders. Hinge to swing the bag through the legs, then snap the hips to float it forward. Keep arms long and avoid squatting the swing or rounding the back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half.description: Set up half-kneeling with the kettlebell on the same side as the front leg. Rack it bottoms-up, brace, and press overhead without letting the wrist tilt. Keep the front foot heavy and avoid shifting the hips away from the bell.

ff_single_ring_rotational_row.description: Hold one ring with one hand and lean back with feet planted. Pull the chest toward the ring while rotating the free shoulder open, then lower and rotate back under control. Keep the body in one line and avoid yanking with the neck or hips.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_tall_kneeling_ove.description: Kneel tall inside the trap bar and hold the handles with the bar balanced bottoms-up. Brace glutes and abs, then press overhead until the arms lock out. Keep the bar level and avoid arching the low back as the leverage changes.

ff_clubbell_clean.description: Start with one clubbell hanging in front and a firm, stacked grip. Hinge, snap the hips, and guide the clubbell to order at the shoulder without banging the forearm. Keep the handle close and avoid curling the club up with only the arm.

ff_double_clubbell_clean.description: Start with two clubbells hanging in front and a firm, stacked grip. Hinge, snap the hips, and guide the clubbell to order at the shoulder without banging the forearm. Keep the handle close and avoid curling the club up with only the arm.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_clean.description: Start with one clubbell hanging in one hand and the feet grounded. Hinge, snap the hips, and guide the club into order at the shoulder. Keep the handle close and avoid curling the club up with a bent wrist.

ff_trap_bar_z_press.description: Sit on the floor inside the trap bar with legs straight and handles at shoulder height. Brace tall and press the bar overhead without leaning back. Lower to the shoulders under control, keeping the legs grounded and ribs down.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with the trap bar balanced carefully above the chest in the bottoms-up orientation. Grip the handles, press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists stacked and stop if the bar tips out of level.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench under a carefully balanced bottoms-up trap bar and grip the handles evenly. Lower toward the chest with elbows under the handles, then press back to a steady lockout. Keep the bar level and avoid bouncing or twisting to save a missed rep.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_spin_to_sid.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles and stand just wider than shoulder width. Swing it through the legs, drive the hips forward, and drop into the squat as the bag floats in front. Keep the back long and shoulders packed instead of rounding to chase the swing.

ff_bulgarian_bag_swipe.description: Grip the Bulgarian bag by the main handles with feet about shoulder width. Swing it between the legs and sweep it up across the body in a swipe, finishing tall before the next rep. Keep the hinge clean and avoid rounding the back as the bag changes direction.

ff_bulgarian_bag_snatch.description: Stand wide with the Bulgarian bag between the legs and grip the main handles. Hinge, drive the hips, and punch the bag overhead after the hip drive. Keep the elbows long at the top and avoid muscling the bag up with a rounded back.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_rotational_.description: Stand wide with the Bulgarian bag between the legs and grip the main handles. Hinge, drive the hips, and rotate through alternating sides as the bag travels overhead. Keep the elbows long at the top and avoid muscling the bag up with a rounded back.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_side_swing_.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles and stand just wider than shoulder width. Swing it to one side, absorb the arc, then drop into a squat as it floats forward. Stand and alternate sides, keeping the back long instead of chasing the bag.

ff_bulgarian_bag_dead_clean.description: Place the Bulgarian bag on the floor between the feet and grip the handles. Hinge, drive through the legs, and clean the bag to the front rack against the chest. Catch softly with ribs down and avoid curling it up while the hips stay still.

ff_bulgarian_bag_swing.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles with feet slightly wider than the shoulders. Hinge to send the bag behind you, then snap the hips to float it forward. Keep arms long and avoid turning the swing into a squat.

ff_bulgarian_bag_front_rack_squat.description: Set the Bulgarian bag in the front rack against the chest and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat between the heels and stand tall while keeping even pressure through both feet. Keep the torso upright and avoid letting the soft bag slide or pull you forward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_squat.description: Set the Bulgarian bag across the upper back and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat between the heels and stand tall while keeping even pressure through both feet. Keep the torso upright and avoid letting the soft bag slide or pull you forward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_swing_squat.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles and let it swing back through the legs. Drive the hips forward, then sit into a squat as the bag rises in front. Keep shoulders packed and avoid rounding at the bottom of the hinge.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_forward_lun.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles at chest height. Step forward into a lunge and rotate the bag toward the front leg, then push back and alternate sides. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and rotate from the ribs, not by collapsing the low back.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_doubl.description: Stand on one leg with a battle-rope end in each hand and the free knee lifted. Make fast double waves by moving both hands up and down together. Keep the ribs stacked over the hips and avoid letting the ropes pull you off the standing foot.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_power.description: Stand on one leg with a rope end in each hand and the free knee slightly lifted. Raise both rope ends, then slam them down together while keeping balance on the standing leg. Brace before each slam and avoid letting the torso fold over the rope.

ff_landmine_v_grip_tall_kneeling_chest_p.description: Set a V-grip attachment on the landmine sleeve and kneel tall facing the anchor. Hold the handles at chest height, brace the glutes, and press the sleeve forward and slightly up. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_double_clubbell_swipe_to_alternating_.description: Start with two clubbells in order and feet grounded. Sit slightly as the club swings down, then stand and then alternate into a shield cast on the next side. Keep the club close enough to control and avoid rounding the back at the bottom of the swipe.

ff_double_clubbell_order_cyclist_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated, feet close, and hold two clubbells in order at the shoulders. Bend the knees forward as you squat tall, then drive through the balls of the feet to stand. Keep the clubbell order stacked and avoid folding at the hips like a hinge.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_cyclist_squ.description: Stand with heels elevated and one clubbell in order at the shoulder. Squat tall with knees traveling forward, then drive through the balls of the feet to stand. Keep the loaded elbow close and avoid folding at the hips.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Sit on the floor with legs straight and two kettlebells in a bottoms-up rack. Press one bell overhead, lower it, then press the other while staying tall. Keep the wrists stacked and avoid leaning back or bending the knees for help.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_clap.description: Stand on one leg with a battle-rope end in each hand. Sweep the hands out and in so the ropes clap together while the standing knee stays softly bent. Keep the chest tall and avoid hopping to save balance.

ff_single_arm_macebell_mill.description: Hold one macebell in guard with the head angled away from the body. Lead the mill from the elbow, letting the mace circle across and cast behind the shoulder before returning to guard. Keep the core braced and avoid steering the long lever with a bent wrist.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_low_switch_cossack_sq.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_alternatin.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack and brace before squatting. Stand hard from the squat and continue into an overhead press on alternating sides. Keep the rack tight and do not let the bells crash or drift forward during the clean.

Clean two kettlebells to the rack and pause with elbows close. Press one bell overhead while the other stays parked, lower it, then press the opposite side. Keep the torso still and avoid leaning back as fatigue builds.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Clean two kettlebells to a bottoms-up rack and brace before squatting. Stand hard from the squat and continue into an overhead press with both arms. Keep the rack tight and do not let the bells crash or drift forward during the clean.

Clean two kettlebells to a bottoms-up rack and brace before squatting. Stand hard from the squat and continue into an overhead press on alternating sides. Keep the rack tight and do not let the bells crash or drift forward during the clean.

Clean two kettlebells to a bottoms-up rack and pause until both handles are vertical. Press one bell overhead, lower it, then press the opposite side. Keep the non-pressing elbow close and avoid rushing the clean into the press.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_split_jerk.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack and pause until the load is steady. Dip straight down, drive up, and split the feet as you lock out overhead with both bells. Recover front foot then back foot, keeping the ribs down instead of chasing the weight forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Clean two kettlebells to a bottoms-up rack and pause until the load is steady. Dip straight down, drive up, and split the feet as you lock out overhead with both bells. Recover front foot then back foot, keeping the ribs down instead of chasing the weight forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_cossack_sq.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and rack both dumbbells at the shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and lock both dumbbells overhead with ribs down; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stand tall before shifting to the other side, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_cossack_squa.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and lock both dumbbells overhead with ribs down; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_alternating_.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold two dumbbells low at your sides; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stand tall before shifting to the other side, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_cossack_squa.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold two dumbbells low at your sides; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_split_.description: Clean one kettlebell to the front rack and pause until the load is steady. Dip straight down, drive up, and split the feet as you lock out overhead on one side. Recover front foot then back foot, keeping the ribs down instead of chasing the weight forward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Clean one kettlebell to the rack on one side and settle your feet. Dip, drive, and split jerk that bell overhead, then recover and switch arms on the next rep. Keep the clean close and avoid stepping wide to catch a drifting lockout.

ff_suspension_low_switch_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold both suspension handles with arms long; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_suspension_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold both suspension handles with arms long; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_suspension_alternating_cos.description: Hold one suspension handle and take a wide Cossack stance. Shift into one leg while the other stays long, using the strap only for balance. Stand tall before alternating sides, keeping the bent heel down.

ff_single_arm_suspension_contralateral_c.description: Hold one suspension handle opposite the working leg and stand wide. Shift toward the working side as the other leg stays straight with toes up. Keep light tension on the strap and avoid pulling yourself upright with the arm.

ff_single_arm_suspension_low_switch_coss.description: Hold one suspension handle and set a wide Cossack stance. Sink into one side, stay low, and use the strap lightly as you switch across. Keep each bent heel planted and avoid bouncing through the middle.

ff_suspension_foot_elevated_cossack_squa.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold both suspension handles with arms long; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_suspension_foot_elevated_c.description: Hold one suspension handle opposite the working leg and elevate the straight-leg foot. Shift into the working hip while the elevated leg stays long. Use the strap for balance, not to yank yourself out of the bottom.

ff_barbell_overhead_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and lock the barbell overhead with hands wide; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_low_switch_cossack.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and rack the barbell across the front shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_low_switch_cossack_.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and set the barbell across the upper back; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and set the barbell across the upper back; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and rack the barbell across the front shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_landmine_goblet_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and cup the sleeve end of the landmine at chest height; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_landmine_goblet_low_switch_cossack_sq.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and cup the sleeve end of the landmine at chest height; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_landmine_goblet_foot_elevated_cossack.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and cup the sleeve end of the landmine at chest height; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_landmine_low_hold_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold the landmine sleeve low in front of the hips; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_landmine_low_hold_low_switch_cossack_.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold the landmine sleeve low in front of the hips; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_landmine_low_hold_foot_elevated_cossa.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold the landmine sleeve low in front of the hips; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Rack one dumbbell opposite the working leg and take a wide stance. Shift into the working side while the other leg stays long with toes up. Keep the elbow lifted and avoid tipping toward the dumbbell.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_low_switch_c.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and lock both dumbbells overhead with ribs down; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_foot_elevate.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and lock both dumbbells overhead with ribs down; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_low_switch.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and rack both dumbbells at the shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_foot_eleva.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and rack both dumbbells at the shoulders; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_altern.description: Rack one dumbbell at the shoulder and stand wide. Squat into one side, stand tall, then alternate to the other side with the rack unchanged. Keep the heel down and avoid letting the dumbbell roll onto the wrist.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_low_sw.description: Rack one dumbbell at the shoulder and set a wide Cossack stance. Sink into one side, stay low, and shift across to the other side. Keep the torso tall and avoid dropping the elbow as you switch.

ff_dumbbell_low_hold_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell low in both hands; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_dumbbell_low_hold_alternating_cossack.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell low in both hands; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stand tall before shifting to the other side, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_low_switch_c.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold two dumbbells low at your sides; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_foot_elevate.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold two dumbbells low at your sides; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_dumbbell_low_hold_low_switch_cossack_.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell low in both hands; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_dumbbell_low_hold_foot_elevated_cossa.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell low in both hands; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Lock one dumbbell overhead opposite the working leg and stand wide. Shift into the working side while the straight-leg toes turn up. Keep the biceps near the ear and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Lock one dumbbell overhead on the working-leg side and take a wide stance. Sit into that side while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel down and avoid leaning under the dumbbell to find depth.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clea.description: Clean one kettlebell to a bottoms-up rack and pause until the load is steady. Dip straight down, drive up, and split the feet as you lock out overhead on one side. Recover front foot then back foot, keeping the ribs down instead of chasing the weight forward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bot.description: Clean one kettlebell to a bottoms-up rack and let the handle settle. Dip, drive, and split jerk it overhead, then recover and alternate arms on the next rep. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid stepping under a tilting bell.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_split_je.description: Start with two kettlebells in a bottoms-up rack and feet under the hips. Dip vertically, drive the load up, and split the feet to catch with the arm locked overhead. Recover under control and avoid pressing late after the legs have stopped helping.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_cossack_sq.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and the clubbell in order at the shoulder; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stand tall before shifting to the other side, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_clubbell_order_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and the clubbell in order at the shoulder; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_clubbell_order_low_switch_cossack_squ.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and the clubbell in order at the shoulder; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_clubbell_order_foot_elevated_cossack_.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and the clubbell in order at the shoulder; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_cos.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and two clubbells in order at the shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stand tall before shifting to the other side, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_cossack_squat.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and two clubbells in order at the shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_low_switch_coss.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and two clubbells in order at the shoulders; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Stay low as you shift across the middle, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_foot_elevated_c.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and two clubbells in order at the shoulders; place the straight-leg foot on a low plate or block. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Hold one clubbell in order opposite the working leg and stand wide. Shift into the working side while the other leg stays long with toes up. Keep the club vertical and avoid falling toward the loaded shoulder.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_alternating.description: Hold one clubbell in order at the shoulder and take a wide Cossack stance. Squat into one side, stand tall, then alternate sides while the club stays parked. Keep the elbow close and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_low_switch_.description: Hold one clubbell in order and stand wide. Sink into one Cossack side, stay low, and shift across to the other side. Keep the club vertical and avoid letting the straight-leg heel pop up.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_foot_elevat.description: Hold one clubbell in order opposite the working leg and elevate the straight-leg foot. Shift into the working hip while keeping the club vertical. Move slowly and avoid using momentum to leave the bottom.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_carry.description: Lap or clean the heavy sandbag into a tight bear hug high on the chest. Stand tall and walk with short, steady steps while squeezing the bag into the torso. Keep the bag above the belly and avoid leaning back to keep it from slipping.

ff_dumbbell_hip_thrust.description: Sit with the upper back against a bench and a dumbbell held across the hips. Plant the feet, tuck the ribs down, and drive the hips up until the torso and thighs form a line. Lower under control and avoid overextending the low back at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_carry.description: Press two dumbbells overhead until the wrists stack over the shoulders. Walk with small, quiet steps while keeping biceps near the ears and ribs down. Stop or lower the weights if the arms drift forward or the low back arches.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_spli.description: Start with one kettlebell in a bottoms-up rack and feet under the hips. Dip vertically, drive the load up, and split the feet to catch with the arm locked overhead. Recover under control and avoid pressing late after the legs have stopped helping.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bot.description: Hike one kettlebell from the hang or floor and snap the hips to bring the handle upward. Rotate into a bottoms-up rack with the bell inverted and the wrist vertical. Catch softly with elbows close, and avoid overgripping so hard that the shoulder rises toward the ear.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clea.description: Hike one kettlebell from the hang and snap the hips to bring the handle upward. Rotate into a bottoms-up rack with the bell inverted over a vertical wrist. Catch softly and avoid letting the elbow flare away from the ribs.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bot.description: Clean one kettlebell to a bottoms-up rack and brace before squatting. Stand hard from the squat and continue into an overhead press on alternating sides. Keep the rack tight and do not let the bells crash or drift forward during the clean.

Clean one kettlebell to a bottoms-up front rack and stabilize before squatting. Sit between the heels, stand tall, then switch arms on the next clean if alternating. Keep the inverted bell over a vertical forearm and avoid collapsing the elbow away from the ribs.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_carry.description: Clean the heavy sandbag to one shoulder and wrap the near arm over it. Walk with short steps, keeping the torso tall and the bag pinned to the shoulder. Switch sides as planned and avoid side-bending so far that the bag controls your stride.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_carry.description: Clean two dumbbells to the shoulders with elbows slightly forward. Walk with short steps while the bells stay parked in the front rack. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid letting the elbows drop until the weights roll onto the wrists.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_carry.description: Clean one dumbbell to the shoulder and keep the elbow slightly forward. Walk slowly without leaning away from the loaded side. Keep the wrist stacked and reset if the dumbbell slides out of the front rack.

ff_double_clubbell_order_carry.description: Clean two clubbells to order at the shoulder before you begin walking. Take short steps while the elbow stays close and the clubbell remains vertical. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning away from the loaded side.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_carry.description: Clean one clubbell to order at the shoulder before you begin walking. Take short steps while the elbow stays close and the clubbell remains vertical. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning away from the loaded side.

ff_barbell_overhead_carry.description: Press or jerk the barbell overhead with hands set wide enough for a stable lockout. Walk slowly with the bar stacked over the midfoot. Keep the ribs down and avoid chasing the bar forward with an arched back.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_carry.description: Grip the barbell near its center and press it overhead with one arm once it is balanced. Walk in short steps while keeping the bar level over the shoulder. Keep the torso quiet and stop if either sleeve tips out of control.

ff_barbell_front_rack_carry.description: Rack the barbell across the front shoulders with elbows lifted. Walk with short steps, keeping the bar close to the throat and the torso tall. Do not let the elbows drop or the bar roll into the hands.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Hold a kettlebell at each side in a suitcase carry stance. Step one foot back, lower until the front leg owns the load, then drive through the front foot and alternate legs. Keep the bells quiet beside the hips instead of letting them swing or pull the torso forward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sum.description: Stand wide over one kettlebell with toes turned slightly out. Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle, then push the floor apart and stand without yanking with the arm. Alternate hands only after the bell is settled, keeping the chest lifted and back long.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_march.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side with the free arm relaxed or slightly out for balance. Stand tall and march slowly, lifting one knee at a time without leaning away from the bell. Keep ribs stacked over hips and set the bell down if your posture starts to tilt.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Clean one kettlebell bottoms-up overhead with the wrist vertical before you start marching. Lift one knee at a time with slow, quiet footwork while the inverted bell stays stacked over the forearm. Do not rush the steps or let the bell wobble away from a vertical wrist.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Clean one kettlebell bottoms-up in the front rack with the elbow close before you start marching. Lift one knee at a time with slow, quiet footwork while the inverted bell stays stacked over the forearm. Do not rush the steps or let the bell wobble away from a vertical wrist.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_snatch.description: Hike two kettlebells between the legs and drive the hips so the bell path rises close to the body. Punch to overhead, then lower to the rack before the next backswing for the half-snatch rhythm. Keep the wrist neutral and avoid letting the bell crash onto the forearm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_hal.description: Hike one kettlebell between the legs and drive the hips so the bell path rises close to the body. Punch to overhead, then lower to the rack before the next backswing for the half-snatch rhythm. Keep the wrist neutral and avoid letting the bell crash onto the forearm.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_snatch.description: Hike one kettlebell between the legs and drive the hips to send it close to the body. Punch to overhead, then lower to the rack before the next backswing. Keep the wrist neutral and avoid crashing the bell onto the forearm.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_snatch_to_thru.description: Hike two kettlebells between the legs and snap to an overhead half snatch, then lower to the rack instead of dropping all the way down. Sit into a front-rack squat and drive up into the thruster press. Keep the bells close on the descent and avoid turning the squat into a loose forward hinge.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_cossack_sq.description: Set a wide Cossack stance and lock both kettlebells overhead; let the straight-leg toes turn up as the heel stays down. Shift your hips toward one leg and bend that knee while the other leg stays long. Press back to the start before repeating, keeping the bent-leg heel planted and the load from tipping you forward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_squat.description: Clean or lap the heavy sandbag into a tight bear hug high on the chest. Squat between the heels, then drive the floor away to stand while squeezing the bag. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the bag slide below the belly.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_squat.description: Clean the heavy sandbag to one shoulder and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat with the bag pinned to the shoulder, then stand without twisting. Keep pressure even through both feet and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_heavy_sandbag_turkish_get_up.description: Lie beside the heavy sandbag and hug or shoulder it into a secure loaded position before rolling to your back. Move through elbow, hand, bridge, half-kneeling, and stand while keeping the bag pinned close. Reverse the path slowly and avoid rushing any transition where the bag shifts.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_fo.description: Clean the heavy sandbag into a bear hug high on the chest. Step forward and lower until the front leg controls the lunge, then drive through the front foot and continue. Keep the bag squeezed to the torso and avoid letting it pull the chest down.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_re.description: Clean the heavy sandbag into a bear hug high on the chest. Step back and lower until the front leg controls the lunge, then drive through the front foot and continue. Keep the bag squeezed to the torso and avoid letting it pull the chest down.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_fo.description: Clean the heavy sandbag to one shoulder and secure it with the near arm. Step forward into each lunge while keeping the torso tall under the uneven load. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid collapsing toward the bag side.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_re.description: Clean the heavy sandbag to one shoulder and secure it with the near arm. Step back into each lunge while keeping the torso tall under the uneven load. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid collapsing toward the bag side.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_walking_lunge.description: Clean the heavy sandbag to one shoulder and secure it with the near arm. Walk forward into each lunge while keeping the torso tall under the uneven load. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid collapsing toward the bag side.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_clean.description: Straddle the heavy sandbag, hinge, and lap it close with a braced back. Extend the hips hard, guide the bag up the torso, and catch it on one shoulder and stand tall. Use the same shoulder until the set calls for a switch, keeping the bag close instead of rowing it away from the body.

ff_heavy_sandbag_over_the_shoulder.description: Straddle the heavy sandbag, hinge, and lap it close with a braced back. Extend the hips hard, guide the bag up the torso, and continue the drive so the bag rolls over the shoulder behind you. Use the same shoulder until the set calls for a switch, keeping the bag close instead of rowing it away from the body.

ff_heavy_sandbag_alternating_shoulder_cl.description: Straddle the heavy sandbag, hinge, and lap it close with a braced back. Extend the hips hard, guide the bag up the torso, and catch it on one shoulder and stand tall. Alternate shoulders each rep, keeping the bag close instead of rowing it away from the body.

ff_heavy_sandbag_alternating_over_the_sh.description: Straddle the heavy sandbag, hinge, and lap it close with a braced back. Extend the hips hard, guide the bag up the torso, and continue the drive so the bag rolls over the shoulder behind you. Alternate shoulders each rep, keeping the bag close instead of rowing it away from the body.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_walking_lunge.description: Clean the heavy sandbag into a bear hug high on the chest. Step into the walking lunge and lower until the front leg controls the lunge, then drive through the front foot and continue. Keep the bag squeezed to the torso and avoid letting it pull the chest down.

ff_double_dumbbell_turkish_get_up.description: Lie on your back with a dumbbell locked out in each hand over the shoulders. Move through elbow, hand, bridge, half-kneeling, and stand while both weights stay stacked. Reverse slowly and avoid rushing when the dumbbells drift away from vertical.

ff_single_arm_macebell_reverse_mill.description: Hold one macebell in a guard position with feet grounded. Lead with the elbow through the reverse mill, circling away from the centerline before the mace casts behind the shoulder. Keep the wrist quiet and turn the torso only enough to keep the head path smooth.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_sissy_squat.description: Stand tall with feet about hip width and hold a rack or post lightly with one hand. Rise onto the balls of the feet as the knees travel forward and the torso leans back in one line. Return by driving through the toes, avoiding a hip hinge or collapse at the waist.

ff_bodyweight_sissy_squat.description: Stand tall for the sissy squat with feet about hip width and arms forward for balance. Rise onto the balls of the feet as the knees travel forward and the torso leans back in one line. Return by driving through the toes, avoiding a hip hinge or collapse at the waist.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_knee_over_to.description: Bodyweight Hand Assisted Knee Over Toe Split Squat: use a light hand support beside you and set a split stance with the front foot flat. Let the front knee travel forward over the toes as you lower, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the heel controlled and avoid collapsing the arch or tipping the torso forward.

ff_superband_sissy_squat.description: Superband Sissy Squat: anchor a superband behind the knees or hips so it assists the upright lean of the sissy squat. Rise onto the balls of the feet, send the knees forward, and lower with the body in one long line before pulling back to stand. Keep the hips extended and avoid folding at the waist to make the rep easier.
