ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_walking_lu.description: Double Dumbbell Front Rack Walking Lunge: hold the dumbbells in the front rack position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_walking_.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Suitcase Walking Lunge: hold the dumbbell in the suitcase position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_walking_.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Overhead Walking Lunge: hold the dumbbell in the overhead position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_walkin.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Front Rack Walking Lunge: hold the dumbbell in the front rack position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_walking_lunge.description: Barbell Front Rack Walking Lunge: hold the barbell in the front rack position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_horse_stance_squat.description: Kettlebell Goblet Horse Stance Squat: hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height. Set a wide horse stance with toes slightly turned out, then sit straight down between the heels. Drive through both feet to stand, keeping the chest tall and knees outside the arches.

ff_double_kettlebell_alternating_forward.description: Double Kettlebell Alternating Forward Lunge Snatch: start with two kettlebells hanging at the sides. Step forward into a lunge, drive back to standing, and guide the bells into a snatch as you alternate legs. Keep the bells close and avoid pulling them in a wide arc.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_rever.description: Alternating Single Arm Clubbell Reverse Mill: grip the clubbell securely and set an athletic stance. Guide the reverse mill arc beside and behind the body, rotating through the shoulders while the handle stays controlled. Keep the path smooth and avoid leaning back or letting the head of the tool crash through the turn.

ff_macebell_mill.description: Macebell Mill: grip the macebell securely and set an athletic stance. Guide the mill arc beside and behind the body, rotating through the shoulders while the handle stays controlled. Keep the path smooth and avoid leaning back or letting the head of the tool crash through the turn.

ff_double_clubbell_order_walking_lunge.description: Double Clubbell Order Walking Lunge: hold the clubbells in the order position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_walking_lun.description: Single Arm Clubbell Order Walking Lunge: hold the clubbell in the order position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_clubbell_order_walking_lunge.description: Clubbell Order Walking Lunge: hold the clubbell in the order position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_bodyweight_box_jump.description: Bodyweight Box Jump: stand facing a stable box with feet about hip width. Dip the hips, swing the arms, and jump onto the box, landing softly with the whole foot then step down before the next rep. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes instead of rebounding immediately.

ff_bodyweight_lateral_box_jump.description: Bodyweight Lateral Box Jump: stand beside a stable box with feet about hip width. Dip the hips, swing the arms, and jump sideways onto the box, landing softly with the whole foot then step down before the next rep. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes instead of rebounding immediately.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_lateral_box_ju.description: Bodyweight Alternating Lateral Box Jump: stand beside a stable box with knees soft. Dip the hips and jump sideways onto the box, landing with the whole foot before stepping down to alternate directions. Stick each landing with knees aligned over toes instead of rebounding immediately.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_planche.description: Bodyweight Straddle Planche: place the hands on the floor, turn the elbows straight, and lean the shoulders well forward of the wrists. Hold the body in a straddle planche shape with legs wide and hips level. Push the floor away and step down before the elbows bend or the hips drop.

ff_macebell_single_leg_offset_bent_over_.description: Macebell Single Leg Offset Bent Over Row: balance on one leg and hinge forward with the macebell hanging offset below the shoulder. Row the handle toward the lower ribs, then lower without changing hip height. Keep the pelvis square and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bird_dog_row.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bird Dog Row: set one hand and the opposite knee on a bench, then extend the free leg. Hold the kettlebell under the free shoulder and row it toward the hip. Keep the hips square instead of twisting open as the bell rises.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_snatch_to_fron.description: Double Kettlebell Half Snatch to Front Rack Squat: swing or pull both kettlebells close and punch into a controlled half-snatch catch. Lower them to the front rack, squat, and stand before resetting. Keep the bells close on the drop and avoid crashing them into the forearms.

ff_single_arm_suspension_ipsilateral_pis.description: Single Arm Suspension Ipsilateral Pistol Squat: hold the suspension trainer securely with the shoulder packed and the handle controlled. Move through the named arc with a braced torso and smooth hand path. Use a lighter tool if the bell pulls the ribs open or changes your stance.

ff_kettlebell_single_leg_standing_hip_fl.description: Kettlebell Single Leg Standing Hip Flexion: hold a kettlebell in front of the body and stand tall on one leg. Lift the free knee into hip flexion without leaning back, then lower the foot with control. Keep the pelvis level and avoid swinging the leg to create height.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebells in the bottoms-up overhead position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Work one side at a time or return through center before repeating. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_360.description: Alternating Single Arm Macebell 360: hold the macebell near the lower handle and start with the head vertical in front of the chest. Cast it around one side of the head and behind the body, then pull it back to the front while alternating lead hands or directions each rep. Keep elbows close and ribs down instead of letting the weight yank the shoulders open.

ff_double_kettlebell_low_hold_cossack_sq.description: Double Kettlebell Low Hold Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebells in the low hold position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Work one side at a time or return through center before repeating. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_low_hold_alternatin.description: Double Kettlebell Low Hold Alternating Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebells in the low hold position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Move side to side each rep through the center. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_cossack.description: Kettlebell Goblet Alternating Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebell in the goblet position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Move side to side each rep through the center. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_carry.description: Barbell Zercher Carry: clean or pick up the barbell into the Zercher position before you start walking. Take short, quiet steps while keeping the ribs down, shoulders level, and gaze forward. Turn around under control and avoid leaning back or sideways to counter the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seated_o.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Seated Overhead Press: hold the kettlebells bottoms-up overhead with the forearm vertical and ribs braced. Press until the arm is stacked overhead, then lower to the start under control. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Alternating Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Seated Overhead Press: sit tall with both kettlebells bottoms-up at the shoulders. Press one bell overhead while the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the wrists stacked and avoid leaning back as the bell wobbles.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seat.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Seated Overhead Press: hold the kettlebell bottoms-up overhead with the forearm vertical and ribs braced. Press until the arm is stacked overhead, then lower to the start under control. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_macebell_offset_overhead_press.description: Macebell Offset Overhead Press: hold the macebell overhead with the forearm vertical and ribs braced. Press until the arm is stacked overhead, then lower to the start under control. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_sots_press.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Sots Press: sit into the bottom of a squat with the dumbbell at shoulder height and the chest lifted. Press the working arm overhead without standing up, then lower back to the rack. Keep heels down and ribs braced instead of leaning back to create room.

ff_double_dumbbell_sots_press.description: Double Dumbbell Sots Press: sit into the bottom of a squat with the dumbbells at shoulder height and the chest lifted. Press the weights overhead without standing up, then lower back to the rack. Keep heels down and ribs braced instead of leaning back to create room.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_alte.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebell in the front rack position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Move side to side each rep through the center. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_ipsilateral_cos.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Ipsilateral Cossack Squat Thruster: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebell in the loaded position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. As you stand, use the leg drive to press the kettlebell overhead, then lower it back to the rack before the next side. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_foot_elevated_cossa.description: Kettlebell Goblet Foot Elevated Cossack Squat: hold the kettlebell at the chest and take a wide stance. Place the straight-side foot on a low support, shift into the working hip, and press back up. Keep the heel down and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Double Dumbbell Overhead Alternating Step Up: Hold the dumbbells in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Alternate lead legs after each controlled step down. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Overhead Contralateral Step Up: Hold the dumbbell overhead opposite the stepping leg. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_step_up.description: Barbell Overhead Alternating Step Up: Hold the barbell in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Alternate lead legs after each controlled step down. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_barbell_overhead_step_up.description: Barbell Overhead Step Up: Hold the barbell in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Suitcase Contralateral Step Up: Hold the dumbbell suitcase opposite the stepping leg. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Suitcase Ipsilateral Step Up: Hold the dumbbell suitcase on the same side as the stepping leg. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Overhead Ipsilateral Step Up: Hold the dumbbell overhead on the same side as the stepping leg. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Alternating Cossack Squat: lock one bottoms-up kettlebell overhead and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, return through center, and alternate sides with the arm still stacked. Keep the wrist vertical and shorten the range if the bell tips.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_low_switch_cossack_.description: Kettlebell Goblet Low Switch Cossack Squat: hold the kettlebell at the chest and take a wide stance. Sit into one hip, stay low through center, and switch to the other side only as far as both heels stay controlled. Keep the chest lifted and the bent knee tracking over the toes.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_cossack_squat.description: Kettlebell Low Hold Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebell in the low hold position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Work one side at a time or return through center before repeating. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_alternating_cossa.description: Kettlebell Low Hold Alternating Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebell in the low hold position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Move side to side each rep through the center. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Ipsilateral Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the kettlebell in the front rack position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Work one side at a time or return through center before repeating. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_carry.description: Double Kettlebell Front Rack Carry: clean or pick up the kettlebells into the front rack position before you start walking. Take short, quiet steps while keeping the ribs down, shoulders level, and gaze forward. Turn around under control and avoid leaning back or sideways to counter the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_carry.description: Double Kettlebell Overhead Carry: clean or pick up the kettlebells into the overhead position before you start walking. Take short, quiet steps while keeping the ribs down, shoulders level, and gaze forward. Turn around under control and avoid leaning back or sideways to counter the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Carry: clean both kettlebells bottoms-up and press them overhead before walking. Take short, quiet steps with ribs down and shoulders level. Turn under control and avoid leaning back to keep the bells vertical.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_carry.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Overhead Carry: clean or pick up the kettlebell into the overhead position before you start walking. Take short, quiet steps while keeping the ribs down, shoulders level, and gaze forward. Turn around under control and avoid leaning back or sideways to counter the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Carry: clean one kettlebell bottoms-up and press it overhead before walking. Take short, quiet steps with the arm stacked over the shoulder. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the bell.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_b.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Half Kneeling Bent Press: rack the kettlebell at one shoulder and set the half-kneeling base before bracing. Hinge and rotate slightly away as you press yourself under the weight, then stand or stack the torso beneath the locked-out arm. Keep your eyes on the bell and avoid pressing with a loose wrist or rounded back.

ff_double_clubbell_order_step_up.description: Double Clubbell Order Step Up: Hold the clubbells in the order position. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_ste.description: Double Clubbell Order Alternating Step Up: Hold the clubbells in the order position. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Alternate lead legs after each controlled step down. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Single Arm Clubbell Order Contralateral Step Up: Hold the clubbell order opposite the stepping leg. Place the whole working foot on a stable box, drive through that foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Single Arm Clubbell Order Ipsilateral Step Up: hold the clubbell in order position on the same side as the stepping leg. Place the whole foot on a stable box, drive up, and step down with control. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_bar_advanced_tuck_back_lever.description: Bar Advanced Tuck Back Lever: hang from the bar and enter the advanced tuck back lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_bar_advanced_tuck_front_lever.description: Bar Advanced Tuck Front Lever: hang from the bar and enter the advanced tuck front lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_sots_pres.description: Alternating Double Dumbbell Sots Press: sit into the bottom of a squat with the dumbbells at shoulder height and the chest lifted. Press the weights overhead without standing up, then lower back to the rack. Keep heels down and ribs braced instead of leaning back to create room.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_sots_press.description: Double Dumbbell Seesaw Sots Press: sit into the bottom of a squat with the dumbbells at shoulder height and the chest lifted. Press the weights overhead without standing up, then lower back to the rack. Keep heels down and ribs braced instead of leaning back to create room.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Suspension Single Leg Standing Bent Knee Mid Row: hold the suspension handles and lean back while standing on one bent-knee leg. Pull the chest toward the handles, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the hips lifted and initiate the pull with the shoulder blades.

ff_bar_circle_front_lever.description: Bar Circle Front Lever: hang from the bar with shoulders depressed and lift into a front lever. Sweep the body through a controlled circular path only as far as you can keep the hips level. Return to the start without bending the elbows or letting momentum carry the circle.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Suspension Single Leg Standing Bent Knee Low Row: lengthen the straps and lean back while balancing on one bent-knee leg. Row the lower ribs toward the handles, then return slowly to straight arms. Keep the pelvis level and avoid dropping the hips to finish the pull.

ff_single_arm_suspension_rotational_row.description: Single Arm Suspension Rotational Row: adjust the suspension trainer and hold one handle while feet planted. Let the torso rotate open as you lower, then row and rotate back to square. Keep the hips lifted and shoulder blades initiating the pull instead of yanking with the biceps.

ff_alternating_double_indian_club_outer_.description: Alternating Double Indian Club Outer Heart Shaped Swing: stand tall with light Indian clubs at your sides. Swing the clubs outward in alternating heart-shaped arcs, letting each wrist turn over smoothly before the next side starts. Keep the shoulders relaxed and avoid speeding up until both paths match.

ff_double_indian_club_parallel_swing.description: Double Indian Club Parallel Swing: stand tall with a light Indian club in each hand. Swing both clubs in parallel arcs beside the body, matching height and tempo from side to side. Keep the wrists smooth and avoid muscling the pattern with stiff shoulders.

ff_suspension_feet_elevated_decline_push.description: Suspension Feet Elevated Decline Push Up: place the feet in the suspension straps and hands under the shoulders in a decline plank. Lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the straps quiet and avoid letting the hips sag below the shoulders.

ff_bar_front_lever_pull_to_inverted_hang.description: Bar Front Lever Pull to Inverted Hang: hang from the bar with active shoulders and lift into a tight front lever. Pull the body as one piece toward an inverted hang, then lower back through the lever with control. Keep arms straight as long as the drill requires and avoid kicking the legs to start the pull.

ff_bar_full_back_lever.description: Bar Full Back Lever: hang from the bar and enter the full back lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_bar_full_front_lever.description: Bar Full Front Lever: hang from the bar and enter the full front lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_slider_atomic_push_up.description: Slider Atomic Push Up: place the feet on sliders and start in a strong push-up plank. Lower the chest, press up, then slide the knees toward the elbows before extending back to plank. Keep the ribs braced and avoid piking the hips to make the tuck easier.

ff_slider_reach_push_up.description: Slider Reach Push Up: put one hand on a slider and set a push-up plank with feet wide enough to stay stable. Lower the chest as the slider hand reaches forward, then pull it back while pressing up. Keep the shoulders square and avoid letting the low back sag.

ff_bar_half_lay_back_lever.description: Bar Half Lay Back Lever: hang from the bar and enter the half-lay back lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_bar_half_lay_front_lever.description: Bar Half Lay Front Lever: hang from the bar and enter the half-lay front lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_bar_hanging_knee_raise.description: Bar Hanging Knee Raise: hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Lift the knees toward the chest, then lower until the hips are still again. Start each rep from control and avoid swinging the legs to create momentum.

ff_bar_hanging_knees_to_elbows.description: Bar Hanging Knees to Elbows: hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Lift the knees toward the elbows, then lower until the hips are still again. Start each rep from control and avoid swinging the legs to create momentum.

ff_bar_hanging_knees_to_wrists.description: Bar Hanging Knees to Wrists: hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Lift the knees toward the wrists, then lower until the hips are still again. Start each rep from control and avoid swinging the legs to create momentum.

ff_bar_hanging_leg_raise.description: Bar Hanging Leg Raise: hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Lift the straight or softly bent legs upward, then lower until the hips are still again. Start each rep from control and avoid swinging the legs to create momentum.

ff_bar_hanging_toes_to_bar.description: Bar Hanging Toes to Bar: hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Lift the toes toward the bar, then lower until the hips are still again. Start each rep from control and avoid swinging the legs to create momentum.

ff_bar_hanging_oblique_knee_raise.description: Bar Hanging Oblique Knee Raise: hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders pulled away from the ears. Lift the knees toward one side of the chest, then lower until the hips are still again. Start each rep from control and avoid swinging the legs to create momentum.

ff_bar_inverted_hang.description: Bar Inverted Hang: hang from the bar with an overhand grip, then lift the hips until the body is upside down. Hold the inverted position with arms long and shoulders active before lowering under control. Keep the body still and avoid rolling in with a hard swing.

ff_bar_hanging_l_sit_flutter_kicks.description: Bar Hanging L Sit Flutter Kicks: place two kettlebells as stable handles and press down through them. Lift the hips and extend the legs forward into an L-sit, holding only as long as the shoulders stay down. Keep the chest tall and avoid collapsing between the arms.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_i.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Half Kneeling Ipsilateral Overhead Press: hold the kettlebell overhead with the forearm vertical and ribs braced. Press until the arm is stacked overhead, then lower to the start under control. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_suspension_rotational_row.description: Suspension Rotational Row: adjust the suspension trainer and hold both handles while feet planted. Let the torso rotate open as you lower, then row and rotate back to square. Keep the hips lifted and shoulder blades initiating the pull instead of yanking with the biceps.

ff_slider_alternating_reach_push_up.description: Slider Alternating Reach Push Up: put one hand on a slider and set a push-up plank with feet wide enough to stay stable. Lower the chest as the slider hand reaches forward, alternating sides each rep, then pull it back while pressing up. Keep the shoulders square and avoid letting the low back sag.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_z_pre.description: Alternating Single Arm Landmine Z Press: sit on the floor with legs straight and the landmine bar at one shoulder. Press the bar forward and up, alternating arms or sides as prescribed, then lower with control. Keep the torso upright and avoid bending the knees or leaning away from the landmine.

ff_single_arm_macebell_bull_whip.description: Single Arm Macebell Bull Whip: grip the macebell securely and set an athletic stance. Guide the bull-whip arc beside and behind the body, rotating through the shoulders while the handle stays controlled. Keep the path smooth and avoid leaning back or letting the head of the tool crash through the turn.

ff_bar_single_leg_out_back_lever.description: Bar Single Leg Out Back Lever: hang from the bar and enter the single-leg-out back lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_bar_single_leg_out_front_lever.description: Bar Single Leg Out Front Lever: hang from the bar and enter the single-leg-out front lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_push_pre.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Push Press: rack the kettlebells bottoms-up at the shoulders with feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive through the legs, and finish the press overhead before lowering to the rack. Keep the dip vertical and avoid letting the bells tip or drift forward.

ff_bar_straddle_back_lever.description: Bar Straddle Back Lever: hang from the bar and enter the straddle back lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_bar_straddle_front_lever.description: Bar Straddle Front Lever: hang from the bar and enter the straddle front lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Alternating Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Push Press: rack the kettlebells bottoms-up at the shoulders with feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive through the legs, and finish the press overhead before lowering to the rack. Keep the dip vertical and avoid letting the bells tip or drift forward.

Alternating Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Press: hold the kettlebells bottoms-up overhead with the forearm vertical and ribs braced. Press until the arm is stacked overhead, then lower to the start under control. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_bar_tuck_back_lever.description: Bar Tuck Back Lever: hang from the bar and enter the tuck back lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_o.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Seesaw Overhead Press: hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead with a tall brace. Press one side overhead as the other lowers toward the rack, then switch in a smooth seesaw rhythm. Keep both wrists stacked and avoid turning the press into a side bend.

ff_bar_tuck_front_lever.description: Bar Tuck Front Lever: hang from the bar and enter the tuck front lever from a controlled tuck or inverted position. Hold the body line with straight arms and active shoulders, then exit slowly. Use an easier shape if the hips sag, elbows bend, or shoulders shrug.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half_kne.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Half Kneeling Seesaw Overhead Press: hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead with a tall brace. Press one side overhead as the other lowers toward the rack, then switch in a smooth seesaw rhythm. Keep both wrists stacked and avoid turning the press into a side bend.

ff_ring_full_planche_push_up.description: Ring Full Planche Push Up: set the rings under the shoulders and lean forward until the shoulders pass the hands. Hold the straight-body planche line as you bend the elbows, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the hips from dropping and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

ff_ring_straddle_planche_push_up.description: Ring Straddle Planche Push Up: set the rings under the shoulders and lean forward until the shoulders pass the hands. Hold the straddled planche line as you bend the elbows, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the hips from dropping and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

ff_bodyweight_fingertip_full_planche_pus.description: Bodyweight Fingertip Full Planche Push Up: set the hands under the shoulders and lean forward until the shoulders pass the hands. Hold the straight-body planche line as you bend the elbows, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the hips from dropping and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

ff_parallette_wall_supported_handstand_p.description: Parallette Wall Supported Handstand Push Up: grip the parallettes under the shoulders and set a straight plank from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_ring_grip_push_up.description: Ring Grip Push Up: grip the rings under the shoulders and set a straight plank from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_press.description: Single Arm Barbell Overhead Press: hold the barbell overhead with the forearm vertical and ribs braced. Press until the arm is stacked overhead, then lower to the start under control. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_ring_shoulder_stand.description: Ring Shoulder Stand: start in ring support with the straps vertical and elbows straight. Tuck the knees, lift the hips, and carefully stack the body into a shoulder stand between the rings. Keep pressure through the hands and exit slowly instead of dumping weight onto the neck.

ff_ring_strap_handstand.description: Ring Strap Handstand: set the hands on the rings and kick or press into a vertical handstand. Push tall through the shoulders, keep ribs tucked, and balance with small finger pressure changes. Step down under control instead of collapsing through the shoulders.

ff_ring_handstand.description: Ring Handstand: set the hands on the rings and kick or press into a vertical handstand. Push tall through the shoulders, keep ribs tucked, and balance with small finger pressure changes. Step down under control instead of collapsing through the shoulders.

ff_ring_strap_handstand_push_up.description: Ring Strap Handstand Push Up: grip the rings under the shoulders and set a straight plank from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_press_handstand.description: Bodyweight Straddle Press Handstand: place the hands on the floor with feet wide in a straddle. Shift the shoulders over the hands, press the hips upward, and bring the legs to a stacked handstand. Move slowly and avoid jumping past the press with a loose back.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_step_up.description: Bodyweight Alternating Step Up: stand facing a stable box with arms free for balance. Place the whole foot on the box, drive through that leg to stand, then step down and alternate sides. Keep the knee aligned over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing foot.

ff_bodyweight_step_up.description: Bodyweight Step Up: stand facing a stable box with one foot fully planted on top. Drive through the box foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Complete the planned reps on one lead leg before switching, and avoid pushing off the trailing foot.

ff_macebell_order_cossack_squat.description: Macebell Order Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the macebell in the order position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Work one side at a time or return through center before repeating. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_cossack_sq.description: Macebell Order Alternating Cossack Squat: take a wide stance and hold the macebell in the order position. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then press back through the whole foot. Move side to side each rep through the center. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_macebell_order_low_switch_cossack_squ.description: Macebell Order Low Switch Cossack Squat: hold the macebell in the order position and take a wide stance. Sit into one hip, stay low through center, and switch to the other side only as far as both heels stay controlled. Keep the mace close and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_macebell_order_foot_elevated_cossack_.description: Macebell Order Foot Elevated Cossack Squat: hold the macebell in the order position and place the straight-side foot on a low support. Shift into the working hip, bend that knee, then press back through the whole foot. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the mace pull you forward.

ff_bodyweight_pike_press_handstand.description: Bodyweight Pike Press Handstand: start in a pike with hands on the floor and hips high. Shift the shoulders over the hands, press the hips upward, and bring the legs toward a stacked handstand. Move slowly and avoid jumping past the press with a loose back.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_straddle_press_ha.description: Parallette L Sit to Straddle Press Handstand: place two kettlebells as stable handles and press down through them. Lift the hips and extend the legs forward into an L-sit, holding only as long as the shoulders stay down. Keep the chest tall and avoid collapsing between the arms.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_pike_press_handst.description: Parallette L Sit to Pike Press Handstand: place two kettlebells as stable handles and press down through them. Lift the hips and extend the legs forward into an L-sit, holding only as long as the shoulders stay down. Keep the chest tall and avoid collapsing between the arms.

ff_heavy_sandbag_clean_to_push_press.description: Heavy Sandbag Clean to Push Press: start with the heavy sandbag on the floor between the feet. Lap or clean the bag to the front of the body, reset the brace, then dip and drive it overhead in a push press. Keep the bag close and avoid rounding the back as you pick it from the floor.

ff_ring_tuck_front_lever_pull_up.description: Ring Tuck Front Lever Pull Up: hang from the rings and set the tuck front lever position before pulling. Drive the rings toward the ribs while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to the lever or hang with control. Scale the lever shape before letting the legs swing or the hips sag.

ff_ring_advanced_tuck_front_lever_pull_u.description: Ring Advanced Tuck Front Lever Pull Up: hang from the rings and set the advanced tuck front lever position before pulling. Drive the rings toward the ribs while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to the lever or hang with control. Scale the lever shape before letting the legs swing or the hips sag.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_walking_lu.description: Double Kettlebell Suitcase Walking Lunge: hold the kettlebells in the suitcase position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_march.description: Double Clubbell Order March: hold the clubbells in the order position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_march.description: Single Arm Clubbell Order March: hold the clubbell in the order position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_ring_straddle_front_lever_pull_up.description: Ring Straddle Front Lever Pull Up: hang from the rings and set the straddle front lever position before pulling. Drive the rings toward the ribs while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to the lever or hang with control. Scale the lever shape before letting the legs swing or the hips sag.

ff_single_arm_ring_push_up.description: Single Arm Ring Push Up: set one ring low and place the working hand on it with the body in a wide, braced plank. Lower the chest toward the ring, then press back up while the free arm assists only as needed. Keep the ring under control and avoid rotating the hips open.

ff_ring_l_sit_muscle_up.description: Ring L Sit Muscle Up: hang from the rings with a false grip if used and legs held in an L-sit. Pull the rings low to the ribs, roll the chest over the hands, then press to a stable support. Keep the transition close and avoid a big kick to get over the rings.

ff_ring_strict_muscle_up.description: Ring Strict Muscle Up: hang from the rings with a false grip if used and legs quiet below you. Pull the rings low to the ribs, roll the chest over the hands, then press to a stable support. Keep the transition close and avoid a big kick to get over the rings.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_walkin.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Suitcase Walking Lunge: hold the kettlebell in the suitcase position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_walk.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Walking Lunge: hold the kettlebell in the front rack position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_walkin.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Overhead Walking Lunge: hold the kettlebell in the overhead position before taking a long, steady step. Lower both knees under control, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next stride. Keep the torso stacked and avoid short steps that make the front knee dive inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Walking Lunge: clean one kettlebell bottoms-up and press it overhead. Step into each lunge, lower both knees, and drive through the front foot into the next stride. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid leaning away from the bell.

ff_single_arm_landmine_isometric_split_s.description: Single Arm Landmine Isometric Split Squat Shoulder Press: set a split stance and hold the landmine sleeve at one shoulder. Sink into the split squat and hold that depth while pressing the bar forward and up. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid rotating toward the bar.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_pendulum_t.description: Single Arm Clubbell Inside Pendulum to Outside Pendulum: hold one clubbell and stand with knees soft. Swing through the inside pendulum, then guide the club into the outside pendulum path while the torso stays braced. Keep the bell close enough to control and avoid twisting the low back to chase range.

ff_clubbell_inside_pendulum.description: Clubbell Inside Pendulum: hold the clubbell securely and stand with knees soft. Swing the club through an inside pendulum path, letting the shoulder guide the arc while the torso stays braced. Keep the bell close enough to control and avoid twisting the low back to chase range.

ff_clubbell_inside_pendulum_to_outside_p.description: Clubbell Inside Pendulum to Outside Pendulum: hold the clubbell securely and stand with knees soft. Swing through the inside pendulum, then guide the club into the outside pendulum path with a steady grip. Keep the bell close enough to control and avoid twisting the low back to chase range.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_outside_circle.description: Single Arm Clubbell Outside Circle: stand tall with the clubbell in the single-arm setup. Trace the outside circle around the shoulder with a soft elbow and steady grip. Keep the ribs down and slow the circle if the club pulls you off balance.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_outside_c.description: Alternating Double Clubbell Outside Circle: stand tall with the clubbells in the two-hand or double-club setup. Trace the outside circle around the shoulder with a soft elbow and steady grip. Keep the ribs down and slow the circle if the club pulls you off balance.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_circle_to_outs.description: Double Clubbell Inside Circle to Outside Circle: stand tall with the clubbells in the two-hand or double-club setup. Trace the inside circle into the outside circle around the shoulder with a soft elbow and steady grip. Keep the ribs down and slow the circle if the club pulls you off balance.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Squat: hold the kettlebell securely with the shoulder packed and the handle controlled. Move through the named arc with a braced torso and smooth hand path. Use a lighter tool if the bell pulls the ribs open or changes your stance.

ff_kettlebell_romanian_deadlift.description: Kettlebell Romanian Deadlift: hold the kettlebell with arms long and knees softly bent. Hinge by pushing the hips back until the hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the bell close and avoid rounding the back to reach lower.

ff_barbell_snatch_balance.description: Barbell Snatch Balance: hold the barbell in a snatch-width grip on the upper back with feet in your pulling stance. Dip straight down, drive under the bar, and punch into an overhead squat receiving position before standing. Keep the bar stacked over midfoot and avoid pressing it out after you land.

ff_parallette_straddle_press_to_handstan.description: Parallette Straddle Press to Handstand: start on the parallettes with legs wide and hips piked. Shift the shoulders over the hands, press the hips upward, and bring the legs to a stacked handstand. Keep elbows locked and avoid jumping past the press.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_march.description: Double Dumbbell Suitcase March: hold the dumbbells in the suitcase position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_march.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Suitcase March: hold the dumbbell in the suitcase position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_march.description: Double Dumbbell Front Rack March: hold the dumbbells in the front rack position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_march.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Front Rack March: hold the dumbbell in the front rack position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_march.description: Double Dumbbell Overhead March: hold the dumbbells in the overhead position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_march.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Overhead March: hold the dumbbell in the overhead position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_barbell_front_rack_march.description: Barbell Front Rack March: hold the barbell in the front rack position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_barbell_back_rack_march.description: Barbell Back Rack March: hold the barbell in the back rack position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_forward.description: Barbell Back Rack Alternating Forward Lunge: set the barbell across the upper back and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid tipping the chest forward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_reverse.description: Barbell Back Rack Alternating Reverse Lunge: set the barbell across the upper back and stand tall. Step one foot back into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot and alternate. Keep the torso stacked and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_lateral_.description: Barbell Overhead Alternating Lateral Lunge: press the barbell overhead and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep the bar stacked over midfoot and the working knee aligned over the toes.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_latera.description: Barbell Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge: set the barbell in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep elbows high and the working knee aligned over the toes.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_lateral.description: Barbell Back Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge: set the barbell across the upper back and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep the working foot flat and the knee tracking over the toes.

ff_kettlebell_single_leg_romanian_deadli.description: Kettlebell Single Leg Romanian Deadlift: hold the kettlebell with arms long and knees softly bent. Hinge by pushing the hips back until the hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the bell close and avoid rounding the back to reach lower.

ff_single_arm_ring_eccentric_chin_up.description: Single Arm Ring Eccentric Chin Up: hang from the rings with palms facing you and shoulders active. Pull the sternum toward the rings, then lower as slowly as you can on the working arm. Keep the body quiet and avoid kicking or craning the neck to finish.

ff_parallette_freestanding_handstand.description: Parallette Freestanding Handstand: set the hands on the parallettes and kick or press into a vertical handstand. Push tall through the shoulders, keep ribs tucked, and balance with small finger pressure changes. Step down under control instead of collapsing through the shoulders.

ff_ring_gironda_sternum_chin_up.description: Ring Gironda Sternum Chin Up: hang from the rings with palms facing you and shoulders active. Pull the sternum toward the rings, keeping the chest high before lowering to straight arms. Keep the body quiet and avoid kicking or craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Contralateral Pistol Squat: rack the kettlebell opposite the working leg and stand on one foot. Reach the free leg forward, sit into the pistol, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the heel grounded and stop before the knee caves inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Ipsilateral Pistol Squat: hold the kettlebell securely with the shoulder packed and the handle controlled. Move through the named arc with a braced torso and smooth hand path. Use a lighter tool if the bell pulls the ribs open or changes your stance.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Contralateral Pistol Squat: rack the kettlebell bottoms-up opposite the working leg. Reach the free leg forward, sit into the pistol, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the wrist vertical and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Ipsilateral Pistol Squat: rack the kettlebell bottoms-up on the working-leg side. Reach the free leg forward, sit into the pistol, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_pistol_squat.description: Kettlebell Horn Grip Pistol Squat: hold the kettlebell securely with the shoulder packed and the handle controlled. Move through the named arc with a braced torso and smooth hand path. Use a lighter tool if the bell pulls the ribs open or changes your stance.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Overhead Contralateral Pistol Squat: hold the kettlebell securely with the shoulder packed and the handle controlled. Move through the named arc with a braced torso and smooth hand path. Use a lighter tool if the bell pulls the ribs open or changes your stance.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Overhead Ipsilateral Pistol Squat: hold the kettlebell securely with the shoulder packed and the handle controlled. Move through the named arc with a braced torso and smooth hand path. Use a lighter tool if the bell pulls the ribs open or changes your stance.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Contralateral Pistol Squat: press the bottoms-up kettlebell overhead opposite the working leg. Reach the free leg forward, sit into the pistol, then stand through the whole foot. Keep the wrist stacked and shorten the range if the bell tips.

Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Overhead Ipsilateral Pistol Squat: press the bottoms-up kettlebell overhead on the working-leg side. Reach the free leg forward, sit into the pistol, then stand through the whole foot. Keep the arm stacked and stop before the knee collapses inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_l_sit.description: Double Kettlebell L Sit: place two kettlebells as stable handles and press down through them. Lift the hips and extend the legs forward into an L-sit, holding only as long as the shoulders stay down. Keep the chest tall and avoid collapsing between the arms.

ff_double_kettlebell_l_sit_lift_over.description: Double Kettlebell L Sit Lift Over: set two kettlebells as hand supports and press the handles down to lift into an L-sit. Keeping the legs straight or softly bent, lift them over a small target from one side to the other. Keep shoulders depressed and avoid rocking backward to clear the object.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_wind.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Windmill: clean one kettlebell bottoms-up and lock it overhead. Angle the feet away from the loaded side, push the hips back, and slide the free hand down the leg. Keep eyes on the bell and avoid letting the wrist tip or the back round.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_bent.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Bent Press: rack the kettlebell bottoms-up at one shoulder and set the feet before bracing. Hinge and rotate slightly away as you press yourself under the weight, then stand or stack the torso beneath the locked-out arm. Keep your eyes on the bell and avoid pressing with a loose wrist or rounded back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Half Kneeling Bent Press: set a half-kneeling base and rack the kettlebell bottoms-up. Hinge and rotate slightly away as you press yourself under the bell, then stack the torso beneath the locked-out arm. Keep eyes on the bell and avoid a loose wrist.

ff_ring_l_sit_archer_pull_up.description: Ring L Sit Archer Pull Up: place two kettlebells as stable handles and press down through them. Lift the hips and extend the legs forward into an L-sit, holding only as long as the shoulders stay down. Keep the chest tall and avoid collapsing between the arms.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_march.description: Double Kettlebell Overhead March: hold the kettlebells in the overhead position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_march.description: Single Arm Kettlebell Overhead March: hold the kettlebell in the overhead position and stand tall before lifting one knee. March in place or forward with slow, even steps, returning each foot quietly under the hips. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Double Kettlebell Suitcase Alternating Forward Lunge: hold kettlebells at your sides and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep shoulders level and avoid swinging the bells.

Double Kettlebell Suitcase Alternating Lateral Lunge: hold kettlebells at your sides and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep the working foot flat and avoid twisting toward one bell.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Double Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge: rack both kettlebells and stand tall with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep elbows close and the working knee aligned over the toes.

Double Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Forward Lunge: hold two kettlebells in the front rack and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep elbows close and avoid tipping forward.

Double Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Reverse Lunge: hold two kettlebells in the front rack and stand tall. Step one foot back into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot and alternate. Keep ribs down and the front knee aligned over the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge: rack both kettlebells bottoms-up and stand tall. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep elbows under the bells and shorten the step if either wrist tips.

Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Alternating Forward Lunge: rack both kettlebells bottoms-up and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the front knee cave.

Double Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Alternating Reverse Lunge: rack both kettlebells bottoms-up and stand tall. Step one foot back into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot and alternate. Keep the bells vertical and avoid tipping forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_alternating_.description: Double Dumbbell Suitcase Alternating Lateral Lunge: hold dumbbells at your sides and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep the working foot flat and avoid twisting toward one dumbbell.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_alternatin.description: Double Dumbbell Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge: hold dumbbells at the shoulders and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep elbows lifted and the working knee over the toes.

Double Dumbbell Front Rack Alternating Forward Lunge: hold dumbbells at the shoulders and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep elbows lifted and avoid tipping forward.

Double Dumbbell Front Rack Alternating Reverse Lunge: hold dumbbells at the shoulders and stand tall. Step one foot back into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot and alternate. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Double Dumbbell Overhead Alternating Lateral Lunge: press both dumbbells overhead and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate. Keep the weights stacked and the working knee over the toes.

Double Dumbbell Overhead Alternating Forward Lunge: press both dumbbells overhead and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep the weights stacked over the shoulders and avoid arching.

Double Dumbbell Overhead Alternating Reverse Lunge: press both dumbbells overhead and stand tall. Step one foot back into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot and alternate. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weights drift forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_alternat.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Suitcase Alternating Forward Lunge: hold one dumbbell at your side and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive back to start and alternate legs. Keep shoulders level and avoid leaning toward the dumbbell.

Single Arm Dumbbell Suitcase Alternating Reverse Lunge: hold one dumbbell at your side and stand tall. Step one foot back into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot and alternate. Keep the torso upright and avoid leaning toward the dumbbell.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_low_h.description: Alternating Single Arm Dumbbell Low Hold Alternating Lateral Lunge: hold one dumbbell low in the working hand and stand tall. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and switch hands as you alternate. Keep the working foot flat and avoid twisting toward the weight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_altern.description: Single Arm Dumbbell Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge: hold one dumbbell at the shoulder and stand with feet under the hips. Step wide to one side, sit into that hip, then push back to center and alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and knee aligned over the toes.

Hold the dumbbell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

Hold the dumbbell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_alternat.description: Lock the dumbbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

Lock the dumbbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

Lock the dumbbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Lock the kettlebells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

Lock the kettlebells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

Lock the kettlebells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_lateral_l.description: Hold the dumbbells close to the chest in a goblet position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_forward_l.description: Hold the dumbbells close to the chest in a goblet position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_reverse_l.description: Hold the dumbbells close to the chest in a goblet position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_lateral_lu.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Clubbell Order Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_forward_lu.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes. Use the Clubbell Order Alternating Forward Lunge setup.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_reverse_lu.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load. Use the Clubbell Order Alternating Reverse Lunge setup.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_lat.description: Hold the clubbells in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_for.description: Hold the clubbells in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_rev.description: Hold the clubbells in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_alternating.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Single Arm Clubbell Order Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes. Use the Single Arm Clubbell Order Alternating Forward Lunge setup.

Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load. Use the Single Arm Clubbell Order Alternating Reverse Lunge setup.

ff_landmine_low_hold_alternating_lateral.description: Hold the landmine bar low at your sides and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_landmine_goblet_alternating_lateral_l.description: Hold the landmine bar close to the chest in a goblet position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_landmine_goblet_alternating_reverse_l.description: Hold the landmine bar close to the chest in a goblet position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_lateral_lu.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_forward_lu.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_reverse_lu.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Lock the bottoms-up kettlebells overhead with wrists stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

Lock the bottoms-up kettlebells overhead with wrists stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

Lock the bottoms-up kettlebells overhead with wrists stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Hold the kettlebell at your side like a suitcase and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_cyclist_sq.description: Hold the dumbbells in a front-rack position at the shoulders. Elevate the heels, use a narrow stance, and squat straight down before standing through the balls and heels of the feet. Keep the knees tracking with the toes and avoid relaxing the brace at the bottom.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_cyclist_squa.description: Lock the dumbbells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders. Elevate the heels, use a narrow stance, and squat straight down before standing through the balls and heels of the feet. Keep the knees tracking with the toes and avoid relaxing the brace at the bottom.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_cyclis.description: Hold the dumbbell in a front-rack position at the shoulder. Elevate the heels, use a narrow stance, and squat straight down before standing through the balls and heels of the feet. Keep the knees tracking with the toes and avoid relaxing the brace at the bottom.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_cyclist_.description: Lock the dumbbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder. Elevate the heels, use a narrow stance, and squat straight down before standing through the balls and heels of the feet. Keep the knees tracking with the toes and avoid relaxing the brace at the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_lateral_lunge.description: Set up tall with no external load and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Bodyweight Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_forward_lunge.description: Set up tall with no external load and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_reverse_lunge.description: Set up tall with no external load and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_macebell_order_cyclist_squat.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Elevate the heels, use a narrow stance, and squat straight down before standing through the balls and heels of the feet. Keep the knees tracking with the toes and avoid relaxing the brace at the bottom.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_squat.description: Lock the dumbbells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders. Set the feet about shoulder width, sit the hips between the heels, and stand while keeping the load over the midfoot. Keep the ribs down and shorten depth if the arms drift forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_squat.description: Lock the dumbbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder. Set the feet about shoulder width, sit the hips between the heels, and stand while keeping the load over the midfoot. Keep the ribs down and shorten depth if the arms drift forward.

ff_macebell_order_squat.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Set the feet about shoulder width, sit the hips between the heels, and stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_bulgarian_.description: Hold the dumbbells in a front-rack position at the shoulders.
