Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_bulgarian_sp.description: Lock the dumbbells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Lock the dumbbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder on the same side as the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Hold the dumbbell at your side like a suitcase on the opposite the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Hold the dumbbell at your side like a suitcase on the same side as the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Hold the dumbbell in a front-rack position at the shoulder on the opposite the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Hold the dumbbell in a front-rack position at the shoulder on the same side as the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and keep the loaded position steady.

ff_clubbell_order_march.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. March in place or forward by lifting one knee at a time while the loaded position stays quiet. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_macebell_order_walking_lunge.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep steps long enough to stay balanced and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_suspension_alternating_lateral_lunge.description: Set up tall with no external load and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Suspension Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_l.description: Rest the Bulgarian bag across the upper back in a back-rack position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_walking_lunge.description: Rest the Bulgarian bag across the upper back in a back-rack position. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep steps long enough to stay balanced and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_f.description: Rest the Bulgarian bag across the upper back in a back-rack position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_la.description: Hold the sandbag securely and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Heavy Sandbag Bear Hug Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_la.description: Hold the sandbag securely and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Heavy Sandbag Shoulder Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_latera.description: Hold the sandbag in a front-rack position at the shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_walking_lunge.description: Hold the sandbag in a front-rack position at the shoulders. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep steps long enough to stay balanced and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_forwar.description: Hold the sandbag in a front-rack position at the shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_revers.description: Hold the sandbag in a front-rack position at the shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_plate_overhead_walking_lunge.description: Lock the plate overhead with arms stacked over shoulders. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep steps long enough to stay balanced and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_lateral_lu.description: Lock the plate overhead with arms stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_forward_lu.description: Lock the plate overhead with arms stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_reverse_lu.description: Lock the plate overhead with arms stacked over shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_barbell_single_leg_standing_bent_knee.description: Lock the barbell overhead with arms stacked over shoulders. Stand on one leg with the free knee bent and lifted enough to challenge balance. Press overhead until the arms are straight, then lower back to the shoulders. Brace the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_single_arm_barbell_single_leg_standin.description: Lock the barbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder on the opposite the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free knee bent and lifted enough to challenge balance. Press overhead until the arms are straight, then lower back to the shoulders. Brace the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_bodyweight_tuck_planche.description: Set the hands on the floor and lean the shoulders forward while keeping the elbows locked. Tuck the knees or hold the planche shape with the floor pressed away. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid jumping into a bent-elbow hold.

ff_single_arm_barbell_single_leg_standin.description: Lock the barbell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder on the same side as the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free knee bent and lifted enough to challenge balance. Press overhead until the arms are straight, then lower back to the shoulders. Brace the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_double_kettlebell_push_up_to_l_sit.description: Set two kettlebells on the floor as handles and start in a straight-body push-up plank. Lower the chest between the bells, press back up, then step or jump the feet through to a supported L-sit. Press the handles down and keep the ribs tight so the shoulders do not sink or the hips drag.

ff_bar_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Bar Hanging L Sit setup.

ff_ring_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Ring Hanging L Sit setup.

ff_single_arm_bar_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Single Arm Bar Hanging L Sit setup.

ff_single_arm_ring_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Single Arm Ring Hanging L Sit setup.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Hold the kettlebell at your side like a suitcase and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_parallette_l_sit_flutter_kicks.description: Press down through the parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pushed away from the ears. Lift the hips, extend or tuck the legs in front, and hold the seated support shape without sinking between the shoulders. Keep the ribs tight and avoid bending the elbows to buy height. Use the Parallette L Sit Flutter Kicks setup.

ff_bodyweight_single_arm_contralateral_s.description: Set the hands on the floor under the shoulders and form a straight line from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back until the arms are straight. Keep the ribs braced and avoid reaching the head forward to finish the rep. Use the Bodyweight Single Arm Contralateral Single Leg Push Up setup.

ff_parallette_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Set the hands on the parallettes and lean the shoulders forward past the wrists into the tuck or pseudo-planche position. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid dropping the hips or flaring the elbows.

ff_ring_maltese_push_up.description: Set the hands on the rings under the shoulders and form a straight line from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back until the arms are straight. Keep the ribs braced and avoid reaching the head forward to finish the rep.

ff_ring_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Set the hands on the rings and lean the shoulders forward past the wrists into the tuck or pseudo-planche position. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid dropping the hips or flaring the elbows. Use the Ring Tuck Planche Push Up setup.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_tuck_planche_push.description: Start in an L-sit on parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean the shoulders forward into a tuck planche, then bend the elbows for the push-up. Press back to support without dropping the hips or letting the elbows flare.

ff_ring_l_sit_to_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Start in a ring L-sit with straps vertical, arms straight, and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean forward into a tuck planche, then lower into a controlled ring push-up. Keep the rings close and shorten the lean if the straps swing or the elbows flare.

ff_bar_advanced_tuck_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar, set the shoulders down, and lift into a advanced tuck front-lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to the lever hang. Use a smaller tuck if needed and avoid kicking the legs to start the pull.

ff_bar_gironda_sternum_chin_up.description: Set up for Bar Gironda Sternum Chin Up with the implement controlled and the body braced. Move through the main pattern slowly enough to keep balance and alignment. Keep the loaded position steady and reduce speed or range if the torso twists or the joints lose position.

ff_bar_l_sit_archer_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Bar L Sit Archer Pull Up setup.

ff_bar_straddle_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar, set the shoulders down, and lift into a straddle front-lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to the lever hang. Use a smaller tuck if needed and avoid kicking the legs to start the pull.

ff_bar_strict_muscle_up.description: Hang from the bar with a strong overhand or false grip and shoulders active. Pull the chest high, roll the shoulders over the bar, and press to a straight-arm support. Keep the bar close and avoid turning a strict rep into a big kip.

ff_bar_tuck_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar, set the shoulders down, and lift into a tuck front-lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to the lever hang. Use a smaller tuck if needed and avoid kicking the legs to start the pull.

ff_bodyweight_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Set the hands on the floor and lean the shoulders forward past the wrists into the tuck or pseudo-planche position. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid dropping the hips or flaring the elbows.

ff_double_clubbell_asynchronous_mill.description: Stand tall with a pair of clubbells and start with the handle controlled near the shoulder. Guide each bell through the mill path around the shoulder and back to the front, matching the asynchronous timing if shown. Keep the ribs down and avoid letting the arc pull you into a back bend.

ff_clubbell_order_step_up.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then step down under control and alternate sides if required. Avoid pushing hard off the trailing leg or letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_step_up.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then step down under control and alternate sides if required. Avoid pushing hard off the trailing leg or letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_bodyweight_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands with arms straight and shoulders pushed away from the ears. Lift the hips, extend or tuck the legs in front, and hold the seated support shape without sinking between the shoulders. Keep the ribs tight and avoid bending the elbows to buy height. Use the Bodyweight L Sit setup.

ff_bodyweight_single_arm_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands with arms straight and shoulders pushed away from the ears. Lift the hips, extend or tuck the legs in front, and hold the seated support shape without sinking between the shoulders. Keep the ribs tight and avoid bending the elbows to buy height. Use the Bodyweight Single Arm L Sit setup.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_low.description: Hold the kettlebell at your side like a suitcase and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_alte.description: Hold the kettlebell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

Hold the kettlebell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes. Use the Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Forward Lunge setup.

Hold the kettlebell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load. Use the Single Arm Kettlebell Front Rack Alternating Reverse Lunge setup.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the kettlebell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load. Use the Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Alternating Lateral Lunge setup.

Hold the kettlebell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes. Use the Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Alternating Forward Lunge setup.

ff_parallette_v_sit_to_pike_press_handst.description: Start in a V-sit on parallettes with arms straight and legs lifted. Fold the hips high, shift weight into the hands, and press the legs upward until they stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand and avoid jumping past the pike press.

ff_ring_pseudo_planche_push_up.description: Set the hands on the rings and lean the shoulders forward past the wrists into the tuck or pseudo-planche position. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid dropping the hips or flaring the elbows. Use the Ring Pseudo Planche Push Up setup.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled on the opposite the working leg. Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out. Shift into one hip while the other leg stays long, then push through the bent-leg foot to return or switch sides. Use the Single Arm Macebell Order Contralateral Cossack Squat setup.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_low_switch_.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out. Shift into one hip while the other leg stays long, then push through the bent-leg foot to return or switch sides. Keep the working heel planted and avoid folding the chest toward the floor.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_alternating.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled and alternate sides with the same setup. Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out. Shift into one hip while the other leg stays long, then push through the bent-leg foot to return or switch sides. Keep the working heel planted and avoid folding the chest toward the floor.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_foot_elevat.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled on the opposite the working leg. Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out. Shift into one hip while the other leg stays long, then push through the bent-leg foot to return or switch sides. Use the Single Arm Macebell Order Foot Elevated Contralateral Cossack Squat setup.

ff_stability_ball_feet_elevated_glute_br.description: Lie on your back with both heels on the stability ball and knees softly bent. Press the heels into the ball to lift the hips, pause, then lower without letting the ball roll away. Keep the pelvis level and avoid arching the low back at the top.

ff_bodyweight_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders and feet behind you. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep the plank steady as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_suspension_feet_elevated_mountain_cli.description: Set the feet in the straps or rings and place the hands under the shoulders in a strong plank. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep the plank steady as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high. Use the Suspension Feet Elevated Mountain Climber setup.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands with arms straight and shoulders pushed away from the ears. Lift the hips, extend or tuck the legs in front, and hold the seated support shape without sinking between the shoulders. Keep the ribs tight and avoid bending the elbows to buy height. Use the Bodyweight Straddle L Sit setup.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the kettlebell in a front-rack position at the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load. Use the Single Arm Kettlebell Bottoms Up Front Rack Alternating Reverse Lunge setup.

ff_bodyweight_cossack_squat.description: Set up tall with no external load. Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out. Shift into one hip while the other leg stays long, then push through the bent-leg foot to return or switch sides. Keep the working heel planted and avoid folding the chest toward the floor.

ff_bodyweight_wall_facing_handstand_push.description: Set the hands on the floor under the shoulders and form a straight line from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back until the arms are straight. Keep the ribs braced and avoid reaching the head forward to finish the rep. Use the Bodyweight Wall Facing Handstand Push Up setup.

ff_ring_pelican_push_up.description: Set the rings or straps low and start in a plank with the hands slightly turned out. Lower the chest while letting the hands drift slightly behind the ribs, then press back to straight arms. Keep the shoulders packed and shorten the range if the elbows flare or the body sags.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_step_up.description: Rest the barbell across the upper back in a back-rack position and alternate sides with the same setup. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then step down under control and alternate sides if required. Avoid pushing hard off the trailing leg or letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_step_u.description: Hold the barbell in a front-rack position at the shoulders and alternate sides with the same setup. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then step down under control and alternate sides if required. Avoid pushing hard off the trailing leg or letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_pelican_push_up.description: Set the rings low, elevate the feet, and start in a straight-body plank with the hands slightly turned out. Lower the chest while letting the hands drift slightly behind the ribs, then press back to straight arms. Keep the shoulders packed and shorten the range if the elbows flare or the body sags.

ff_suspension_pelican_push_up.description: Adjust the suspension handles low and start in a straight-body plank with the hands slightly turned out. Lower the chest while letting the hands drift slightly behind the ribs, then press back to straight arms. Keep the shoulders packed and shorten the range if the elbows flare or the body sags.

ff_ring_tuck_l_sit.description: Press down through the rings with arms straight and shoulders pushed away from the ears. Lift the hips, extend or tuck the legs in front, and hold the seated support shape without sinking between the shoulders. Keep the ribs tight and avoid bending the elbows to buy height. Use the Ring Tuck L Sit setup.

ff_ring_hanging_tuck_l_sit.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Ring Hanging Tuck L Sit setup.

ff_bar_hanging_tuck_l_sit.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs together. Lift the knees or straight legs in front until you reach the tuck, L-sit, straddle, or V-sit shape for the variation. Keep the legs quiet and lower if the body starts swinging. Use the Bar Hanging Tuck L Sit setup.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Lock the kettlebell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

Lock the kettlebell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_ring_mountain_climber.description: Set the feet in the straps or rings and place the hands under the shoulders in a strong plank. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep the plank steady as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high. Use the Ring Mountain Climber setup.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_mountain_climber.description: Set the feet in the straps or rings and place the hands under the shoulders in a strong plank. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep the plank steady as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high. Use the Ring Feet Elevated Mountain Climber setup.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Lock the kettlebell overhead with the arm stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_ring_dead_hang.description: Hang from the rings with hands secure and arms long. Let the body settle while keeping enough shoulder engagement to stay controlled. Breathe steadily and avoid swinging or shrugging hard into the ears.

ff_parallette_kneeling_push_up.description: Set the hands on the parallettes under the shoulders and keep a straight line from shoulders through knees. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back until the arms are straight. Keep the ribs braced and avoid reaching the head forward to finish the rep.

ff_clubbell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall with one clubbell and a secure grip on the handle. Trace the inside or outside circle smoothly around the head or body, keeping elbows soft. Move slowly enough to control the bell and avoid opening the ribs as it passes behind you.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_carry.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the load close and the torso upright. Keep shoulders level and avoid speeding up when the load starts to sway. Use the Single Arm Macebell Order Carry setup.

ff_clubbell_order_carry.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the load close and the torso upright. Keep shoulders level and avoid speeding up when the load starts to sway.

ff_macebell_order_carry.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the load close and the torso upright. Keep shoulders level and avoid speeding up when the load starts to sway. Use the Macebell Order Carry setup.

ff_macebell_order_march.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. March in place or forward by lifting one knee at a time while the loaded position stays quiet. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knee rises. Use the Macebell Order March setup.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_march.description: Hold the macebell in the order position at the shoulder with the handle controlled. March in place or forward by lifting one knee at a time while the loaded position stays quiet. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knee rises. Use the Single Arm Macebell Order March setup.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Lock the bottoms-up kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

Lock the bottoms-up kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step forward, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

Lock the bottoms-up kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked over the shoulder and alternate sides with the same setup. Step back into a reverse lunge, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_stability_ball_ts_ys_is.description: Lie face down over the stability ball with feet wide and light tension through the upper back. Raise the arms into T, Y, and I shapes one at a time, then lower with control. Keep the neck long and avoid shrugging as the arms lift.

ff_resistance_band_reverse_fly.description: Anchor the band or hinge with dumbbells so the arms hang slightly in front of the chest. Open the arms out to the sides with soft elbows, then return until tension stays on the upper back. Keep the neck relaxed and avoid shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.

ff_resistance_band_lateral_raise.description: Stand tall with light dumbbells or band tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders. Use the Resistance Band Lateral Raise setup.

ff_double_dumbbell_lateral_raise.description: Stand tall with light dumbbells or band tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders. Use the Double Dumbbell Lateral Raise setup.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_standing_s.description: Stand tall with the band anchored at elbow height and the working elbow pinned near the ribs. Rotate the forearm away from the body, then return slowly without letting the elbow drift. Use light resistance and avoid twisting the torso to move the hand. Use the Single Arm Resistance Band Standing Shoulder External Rotation setup.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_lateral_raise.description: Sit tall with light dumbbells or band tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_side_lying_should.description: Lie on your side with the working elbow pinned near the ribs and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the forearm away from the body, then return slowly without letting the elbow drift. Use light resistance and avoid twisting the torso to move the hand. Use the Single Arm Dumbbell Side Lying Shoulder External Rotation setup.

ff_miniband_standing_shoulder_external_r.description: Stand tall with the band anchored at elbow height and the working elbow pinned near the ribs. Rotate the forearm away from the body, then return slowly without letting the elbow drift. Use light resistance and avoid twisting the torso to move the hand. Use the Miniband Standing Shoulder External Rotation setup.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_side_lying_should.description: Lie on your side with the working elbow pinned near the ribs and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the forearm across the body, then return slowly without letting the elbow drift. Use light resistance and avoid twisting the torso to move the hand. Use the Single Arm Dumbbell Side Lying Shoulder Internal Rotation setup.

ff_stability_ball_double_dumbbell_seated.description: Sit tall on the stability ball with feet planted wide and dumbbells in front of the shoulders, palms facing you. Rotate the palms forward as you press overhead, then reverse the path back to the start. Keep the ribs stacked over the hips and avoid rolling on the ball.

ff_single_arm_cable_lateral_raise.description: Stand tall with light cable tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_overhead_press.description: Sit tall with the dumbbells at the shoulders and feet planted. Press overhead until the arms are straight, then lower back to the shoulders. Brace the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_press.description: Lock the dumbbells overhead with arms stacked over shoulders. Press overhead until the arms are straight, then lower back to the shoulders. Brace the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_double_dumbbell_shrug.description: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand and arms straight at the sides. Lift the shoulders straight up, pause briefly, then lower them under control. Keep the neck long and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_standing_scaption.description: Stand tall with light dumbbells or band tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms a wide V slightly forward of the body to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders.

ff_ez_bar_kneeling_rollout.description: Grip the EZ bar on the floor with hands under shoulders and brace the abs before moving. Roll the bar forward from the knees or standing position, then pull it back using the abs and lats. Stop before the low back arches or the hips sag. Use the EZ Bar Kneeling Rollout setup.

ff_ez_bar_standing_rollout.description: Grip the EZ bar on the floor with hands under shoulders and brace the abs before moving. Roll the bar forward from the knees or standing position, then pull it back using the abs and lats. Stop before the low back arches or the hips sag. Use the EZ Bar Standing Rollout setup.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_scaption.description: Sit tall with light dumbbells or band tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms a wide V slightly forward of the body to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders.

ff_stability_ball_double_dumbbell_seated.description: Sit tall on the stability ball with feet planted wide with light dumbbells or band tension and arms near the thighs. Raise the arms a wide V slightly forward of the body to about shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight or hiking the shoulders.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_bicep_cur.description: Stand tall with the dumbbells at your sides using a palms-up grip. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the weights with the hips.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Set the upper arm against the incline bench pad with the dumbbell hanging from a straight elbow. Curl the weight toward the shoulder, then lower until the arm is long again. Keep the upper arm glued to the pad and avoid lifting the shoulder to finish. Use the Alternating Double Dumbbell Incline Bench Bicep Curl setup.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_hammer_cu.description: Stand tall with the dumbbells at your sides using a neutral grip. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the weights with the hips. Use the Alternating Double Dumbbell Hammer Curl setup.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_cross_bod.description: Stand tall with the dumbbells at your sides using a neutral grip. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the weights with the hips. Use the Alternating Double Dumbbell Cross Body Hammer Curl setup.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pre.description: Set the upper arm against the incline bench pad with the dumbbell hanging from a straight elbow. Curl the weight toward the shoulder, then lower until the arm is long again. Keep the upper arm glued to the pad and avoid lifting the shoulder to finish. Use the Single Arm Dumbbell Incline Bench Preacher Curl setup.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_bicep_curl.description: Stand tall with the dumbbells at your sides using a palms under one dumbbell end. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the weights with the hips. Use the Dumbbell Crush Grip Bicep Curl setup.

ff_double_dumbbell_zottman_curl.description: Stand tall with the dumbbells at your sides using a palms-up curl then palms-down lower. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the weights with the hips.

ff_dumbbell_waiter_curl.description: Stand tall with the dumbbells at your sides using a palms under one dumbbell end. Curl without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the weights with the hips. Use the Dumbbell Waiter Curl setup.

ff_cable_straight_bar_drag_curl.description: Stand facing the low cable and hold the straight bar with palms up. Curl toward the shoulders or drag the elbows slightly back for the drag-curl path, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back against the stack.

ff_cable_rope_hammer_curl.description: Stand facing the low cable and hold the rope with thumbs up. Curl toward the shoulders or drag the elbows slightly back for the drag-curl path, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back against the stack.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_lateral.description: Hold the kettlebells close to the chest in a goblet position and alternate sides with the same setup. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, and keep the opposite leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the working heel down and avoid twisting the torso toward the load.

ff_suspension_bicep_curl.description: Hold the suspension handles, lean back with arms straight, and keep the body in one rigid line. Curl the handles toward the forehead by bending the elbows, then lower with control. Keep upper arms high and avoid turning the rep into a row.

ff_double_dumbbell_tricep_kickback.description: Hinge forward with a dumbbell in each hand and upper arms held close to the ribs. Straighten the elbows behind you, pause, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid swinging the weights.

ff_tire_jump.description: Stand facing the tire or platform with feet about hip width. Dip, swing the arms, and jump onto or over the target before landing softly. Land with knees tracking over toes and step down to reset if balance breaks.

ff_tire_jump_through.description: Stand beside the tire opening with a clear path through or across it. Dip, jump through the tire space, and land quietly on the other side. Use a distance you can clear without twisting and reset if the landing is loud. Use the Tire Jump Through setup.

ff_tire_broad_jump.description: Stand facing the landing area with feet about hip width and arms ready to swing. Hinge and dip, jump forward over or toward the tire, and land softly with both feet. Stick the landing before the next rep and keep knees aligned over toes.

ff_tire_step_up.description: Hold the tire securely. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then step down under control and alternate sides if required. Avoid pushing hard off the trailing leg or letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_tire_alternating_step_up.description: Hold the tire securely and alternate sides with the same setup. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then step down under control and alternate sides if required. Avoid pushing hard off the trailing leg or letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_tire_alternating_toe_taps_around_the_.description: Stand beside or in front of the tire with one foot ready to tap the edge. Tap the tire with one foot, switch quickly, and travel around the tire in small steps. Stay light on the feet and avoid stomping or letting the knees cave inward.

ff_dumbbell_seated_overhead_tricep_exten.description: Sit or stand tall with the dumbbell held overhead and elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the weight behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep the ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide. Use the Dumbbell Seated Overhead Tricep Extension setup.

ff_tire_lateral_jump_through.description: Stand beside the tire opening with a clear path through or across it. Dip, jump through the tire space, and land quietly on the other side. Use a distance you can clear without twisting and reset if the landing is loud. Use the Tire Lateral Jump Through setup.

ff_tire_carry.description: Grip the tire securely at a height that lets you stand tall. Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the load close and the torso upright. Keep shoulders level and avoid speeding up when the load starts to sway.

ff_dumbbell_overhead_tricep_extension.description: Sit or stand tall with the dumbbell held overhead and elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the weight behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep the ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide. Use the Dumbbell Overhead Tricep Extension setup.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_floor_pres.description: Lie on the floor with dumbbells over the chest, palms facing each other, and elbows close to the ribs. Lower until the upper arms touch the floor, then press the dumbbells back over the chest. Keep wrists stacked and avoid bouncing the elbows into the floor.

ff_barbell_back_rack_carry.description: Rest the barbell across the upper back in a back-rack position. Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the load close and the torso upright. Keep shoulders level and avoid speeding up when the load starts to sway.

ff_single_arm_barbell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold the barbell at your side like a suitcase. Walk with short, controlled steps while keeping the load close and the torso upright. Keep shoulders level and avoid speeding up when the load starts to sway.

ff_suspension_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Set up tall with no external load. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Use the Suspension Bulgarian Split Squat setup.

ff_suspension_bulgarian_split_squat_jump.description: Set up tall with no external load. Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower mostly straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand without bouncing off the back leg. Use the Suspension Bulgarian Split Squat Jump setup.

ff_bodyweight_lateral_bear_crawl.description: Set up on hands and feet with knees bent under the hips and hovering close to the floor. Move sideways with short opposite hand-and-foot steps while the back stays flat. Keep the hips quiet and avoid rocking side to side.

ff_bodyweight_step_up_jump.description: Set up tall with no external load. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot and leave the platform only as high as you can land softly. Land with the knee tracking over the toes and step down to reset instead of rebounding out of control.

ff_tire_step_up_jump.description: Hold the tire securely. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot and leave the platform only as high as you can land softly. Land with the knee tracking over the toes and step down to reset instead of rebounding out of control.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_step_up_jump.description: Set up tall with no external load and alternate sides with the same setup. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot and leave the platform only as high as you can land softly. Land with the knee tracking over the toes and step down to reset instead of rebounding out of control.

ff_tire_alternating_step_up_jump.description: Hold the tire securely and alternate sides with the same setup. Face the box or tire with the whole working foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot and leave the platform only as high as you can land softly. Land with the knee tracking over the toes and step down to reset instead of rebounding out of control.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_push_press.description: Start with the bar over midfoot, hips set, and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, dip, and drive it overhead into a push press. Keep the bar close and reset the brace before driving overhead. Use the Barbell Power Clean to Push Press setup.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_bench_pres.description: Lie on the bench with dumbbells over the chest, palms facing each other, and feet planted. Lower the weights beside the lower chest with elbows close, then press back up. Keep wrists stacked and avoid letting the dumbbells drift toward the face.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance with a pair of clubbells hanging in front or beside the body. Swing through the inside or outside pendulum path with soft knees and a quiet torso. Let the hips and shoulders guide the arc instead of yanking with the wrist. Use the Double Clubbell Inside Pendulum setup.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_outside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance with one clubbell hanging in front or beside the body. Swing through the inside or outside pendulum path with soft knees and a quiet torso. Let the hips and shoulders guide the arc instead of yanking with the wrist. Use the Single Arm Clubbell Outside Pendulum setup.

ff_double_clubbell_outside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance with a pair of clubbells hanging in front or beside the body. Swing through the inside or outside pendulum path with soft knees and a quiet torso. Let the hips and shoulders guide the arc instead of yanking with the wrist. Use the Double Clubbell Outside Pendulum setup.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_pendulum_to_ou.description: Stand in an athletic stance with a pair of clubbells hanging in front or beside the body. Swing through the inside or outside pendulum path with soft knees and a quiet torso. Let the hips and shoulders guide the arc instead of yanking with the wrist. Use the Double Clubbell Inside Pendulum to Outside Pendulum setup.

ff_clubbell_outside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance with one clubbell hanging in front or beside the body. Swing through the inside or outside pendulum path with soft knees and a quiet torso. Let the hips and shoulders guide the arc instead of yanking with the wrist. Use the Clubbell Outside Pendulum setup.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_circle.description: Stand tall with a pair of clubbells and a secure grip on the handle. Trace the inside or outside circle smoothly around the head or body, keeping elbows soft. Move slowly enough to control the bell and avoid opening the ribs as it passes behind you. Use the Double Clubbell Inside Circle setup.

ff_double_clubbell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall with a pair of clubbells and a secure grip on the handle. Trace the inside or outside circle smoothly around the head or body, keeping elbows soft. Move slowly enough to control the bell and avoid opening the ribs as it passes behind you. Use the Double Clubbell Outside Circle setup.

ff_clubbell_side_to_side_swing.description: Stand with the clubbell to one side and hinge at the hips with a long back. Hike the bell slightly, then drive the hips to swing it side to side across the body. Let the hips create the swing and avoid lifting the club with the shoulder.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_incline_be.description: Lie on the incline bench with dumbbells over the chest, palms facing each other, and feet planted. Lower the weights beside the lower chest with elbows close, then press back up. Keep wrists stacked and avoid letting the dumbbells drift toward the face.

ff_cable_rope_tricep_pushdown.description: Set the cable high, hold the rope with elbows pinned near the ribs, and stand tall. Press the rope down until the elbows are straight, then return only until the forearms rise. Keep upper arms still and avoid leaning your bodyweight onto the cable.

ff_barbell_incline_push_up.description: Set a barbell securely in a rack at about waist height and grip it just wider than the shoulders. Walk the feet back into a straight plank, lower the chest toward the bar, then press back to straight arms. Keep the bar over the lower chest and avoid sagging the hips as you push.

ff_bodyweight_kneeling_push_up.description: Kneel on the floor with hands under the shoulders and knees behind the hips so the body forms a straight line from head to knees. Lower the chest toward the floor, then press back up until the arms are straight. Keep the ribs tucked and avoid bending only at the hips.

ff_suspension_push_up.description: Hold the suspension handles under the shoulders and step back until the straps are taut. Lower the chest between the handles, then press away while keeping the body in one long plank. Keep the straps quiet and avoid letting the elbows flare straight out.

ff_suspension_chest_fly.description: Hold the suspension handles with arms straight in front of the chest and lean forward into a plank. Open the arms out to the sides with soft elbows, then squeeze the handles back together. Keep the hips and ribs stacked, and shorten the angle if the shoulders drift behind you.

ff_stability_ball_double_dumbbell_chest_.description: Lie with the upper back and head supported on a stability ball, feet wide, and hips lifted. Hold two dumbbells above the chest, open the arms in a wide arc, then bring them back over the shoulders. Keep the ball still and avoid dropping the elbows below a comfortable shoulder range.

ff_stability_ball_dumbbell_pullover.description: Rest the upper back on a stability ball with feet planted and one dumbbell held over the chest. With elbows slightly bent, lower the dumbbell behind the head, then pull it back over the chest. Keep the hips lifted and avoid flaring the ribs as the weight moves overhead.

ff_tire_feet_elevated_glute_bridge.description: Lie on your back with both heels on the tire edge and knees bent. Drive through the heels to lift the hips until the torso and thighs line up, then lower under control. Keep the tire contact steady and avoid arching the low back at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_chest_f.description: Lie back on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand above the upper chest. Open the arms in a controlled arc with soft elbows, then bring the dumbbells back together over the shoulders. Keep shoulder blades set on the bench and avoid turning the fly into a press.

ff_suspension_low_row.description: Hold the suspension handles with arms long and walk the feet forward so your body leans back close to the floor. Pull the handles toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are straight. Keep the body rigid and avoid thrusting the hips to finish the row.

ff_tire_flip.description: Stand close to the tire with feet wide, hips back, and hands under the tread. Drive through the legs to lift the tire, step in, then push it over from the chest. Keep the back braced and avoid curling the tire with rounded shoulders.

ff_tire_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Stand straddling the tire opening or marked space with feet outside the target. Jump both feet inside, land in a squat, then jump back out and absorb the landing. Keep knees tracking over toes and avoid landing stiff-legged on the tire edge.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_forward.description: Hold one kettlebell by the horns at chest height. Step forward into a lunge, lower until both knees bend, then push back to standing and alternate legs. Keep the bell close to the sternum and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_feet_elevate.description: Stand with the balls of both feet on a step and a dumbbell hanging at each side. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes and pause briefly. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_calf_raise.description: Stand tall on the floor with a dumbbell at each side in a suitcase hold. Rise onto the balls of the feet, pause, then lower the heels slowly. Keep the ankles tracking straight and avoid rocking the weights for momentum.

ff_suspension_mid_row.description: Hold the suspension handles at chest height and lean back with the body in a straight line. Pull the handles toward the ribs with elbows close to the body, then return to long arms. Keep shoulders away from the ears and avoid bending at the hips.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_calf_raise.description: Sit tall with knees bent and one dumbbell resting securely on each thigh near the knees. Press through the balls of the feet to raise the heels, pause, then lower slowly. Keep the weights balanced and avoid letting the ankles roll outward.

ff_barbell_seated_calf_raise.description: Sit with the balls of the feet planted and a padded barbell resting across the thighs near the knees. Raise the heels as high as you can, pause, then lower until the calves stretch. Keep the bar from rolling and avoid bouncing the knees.

ff_double_clubbell_order_feet_elevated_c.description: Hold two clubbells in order position at the shoulders and stand with the balls of the feet on a step. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes while keeping the clubs parked close. Brace the torso and avoid letting the clubs pull you forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_calf_raise.description: Stand tall with two clubbells held in order position at the shoulders. Rise onto the toes, pause, then lower the heels to the floor with control. Keep the handles stable and avoid leaning back to balance the clubs.

ff_clubbell_order_feet_elevated_calf_rai.description: Hold one clubbell in order position at the shoulder and place the balls of the feet on a step. Lower the heels below the edge, then lift onto the toes without letting the club drift. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid bouncing through the ankle range.

ff_clubbell_order_calf_raise.description: Stand on the floor with a clubbell parked in order position at the shoulder. Raise both heels, pause at the top, then lower slowly. Keep the club close to the body and avoid shifting weight to one foot.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_feet_elevat.description: Hold one clubbell in order position on one shoulder and stand on a step with both forefeet supported. Drop the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes while resisting side bend. Keep the free arm quiet and avoid letting the loaded shoulder collapse.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_calf_raise.description: Stand tall with one clubbell in order position on one shoulder. Lift both heels off the floor, pause, then lower under control. Keep the torso vertical and avoid leaning away from the clubbell.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_feet_elevate.description: Place the balls of both feet on a step and use a wall or rail lightly for balance. Lower the heels below the edge, then press up onto the toes. Keep the assistance light and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_calf_raise.description: Stand near a wall or rail and use the hands only for balance. Rise onto the toes, pause, then lower the heels slowly to the floor. Keep the body tall and avoid pulling yourself up with the arms.

ff_bodyweight_calf_raise.description: Stand tall with feet about hip width and arms relaxed or lightly out for balance. Press through the balls of the feet to lift the heels, pause, then lower slowly. Keep the ankles from rolling outward and avoid bouncing the reps.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_foot_elevate.description: Stand on one forefoot at the edge of a step and hold a wall or rail lightly. Lower the working heel below the step, then rise onto the toe while the free leg stays quiet. Keep the knee mostly straight and avoid using the hand to pull up.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_single_leg_c.description: Stand on one foot near a wall or rail with the free foot off the floor. Lift the heel as high as you can, pause, then lower with control. Keep the hips level and avoid hopping through the rep.

ff_bodyweight_single_leg_calf_raise.description: Balance on one foot with the other foot hovering behind or beside you. Rise onto the ball of the working foot, pause, then lower slowly. Keep the ankle stacked over the toes and avoid twisting the hips for balance.

ff_barbell_front_rack_feet_elevated_calf.description: Hold the barbell in a front rack and stand with the balls of the feet on a step. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes while keeping elbows lifted. Keep the torso upright and avoid letting the bar pull you forward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_reverse.description: Hold a kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the bell tight to the body and avoid pushing off the back toes instead of the front foot.

ff_double_kettlebell_l_sit_flutter_kicks.description: Set two kettlebells on the floor as handles and press down through straight arms. Lift into an L-sit, then make small alternating flutter kicks with the legs. Keep shoulders depressed and lower the feet if the hips drop between the bells.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_feet_eleva.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place the balls of the feet on a step. Drop the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep elbows forward and avoid leaning back under the front-rack load.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_calf_raise.description: Stand tall with two dumbbells held at the shoulders. Raise the heels from the floor, pause, then lower slowly. Keep the torso stacked under the weights and avoid turning the movement into a knee bend.

ff_barbell_back_rack_feet_elevated_calf_.description: Place the barbell across the upper back and stand with the balls of the feet on a step. Lower the heels below the edge, then press up onto the toes. Keep the bar balanced over midfoot and avoid bouncing at the bottom.

ff_tire_mountain_climber.description: Place both hands on the tire edge and step back into a high plank. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep the tire steady under the hands. Brace the trunk and avoid letting the hips pike or sway.

ff_bodyweight_wall_supported_tibialis_ra.description: Stand with your back against a wall and heels a short step away from it. Keep the heels down and lift the toes toward the shins, then lower them slowly. Stay tall against the wall and avoid bending the knees to create the lift.

ff_cable_seated_tibialis_raise.description: Sit facing a low cable with the strap around the forefoot and the heel planted. Pull the toes toward the shin against the cable, then let the foot point forward slowly. Keep the knee still and avoid curling the toes around the strap.

ff_cable_seated_single_leg_tibialis_rais.description: Sit facing a low cable with one foot strapped and the other foot planted for support. Dorsiflex the working foot toward the shin, then lower it under control. Keep the thigh quiet and avoid leaning back to move the cable.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_walking_lunge.description: Hold a kettlebell in a goblet grip at the chest. Step forward into a lunge, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the bell pull the chest down.

ff_dumbbell_seated_tibialis_raise.description: Sit with heels on the floor and a light dumbbell balanced across the forefoot. Lift the toes toward the shins, then lower the dumbbell slowly. Keep the heels planted and avoid swinging the lower legs.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_late.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height. Step to one side, bend that knee while the other leg stays long, then push back to center and alternate. Keep the goblet grip close and avoid twisting the torso toward the stepping leg.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_alternating_later.description: Hold a kettlebell low with both hands in front of the hips. Step laterally, sit into the stepping hip, then drive back to center and switch sides. Keep the working foot flat and avoid letting the bell swing away from the body.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_feet_elevated_.description: Sit with the balls of the feet on a low step and a dumbbell resting on each thigh. Lower the heels below the step, then raise them as high as you can. Keep the dumbbells steady and avoid bouncing through the ankle stretch.

ff_barbell_seated_feet_elevated_calf_rai.description: Sit with the balls of the feet on a low step and a padded barbell across the thighs. Drop the heels below the edge, then lift them by pressing through the forefeet. Keep knees pointed forward and avoid letting the bar roll toward the knees.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_forw.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns against the chest. Step forward into a lunge, push back to standing, and alternate legs. Keep the elbows tucked and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_tire_leg_press.description: Lie on your back with both feet placed firmly against the tire and knees bent. Press the tire away by extending the hips and knees, then guide it back with control. Keep the feet flat on the tread and avoid locking the knees hard at the top.

ff_cable_isometric_split_squat_pallof_pr.description: Set a cable at chest height and stand sideways in a split-squat stance. Hold the split-squat depth while pressing the handle straight out from the sternum, then bring it back. Keep the pelvis square and avoid rotating toward the cable stack.

ff_bodyweight_isometric_split_squat_calf.description: Set a split stance and lower into a steady isometric split squat. Without changing hip height, raise and lower the front heel like a calf raise. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid bobbing the whole body.

ff_bodyweight_isometric_split_squat_foot.description: Place the front forefoot on a small edge and settle into a split-squat hold. Lower the front heel below the edge, then raise it while maintaining the lunge position. Keep the back knee quiet and avoid bouncing out of the calf stretch.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_isometric_sp.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and lower into a split-squat hold. Raise and lower the front heel while the knees stay bent. Keep shoulders level and avoid standing up between calf-raise reps.

Hold dumbbells suitcase style with the front forefoot on a low edge. Stay in the split-squat position as the front heel drops and rises. Keep the weights beside the hips and avoid rocking forward onto the toes.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_reve.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Step one leg back into a reverse lunge, return to tall standing, then alternate. Keep the goblet load close and avoid tipping the chest forward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_walking_lunge.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns in front of the chest. Step forward into each lunge and bring the back leg through into the next rep. Keep the elbows tucked and avoid drifting side to side as you walk.

ff_barbell_back_rack_isometric_split_squ.description: Set a barbell on the upper back and lower into a split-squat hold. Raise and lower the front heel without changing the knee bend. Keep the bar over midfoot and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

Hold a back-rack barbell and place the front forefoot on a low edge in a split stance. Sink into the split squat, lower the front heel, then rise onto the toes. Keep the torso tall and avoid bouncing the bar.

ff_barbell_front_rack_isometric_split_sq.description: Hold the barbell in a front rack and settle into a split-squat hold. Lift and lower the front heel while the hips stay at the same height. Keep elbows high and avoid losing the rack as the calf fatigues.

Front-rack the barbell and place the front forefoot on a low edge. Hold the split squat while the front heel lowers below the edge and rises to the toes. Keep the ribs down and avoid folding under the bar.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_hand_ass.description: Hold a dumbbell at the same side as the working leg and place that forefoot on a step. Use the free hand lightly for balance, lower the heel, then rise onto the toe. Keep the hip stacked over the ankle and avoid pulling with the support hand.

ff_double_kettlebell_straddle_l_sit.description: Set two kettlebells as handles and press down through locked arms. Lift the hips and spread the legs into a straddle L-sit, holding the feet off the floor. Keep shoulders pushed down and avoid letting the bells tip inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_isometric_.description: Hold two dumbbells in the front rack and lower into a split-squat hold. Raise and lower the front heel while maintaining the lunge depth. Keep elbows lifted and avoid rising out of the isometric position.

Front-rack two dumbbells with the front forefoot on a low edge in a split stance. Hold the split squat as the front heel drops and rises. Keep the weights close to the shoulders and avoid shifting onto the back leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_low_.description: Hold one kettlebell in the front rack and stand in a wide stance. Sink into a Cossack squat, stay low as you shift through center to the other side, then continue under control. Keep the racked elbow tight and avoid letting either heel peel up.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_foot.description: Hold the kettlebell in the front rack opposite the working leg and place the long-leg foot on a small elevation. Sit into the working hip for a Cossack squat, then drive back to the start. Keep the load close and avoid rotating toward the elevated foot.

ff_bodyweight_squat_jump.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and arms ready to counterbalance. Squat, jump straight up, and land softly back into the next squat. Keep knees aligned with the toes and avoid collapsing into the landing.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_alternating_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and place one full foot on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down with control, then switch legs. Keep shoulders level and avoid pushing off the floor foot.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and plant one foot fully on the box. Step up by driving through the elevated foot, then lower under control before repeating. Keep elbows up and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_cable_v_grip_seated_low_row.description: Sit at the low cable with feet braced and hold the V grip with arms long. Pull the handle toward the lower ribs, pause, then return until the shoulders reach forward without rounding. Keep the torso tall and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_resistance_band_half_kneeling_face_pu.description: Anchor a resistance band around face height and set a half-kneeling stance facing it. Pull the band toward the nose with elbows high and thumbs moving back, then reach forward. Keep the ribs down and avoid arching through the low back.

ff_bodyweight_prone_cobra.description: Lie face down with legs long and arms angled by the sides. Lift the chest slightly while turning the thumbs up and drawing shoulder blades down and back. Keep the neck long and avoid cranking the low back for height.

ff_barbell_hang_power_snatch.description: Hold the bar in a snatch grip and start from a hang above the knees. Extend the hips hard, pull under, and catch the bar overhead in a partial squat. Keep the bar close to the body and avoid looping it away from you.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_snatch.description: Start from a hang position with a wide snatch grip and the bar close to the thighs. Drive through the hips, pull under, and receive the bar overhead in a full squat before standing. Keep the chest up in the catch and avoid pressing the bar out in front.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_reverse_fly.description: Sit tall or hinge from a seated position with a dumbbell in each hand under the shoulders. Raise the arms out and back with soft elbows, then lower slowly. Keep the neck relaxed and avoid swinging the torso.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_seated_concentrat.description: Sit with feet wide and brace the working upper arm against the inside thigh. Curl the dumbbell toward the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is nearly straight. Keep the shoulder still and avoid twisting the torso to finish.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_bi.description: Sit tall with a dumbbell in each hand and palms facing forward or slightly in. Curl one dumbbell while the other hangs still, lower it, then switch sides. Keep the elbows pinned near the ribs and avoid leaning back.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_front_rai.description: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand in front of the thighs. Raise one arm to shoulder height, lower it under control, then raise the other arm. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the hips to start the lift.

ff_cable_wide_grip_lat_pulldown.description: Sit at the cable pulldown station with a wide overhand grip on the bar. Pull the bar toward the upper chest while driving elbows down, then let the arms reach overhead. Keep the torso mostly upright and avoid yanking the bar behind the neck.

ff_cable_reverse_grip_lat_pulldown.description: Sit at the cable pulldown with an underhand shoulder-width grip. Pull the bar toward the upper chest with elbows tracking down and forward, then return to long arms. Keep the chest lifted and avoid turning it into a backward lean.

ff_cable_v_grip_lat_pulldown.description: Attach a V grip to the high cable and sit with knees secured. Pull the handle toward the upper chest by driving elbows down, then let the shoulders reach up under control. Keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging at the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_bicycle_crunch.description: Lie on your back with hands lightly behind the head and legs lifted. Rotate one shoulder toward the opposite knee as the other leg extends, then switch sides in a pedaling rhythm. Keep the low back controlled and avoid pulling on the neck.

ff_bodyweight_broad_jump.description: Stand with feet about hip width and arms ready to swing. Hinge and load the hips, jump forward, and land on both feet with knees bent. Stick the landing quietly and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_bodyweight_forearm_plank.description: Set the forearms on the floor with elbows under shoulders and legs extended. Brace the glutes and ribs so the body forms one straight line. Breathe steadily and avoid letting the hips sag or hike.

ff_parallette_plank.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders and step back into a straight-arm plank. Press the handles down and hold a long line from head to heels. Keep wrists stacked and avoid sinking between the shoulders.

ff_double_clubbell_clean_to_shoulder_par.description: Start with two clubbells hanging in front of the thighs. Clean them to the shoulder-park position, set the feet, then squat and stand while keeping the clubs close. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the club heads pull the elbows open.

ff_clubbell_shoulder_park_squat.description: Park the clubbell across one shoulder with both hands controlling the handle. Squat between the heels, then stand while keeping the club secure against the shoulder. Brace against the offset load and avoid twisting toward the club.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_shoulder_park_squ.description: Hold one clubbell in a single-arm shoulder-park position with the handle controlled near the chest. Squat to a depth you can own, then drive through both feet to stand. Keep the loaded shoulder packed and avoid leaning away from the club.

ff_clubbell_swing_to_alternating_shoulde.description: Swing the clubbell with a hip hinge, then guide it into a shoulder park on one side. Squat, stand, and switch the shoulder-park side on the next rep. Keep the swing close and avoid catching the club with a loose torso.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bench_press.description: Lie on a flat bench with one dumbbell over the shoulder and the other hand free or braced. Lower the dumbbell to the side of the chest, then press it back up. Keep hips and shoulders square to the bench and avoid rolling toward the loaded side.

ff_double_dumbbell_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand over the chest. Lower both dumbbells beside the lower chest, then press them back over the shoulders. Keep wrists stacked and avoid bouncing the elbows off the bench line.

ff_barbell_split_jerk.description: Hold the bar in the front rack with feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive the bar up, and split the feet to catch with locked arms overhead. Recover front foot then back foot, and avoid pressing the bar after the legs stop driving.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_split_jerk.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the front rack, reset the feet, then dip and split jerk it overhead. Keep the clean close and recover the jerk before lowering.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_press.description: Lie on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand at the upper chest. Press both weights up over the shoulders, then lower with elbows slightly below shoulder height. Keep shoulder blades on the bench and avoid letting the dumbbells drift toward the face.

ff_barbell_power_jerk.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand tall. Dip and drive, then re-bend both knees to catch the bar overhead with feet about squat width. Lock the ribs down and avoid catching with the bar in front of the shoulders.

ff_barbell_squat_jerk.description: Front-rack the bar and set the feet for a vertical dip. Drive the bar overhead, pull under into a full squat, then stand with the bar locked out. Keep the chest up in the catch and avoid chasing the bar forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bent_over_row.description: Hold a kettlebell in each hand and hinge until the torso is angled forward. Row both bells toward the hips, pause, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the spine long and avoid standing up as you pull.

ff_bodyweight_seated_wall_angels.description: Sit with your back, head, and hips against a wall and knees bent comfortably. Slide the arms from a goalpost toward an overhead Y while keeping contact with the wall as much as possible. Keep ribs down and avoid forcing the low back to arch.

ff_parallette_kneeling_close_grip_push_u.description: Grip the parallettes close together and set a kneeling plank. Lower the chest between the handles with elbows tracking near the ribs, then press back up. Keep the shoulders away from the ears and avoid shortening the rep by dropping the head.

ff_double_kettlebell_shrug.description: Stand tall with a kettlebell in each hand at the sides. Lift the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower under control. Keep the elbows straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_split_squat_ju.description: Start in a split stance with knees bent and torso tall. Jump up, switch legs in the air, and land softly in the opposite split stance. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid crashing into the landing.

ff_suspension_y_raise.description: Hold the suspension handles with arms long and lean back slightly. Raise the arms overhead into a Y shape while the body stays plank-straight, then lower slowly. Keep shoulders down and avoid arching the ribs to gain height.
