ff_bodyweight_standing_wall_angels.description: Stand with back, head, and hips near a wall and feet slightly forward. Slide the arms from goalpost to overhead while keeping ribs down. Move only through the range you can control and avoid shrugging the shoulders.

ff_double_kettlebell_mountain_climber.description: Place two kettlebells on the floor as handles and step into a high plank. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep the bells steady. Press through the handles and avoid letting the hips bounce.

ff_bodyweight_split_squat_jump.description: Begin in a split stance with the front foot flat and back heel lifted. Dip into the lunge, jump straight up, and land back in the same split stance. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee fold inward.

ff_bodyweight_duck_walk.description: Squat low with feet about shoulder width and chest lifted. Take small steps forward while staying low and keeping the heels as grounded as your mobility allows. Keep knees tracking with toes and avoid collapsing the chest onto the thighs.

ff_barbell_front_rack_split_squat.description: Hold the barbell in a front rack and set a long split stance. Lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep elbows high and avoid letting the bar pull the torso forward.

ff_barbell_overhead_split_squat.description: Lock the barbell overhead with hands wide enough to keep the shoulders stacked. Set a split stance, lower under control, then stand through the front foot. Keep the bar over the midfoot and avoid leaning forward under the load.

ff_double_dumbbell_split_jerk.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders with feet under the hips. Dip, drive both dumbbells overhead, and split the feet to catch with arms locked. Recover with control and avoid pressing early before the legs finish driving.

ff_suspension_reverse_fly.description: Hold the suspension handles with arms straight and lean back until the straps are taut. Open the arms out to the sides to pull the chest toward the anchor, then return slowly. Keep elbows softly bent and avoid shrugging into the neck.

ff_double_dumbbell_decline_bench_press.description: Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in each hand over the lower chest. Lower the weights beside the ribs, then press them back up over the chest. Keep the shoulder blades pinned and avoid letting the dumbbells drift behind the elbows.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_split_jerk.description: Hold one dumbbell at the shoulder with feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive the dumbbell overhead, and split the feet to catch with the arm locked. Keep the torso vertical and avoid leaning away from the dumbbell.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_snat.description: Start with one kettlebell in a hike position and grip the handle firmly. Hinge, snap the hips, guide the bell close, and punch into a bottoms-up overhead catch. Keep the wrist vertical and use a light load so the bell does not tip or pull forward.

ff_bodyweight_copenhagen_plank_side_rais.description: Place the top foot or knee on a bench and support the body on the lower forearm in a side plank. Lift the hips, then raise and lower the lower leg toward the bench. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_bodyweight_copenhagen_plank_with_leg_.description: Set the top leg on a bench and hold a side plank on the lower forearm. Lift the lower leg toward the bench, pause, then lower without dropping the hips. Keep the pelvis stacked and avoid twisting to chase height.

ff_double_dumbbell_chest_fly.description: Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand over the chest. Open the arms in a wide arc with soft elbows, then squeeze the dumbbells back over the shoulders. Keep the shoulder blades anchored and avoid letting the weights fall too deep.

ff_double_dumbbell_decline_bench_chest_f.description: Lie on a decline bench with two dumbbells above the lower chest. Open the arms out and slightly down with soft elbows, then bring them together over the chest. Keep the movement smooth and avoid turning the fly into a press.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_calf_raise.description: Stand tall with a kettlebell hanging at each side. Rise onto the toes, pause, and lower the heels slowly to the floor. Keep the bells still and avoid rocking forward to create momentum.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_feet_ele.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and stand with the balls of the feet on a step. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes. Keep forearms vertical and avoid letting the rack collapse onto the chest.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_bent_over_row.description: Hinge at the hips and squeeze one dumbbell between both hands under the chest. Row the dumbbell toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the squeeze constant and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_calf_rai.description: Stand with two kettlebells in the front rack and feet about hip width. Raise both heels, pause, then lower slowly. Keep the bells tucked close and avoid leaning back to balance them.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_bicep_curl_to_ove.description: Stand tall and squeeze one dumbbell vertically between both hands at the thighs. Curl it to the chest, then press it overhead before lowering in the same path. Keep elbows close during the curl and avoid leaning back on the press.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_overhead_press.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically between both palms at chest height. Press it overhead until the arms are straight, then lower to the chest with control. Keep the dumbbell squeezed and avoid flaring the ribs.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor and squeeze one dumbbell vertically between both hands over the chest. Lower until the upper arms touch the floor, then press back up. Keep wrists stacked and avoid letting the dumbbell wobble toward the face.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_overhead_tricep_e.description: Stand or sit tall holding one dumbbell squeezed between both hands overhead. Bend the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep elbows pointed forward and avoid arching the low back.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_isometric_.description: Hold kettlebells at the sides and lower into a split-squat hold. Raise and lower the front heel while maintaining the same knee bend. Keep the bells beside the hips and avoid rising out of the lunge.

ff_bodyweight_single_leg_hip_thrust.description: Set the upper back on a bench with one foot planted and the other leg lifted. Drive through the planted heel to raise the hips, pause, then lower slowly. Keep the pelvis level and avoid rotating toward the working side.

ff_stability_ball_single_leg_hip_thrust.description: Rest the upper back on a stability ball with one foot planted and the other leg lifted. Press through the planted heel to raise the hips while the ball stays under the shoulders. Keep the ribs tucked and avoid letting the ball roll side to side.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_isometric_.description: Hold kettlebells in a suitcase grip with the front forefoot on a low edge. Stay in the split squat as the front heel lowers and rises. Keep shoulders level and avoid bouncing the back knee.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_hand_a.description: Hold one kettlebell on the same side as the working leg and place that forefoot on a step. Use the free hand lightly for balance, lower the heel, then rise onto the toe. Keep the kettlebell quiet and avoid leaning into the support hand.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_isometri.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and sink into a split-squat hold. Raise and lower the front heel without changing the hip height. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the bells pull the chest down.

ff_cable_v_grip_seated_floor_lat_pulldow.description: Sit on the floor under a high cable with legs forward and a V grip overhead. Pull the handle toward the upper chest, then reach back up until the arms are long. Sit tall and avoid leaning far backward to finish the pull.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_isometri.description: Front-rack two kettlebells and place the front forefoot on a low edge. Hold the split-squat depth while the front heel drops and rises. Keep the rack tight and avoid shifting weight off the front leg.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_wave.description: Hold one battle-rope end in each hand with knees bent and hips back slightly. Move the arms in opposite rhythms to send alternating waves to the anchor. Keep the torso braced and avoid standing upright as the arms fatigue.

ff_cable_rope_tall_kneeling_tricep_pushd.description: Kneel tall facing a high cable with a rope attachment in both hands. Pin the elbows by the ribs, press the rope down until the arms straighten, then return under control. Keep glutes squeezed and avoid leaning over the rope.

ff_bodyweight_feet_elevated_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on a step and no external load. Lower both heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes and pause. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_stability_ball_reverse_hyperextension.description: Lie face down over a stability ball with hands braced on the floor and legs extended behind you. Lift the legs until they line up with the torso, then lower slowly. Squeeze the glutes and avoid rolling forward over the ball.

ff_bodyweight_frog_reverse_hyperextensio.description: Lie face down with hips supported on a bench or ball and knees bent out to the sides with soles near each other. Lift the thighs by squeezing the glutes, then lower under control. Keep the pelvis heavy and avoid kicking from the low back.

ff_bodyweight_reverse_hyperextension.description: Lie face down with hips at the edge of a bench or stable surface and legs hanging. Lift the legs behind you until they line up with the torso, then lower slowly. Keep the movement driven by the glutes and avoid swinging for height.

ff_cable_rope_tall_kneeling_low_to_high_.description: Attach a rope to a low cable and kneel tall sideways to the stack. Pull the rope diagonally from the low hip across the body to the opposite shoulder, then return slowly. Keep the hips square and avoid twisting through the low back.

ff_bodyweight_v_up.description: Lie on your back with arms overhead and legs long. Lift the arms and legs together toward a V shape, then lower with control. Exhale as you rise and avoid throwing the arms to create momentum.

ff_slider_barbell_front_rack_reverse_lun.description: Hold the barbell in a front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot straight back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep elbows high and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_slider_barbell_back_rack_reverse_lung.description: Place the barbell across the upper back and set one foot on a slider. Slide the foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the bar level and avoid twisting as the slider moves.

ff_slider_reverse_lunge.description: Stand tall with one foot on a slider and the other foot flat. Slide the rear foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward under the hips. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid letting the slider drift outward.

ff_barbell_push_jerk.description: Hold the bar in the front rack with feet under the hips. Dip straight down, drive the bar overhead, and re-bend the knees to catch with locked arms. Stand fully before lowering and avoid pressing the bar out after the catch.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and clean it to the front rack without dropping into a full squat. Reset the feet, dip, drive, and catch the bar overhead in a push jerk. Keep the bar close through the clean and avoid a forward catch overhead.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Pull the bar from the floor, receive it in a front squat, and stand tall. Dip and drive into a push jerk, catching overhead with both knees re-bent. Keep the elbows high out of the clean and avoid rushing the jerk before your balance is set.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_swing.description: Hold one kettlebell and hike it outside the same-side leg rather than between both legs. Snap the hips to swing the bell forward and slightly across the body, then guide it back outside the leg. Keep the arm relaxed and avoid twisting the knees with the swing.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side and step forward into a lunge. At the bottom or as you return, pass the bell under the front thigh to the other hand, then stand and alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid rounding down to reach the pass.

Hold one kettlebell at the side and step the opposite foot back into a reverse lunge. Pass the bell under the front thigh to the other hand, then return to standing and alternate. Keep the front foot grounded and avoid letting the pass pull the knee inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_contralateral_f.description: Hold one kettlebell on the side opposite the leg that will step forward. Step into the lunge as you drive the bell upward into a snatch, then stand with the bell overhead. Keep the bell close and avoid leaning away from the contralateral load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_alternating_for.description: Start with one kettlebell in one hand and feet under the hips. Step forward into a lunge while snatching the bell overhead, return to standing, then switch sides on the next rep. Keep the overhead arm stacked and avoid casting the bell away from the shoulder.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_press.description: Clean two kettlebells to the rack with forearms vertical. Press both bells overhead until the arms are straight, then lower back to the rack. Keep glutes tight and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_cable_straight_bar_bicep_curl.description: Stand facing a low cable with a straight bar held in both hands. Curl the bar toward the shoulders, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep upper arms close to the ribs and avoid rocking backward.

ff_double_kettlebell_glute_bridge_isomet.description: Lie on your back with knees bent and two kettlebells held over the chest. Lift into a glute bridge and hold the hips up while pressing the bells from the floor. Keep hips level and avoid flaring the ribs as the bells press.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack.description: Hold one dumbbell in the front rack on the same side as the foot placed on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid collapsing toward the dumbbell.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_c.description: Hold one dumbbell at the side opposite the foot on the slider. Slide the working foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep shoulders level and avoid letting the dumbbell pull you across the body.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_i.description: Hold one dumbbell at the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge and pull it forward under control. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid leaning toward the loaded side.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_c.description: Press one dumbbell overhead on the side opposite the foot placed on the slider. Slide the foot back into a reverse lunge, then stand while the arm stays locked out. Keep the weight over the shoulder and avoid arching the low back.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_i.description: Hold one dumbbell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back, lower into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the dumbbell drift forward.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_front_rack_rev.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot on a slider. Slide back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep elbows lifted and avoid losing balance as the slider returns.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_suitcase_rever.description: Hold a dumbbell in each hand at the sides and set one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward under the hips. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid swinging the dumbbells.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_overhead_rever.description: Press two dumbbells overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide the foot back into a reverse lunge, then stand while keeping both arms stacked. Use a short slide if needed and avoid flaring the ribs.

ff_double_dumbbell_hammer_curl.description: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand and palms facing each other. Curl both weights toward the shoulders, then lower until the elbows straighten. Keep elbows near the ribs and avoid swinging the dumbbells forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_bench_pre.description: Lie on a flat bench with two dumbbells over the chest. Lower and press one dumbbell while the other stays locked out or parked over the chest, then switch sides. Keep the nonworking side steady and avoid rolling the torso.

ff_slider_single_arm_clubbell_order_cont.description: Hold one clubbell in order position opposite the foot on the slider. Slide the working foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the club parked close and avoid rotating toward the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsi.description: Hold one clubbell in order position on the same side as the foot placed on the slider. Slide back into a reverse lunge and pull forward to stand. Keep the loaded shoulder packed and avoid side-bending toward the club.

ff_slider_double_clubbell_order_reverse_.description: Hold two clubbells in order position and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward under the hips. Keep both clubs tight to the shoulders and avoid letting the elbows flare.

ff_slider_clubbell_order_reverse_lunge.description: Park a clubbell in order position and set one foot on a slider. Slide back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing while keeping the club steady. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid twisting under the offset load.

ff_slider_macebell_order_reverse_lunge.description: Hold the macebell in order position against the shoulder and place one foot on a slider. Slide the foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the long handle controlled and avoid leaning away from the mace head.

ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_cont.description: Hold one macebell in order position opposite the foot placed on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the mace close to the shoulder and avoid rotating toward the front leg.

ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsi.description: Hold one macebell in order position on the same side as the foot placed on the slider. Slide back into a reverse lunge and pull the foot forward under control. Keep ribs stacked and avoid letting the mace tip pull you sideways.

ff_slider_landmine_goblet_reverse_lunge.description: Hold the landmine sleeve in both hands at chest height and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then stand by driving through the front foot. Keep the sleeve close and avoid leaning into the bar path.

ff_slider_landmine_shoulder_contralatera.description: Rack the landmine sleeve on the shoulder opposite the foot placed on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the shoulder under the sleeve and avoid rotating toward the anchor.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_front_rack.description: Hold the landmine sleeve in a front-rack position opposite the foot placed on the slider. Slide that foot back, lower into a reverse lunge, then drive forward to stand. Keep the elbow under the sleeve and avoid letting the bar pull you across midline.

Hold the landmine sleeve in a front rack on the same side as the foot placed on the slider. Slide back into the reverse lunge, then return to standing with the sleeve close. Keep the torso tall and avoid collapsing toward the anchor.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_suitcase_c.description: Hold the landmine sleeve low at the side opposite the foot placed on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep shoulders level and avoid letting the bar swing around the body.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_suitcase_i.description: Hold the landmine sleeve low on the same side as the foot placed on the slider. Slide the foot back, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the sleeve close to the hip and avoid tipping toward the anchor.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_overhead_c.description: Press the landmine sleeve overhead on the side opposite the foot placed on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then stand while keeping the arm stacked. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the sleeve drift behind you.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_overhead_i.description: Press the landmine sleeve overhead on the same side as the foot placed on the slider. Slide back into a reverse lunge and return to standing under control. Keep the bar path steady and avoid leaning away from the anchor.

ff_suspension_squat_jump.description: Hold the suspension handles lightly with elbows bent and feet about shoulder width. Squat, jump vertically with help only as needed, and land softly back into the squat. Keep the straps from yanking you forward and avoid collapsing the knees inward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie on an incline bench with two dumbbells at the upper chest. Press one dumbbell while the other stays steady, lower it, then alternate sides. Keep shoulder blades set and avoid twisting the torso toward the pressing arm.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_decline_b.description: Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Press one dumbbell over the lower chest while the other waits under control, then alternate. Keep both shoulders anchored and avoid letting the resting dumbbell sink too low.

ff_miniband_glute_bridge.description: Place a miniband around the thighs just above the knees and lie on your back with feet planted. Push the knees gently into the band as you lift the hips, pause, then lower. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_top_down_.description: Lie on a flat bench with two dumbbells held near the chest instead of locked out. Press one dumbbell up and lower it back to the chest before switching sides. Keep the nonworking dumbbell controlled and avoid bouncing it off the shoulder line.

Lie on an incline bench with both dumbbells parked at the upper chest. Press one side to lockout, lower it back down, then press the other side. Keep the chest lifted and avoid shrugging the shoulders at the bottom.

Set up on a decline bench with both dumbbells held at the lower chest. Press one dumbbell up, return it to the start, then alternate arms. Keep the elbows under the wrists and avoid letting the weights drift behind the shoulders.

ff_bar_dead_hang.description: Grip a pull-up bar with arms straight and feet off the floor or lightly assisted. Let the body hang long while keeping the shoulders gently active. Breathe steadily and avoid swinging or shrugging hard into the ears.

ff_trap_bar_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar with feet about hip width and grip the handles at your sides. Brace, push the floor away, and stand tall with the handles traveling beside the legs. Keep the chest up and avoid letting the hips shoot up before the bar moves.

ff_trap_bar_staggered_stance_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar with one foot slightly behind the other on the toes. Hinge and bend the front knee to grip the handles, then stand by driving through the front foot. Keep hips square and avoid turning the staggered stance into a twist.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_bicep_cur.description: Stand on the middle of a resistance band and hold one end in each hand. Curl one hand toward the shoulder, lower it, then alternate sides. Keep elbows close to the ribs and avoid leaning back as band tension increases.

ff_landmine_goblet_alternating_curtsy_lu.description: Hold the landmine sleeve in both hands at chest height. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, return to standing, then alternate sides. Keep the sleeve close and avoid letting the front knee dive inward.

ff_landmine_goblet_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the landmine sleeve in a goblet position at the chest. Step one leg back and across the body, lower under control, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep hips mostly square and avoid twisting toward the anchor.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_curtsy_l.description: Lock a barbell overhead with hands wide enough to stack the shoulders. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, return to standing, then alternate. Keep the bar over midfoot and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_curtsy_.description: Place the bar across the upper back and stand tall. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, push through the front foot to return, then alternate. Keep the bar level and avoid rotating the hips excessively.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_curtsy_lu.description: Cradle the barbell in the elbows in a Zercher hold. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep elbows tight to the torso and avoid rounding forward around the bar.

ff_trap_bar_suitcase_carry.description: Stand beside a trap bar and grip the center of one handle with one hand like a suitcase. Walk with slow, even steps while keeping shoulders level. Brace against the offset load and avoid leaning away from the bar.

ff_trap_bar_romanian_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar and hold the handles at your sides. Soften the knees, push the hips back, and lower until the hamstrings load before standing tall. Keep the handles close to the legs and avoid rounding the back for extra depth.

ff_resistance_band_bicep_curl.description: Stand on the center of a resistance band with one handle or end in each hand. Curl both hands toward the shoulders, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms still and avoid snapping the band down.

ff_resistance_band_chest_press.description: Anchor a resistance band behind chest height and hold an end in each hand. Step forward into tension, press the hands straight ahead, then return until elbows pass the ribs. Keep ribs stacked and avoid arching to finish the press.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_chest_pre.description: Anchor the resistance band behind you at chest height and hold both ends. Press one hand forward while the other stays near the ribs, return, then alternate. Keep hips square and avoid rotating the torso with each press.

ff_trap_bar_deficit_deadlift.description: Stand on a low, stable deficit platform inside the trap bar and grip the handles. Brace, push the floor away, and stand tall while the handles stay beside the legs. Use a deficit you can reach with a flat back and avoid yanking from the bottom.

ff_trap_bar_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Stand inside the trap bar, hold the side handles, and place the rear foot on a bench behind you. Bend the front knee and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the trap bar level and avoid tipping the chest forward as the rear knee drops.

ff_trap_bar_squat_jump.description: Stand inside the trap bar with the handles at your sides and feet about shoulder width. Dip into a short squat, jump by driving through the floor, and land softly before resetting. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward on takeoff or landing.

ff_resistance_band_chest_fly.description: Anchor the resistance band behind chest height and stand forward until the arms are lightly tensioned. Sweep the hands together in a wide arc in front of the chest, then reopen without letting the band yank the shoulders back. Keep elbows softly bent and avoid turning the fly into a press.

ff_battle_rope_power_slam_to_burpee.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand in an athletic stance with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, raise both rope ends high and slam them down forcefully. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_resistance_band_low_row.description: Attach the resistance band low near shin height and hold both ends with arms long. Row both elbows back toward the ribs, then return until the arms are long without losing tension. Keep the chest tall and avoid leaning back to finish the pull.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_reverse_lunge.description: Anchor the battle ropes and step back into alternating reverse lunges with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, create alternating waves by driving one arm up as the other arm comes down. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_bodyweight_isometric_split_squat_hold.description: Set a split stance with the front foot flat and the back heel lifted. Lower until both knees are bent and hold that position without bouncing. Keep the torso tall and the front knee tracking over the middle toes.

ff_bodyweight_split_squat.description: Set a split stance with feet far enough apart to lower straight down. Bend both knees, lightly tap or approach the back knee to the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front heel planted and avoid pushing the knee inward.

ff_barbell_frog_pump.description: Lie on your back with soles of the feet together, knees open, and a padded barbell across the hips. Brace the ribs down, squeeze the glutes, and lift the hips until the body forms a short bridge. Lower with control and avoid arching the low back to chase extra height.

ff_barbell_front_rack_calf_raise.description: Hold the barbell in the front rack with elbows lifted. Rise onto the balls of the feet, pause, then lower the heels with control. Keep the ankles tracking straight and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_barbell_back_rack_calf_raise.description: Set the barbell across the upper back. Rise onto the balls of the feet, pause, then lower the heels with control. Keep the ankles tracking straight and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_trap_bar_calf_raise.description: Stand inside the trap bar with handles at your sides. Rise onto the balls of the feet, pause, then lower the heels with control. Keep the ankles tracking straight and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_barbell_zercher_cyclist_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated in a narrow stance and hold the barbell in the crooks of the elbows with forearms angled up. Sit the knees forward and down while keeping the torso upright, then stand by pushing through the whole forefoot and heel. Keep the knees tracking with the toes and avoid rocking off the heel wedge.

ff_trap_bar_rack_pull.description: Set the trap bar on blocks or rack pins around knee height and stand inside the frame. Brace, push the floor away, and extend the hips until standing tall with the handles at your sides. Keep the spine long and avoid jerking the shoulders back at lockout.

ff_resistance_band_mid_row.description: Attach the resistance band around lower-rib height and hold both ends with arms long. Row both elbows back toward the ribs, then return until the arms are long without losing tension. Keep the chest tall and avoid leaning back to finish the pull.

ff_resistance_band_face_pull.description: Stand facing a resistance band anchored around face height with arms extended. Pull the ends toward the sides of the face with elbows high, then reach forward under control. Keep the neck long and avoid leaning back to create the pull.

ff_resistance_band_face_pull_to_overhead.description: Anchor the resistance band around face height and hold the ends with palms down. Pull toward the face with elbows high, rotate the hands up, then press overhead before reversing the path. Use a light band and avoid flaring the ribs or shrugging through the press.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_low_row.description: Attach the resistance band low near shin height and hold one end with the working hand. Row the elbow back toward the ribs, then return until the arm is long without losing tension. Keep the chest tall and avoid leaning back to finish the pull.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_mid_row.description: Attach the resistance band around lower-rib height and hold one end with the working hand. Row the elbow back toward the ribs, then return until the arm is long without losing tension. Keep the chest tall and avoid leaning back to finish the pull.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_chest_pres.description: Anchor the resistance band behind chest height and stand in a split stance with the handle in one hand. Press the hand forward from the ribs until the elbow is straight, then return with control. Keep the hips square and avoid rotating the torso as the band pulls backward.

ff_resistance_band_reverse_grip_bicep_cu.description: Stand on the middle of the resistance band and hold the ends with a palms-down reverse grip. Curl the hands toward the shoulders while the upper arms stay close to the ribs. Lower until the elbows straighten, keeping the wrists firm and avoiding a backward lean.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_reverse_g.description: Stand on the middle of the resistance band and hold the ends with a palms-down reverse grip. Curl one hand at a time toward the shoulder while the other arm stays long. Lower until the elbows straighten, keeping the wrists firm and avoiding a backward lean.

ff_resistance_band_hammer_curl.description: Stand on the middle of the resistance band and hold the ends with a neutral hammer grip. Curl the hands toward the shoulders while the upper arms stay close to the ribs. Lower until the elbows straighten, keeping the wrists firm and avoiding a backward lean.

ff_battle_rope_jumping_jack.description: Anchor the battle ropes and jump the feet out and in like a jumping jack with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, create alternating waves by driving one arm up as the other arm comes down. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_battle_rope_squat_jump_power_slam.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with feet about shoulder width, one rope end in each hand. Dip into a squat jump, drive up, then raise both rope ends and slam them down as you land softly. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward on the landing.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_side_slam.description: Stand facing the battle-rope anchor with knees soft and one rope end in each hand. Rotate both rope ends to one hip, slam them beside that foot, then alternate to the other side. Keep the ribs braced and avoid twisting only through the low back.

ff_battle_rope_squat_jump_alternating_wa.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with feet about shoulder width, one rope end in each hand. Perform a squat jump while keeping alternating waves moving through the ropes. Land quietly, reset the hips back, and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_battle_rope_in_and_out_squat_jump_pow.description: Anchor the battle ropes and jump between narrow and wide squat landings with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, raise both rope ends high and slam them down forcefully. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_battle_rope_in_and_out_squat_jump_alt.description: Anchor the battle ropes and jump between narrow and wide squat landings with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, create alternating waves by driving one arm up as the other arm comes down. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_bodyweight_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Start in a narrow squat stance with arms ready for balance. Jump the feet out to a wider squat, land softly, then jump them back in while keeping rhythm. Keep the chest lifted and avoid letting the band pull the knees inward.

ff_miniband_thigh_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Place a miniband around the thighs just above the knees and Start in a narrow squat stance with arms ready for balance. Jump the feet out to a wider squat, land softly, then jump them back in while keeping rhythm. Keep the chest lifted and avoid letting the band pull the knees inward.

ff_miniband_ankle_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Place a miniband around the ankles and Start in a narrow squat stance with arms ready for balance. Jump the feet out to a wider squat, land softly, then jump them back in while keeping rhythm. Keep the chest lifted and avoid letting the band pull the knees inward.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_hammer_cu.description: Stand on the middle of the resistance band and hold the ends with a neutral hammer grip. Curl one hand at a time toward the shoulder while the other arm stays long. Lower until the elbows straighten, keeping the wrists firm and avoiding a backward lean.

ff_resistance_band_y_raise.description: Stand on a light resistance band or anchor it low in front of you with arms angled down. Raise the hands diagonally into a Y until they are just above shoulder height, then lower slowly. Keep thumbs slightly up and avoid shrugging toward the ears.

ff_battle_rope_isometric_split_squat_pow.description: Anchor the battle ropes and hold the bottom half of a split squat with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, raise both rope ends high and slam them down forcefully. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_split_squat_j.description: Anchor the battle ropes and start in a split stance with one rope end in each hand. Keep alternating waves moving as you jump and switch the lead leg each rep. Land in control with the torso tall and the front knee tracking over the middle toes.

Anchor the battle ropes and start in a split stance with one rope end in each hand. Jump and switch legs while raising both rope ends, then slam the ropes down as you land in the opposite split stance. Land softly with the front knee over the toes instead of letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and rack one kettlebell at the shoulder. Keep the load on the side opposite the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_resistance_band_cuban_press.description: Stand facing a light resistance band anchored in front of you with elbows lifted near shoulder height. Rotate the forearms up, press overhead, then reverse under control. Keep the band light enough that the ribs stay down and the wrists do not fold back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and lock one kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked. Keep the load on the side opposite the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_resistance_band_tall_kneeling_cuban_p.description: Kneel tall facing a light resistance band anchored in front of you with elbows lifted near shoulder height. Rotate the forearms up, press overhead, then reverse under control. Keep the band light enough that the ribs stay down and the wrists do not fold back.

ff_superband_pull_apart.description: Hold a superb band at chest height with arms straight and hands wider than the shoulders. Pull the band apart until the hands move toward the sides of the ribs, then return slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid shrugging as the band stretches.

ff_resistance_band_tall_kneeling_face_pu.description: Kneel tall facing a resistance band anchored around face height with arms extended. Pull the ends toward the sides of the face with elbows high, then reach forward under control. Keep the neck long and avoid leaning back to create the pull.

ff_resistance_band_overhead_press.description: Stand on the middle of the resistance band with the handles at shoulder height. Press both hands overhead until the arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Squeeze the glutes and avoid arching the low back as band tension increases.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_overhead_.description: Stand on the middle of the resistance band with the handles at shoulder height. Press one hand overhead at a time while the other stays racked, then lower to the shoulders. Squeeze the glutes and avoid arching the low back as band tension increases.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_overhead_.description: Stand tall with feet about hip width and hold two dumbbells at shoulder height. Press one dumbbell overhead at a time until the arm is straight, then lower to the shoulder. Keep ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_suspension_skull_crusher.description: Hold the suspension handles and lean forward in a straight-body plank with arms extended. Bend the elbows so the hands travel toward the forehead, then extend the arms to push yourself back. Keep the upper arms fixed and avoid sagging through the hips.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_front_rack_lat.description: Place one foot on a slider and hold two dumbbells at the shoulders. Slide that foot sideways as the standing knee bends into a lateral lunge, then pull the slider back under you. Keep the standing heel down and avoid letting the dumbbells pull the torso forward.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_suitcase_later.description: Place one foot on a slider and hold one dumbbell at each side like suitcases. Slide that foot sideways as the standing knee bends into a lateral lunge, then pull the slider back under you. Keep the standing heel down and avoid letting the dumbbells pull the torso forward.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_overhead_later.description: Place one foot on a slider and hold two dumbbells locked out overhead. Slide that foot sideways as the standing knee bends into a lateral lunge, then pull the slider back under you. Keep the standing heel down and avoid letting the dumbbells pull the torso forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_skull_crusher.description: Lie on a flat bench or the floor and hold one dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the weight toward the sides of the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep upper arms angled steady and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_skull_c.description: Lie on an incline bench and hold one dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the weight toward the sides of the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep upper arms angled steady and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_double_dumbbell_decline_bench_skull_c.description: Lie on a decline bench and hold one dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the weight toward the sides of the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep upper arms angled steady and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_skull_crusher.description: Lie on a flat bench or the floor and squeeze one dumbbell vertically between both hands with arms extended above the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the weight toward the sides of the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep upper arms angled steady and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_incline_bench_sku.description: Lie on an incline bench and squeeze one dumbbell vertically between both hands with arms extended above the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the weight toward the sides of the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep upper arms angled steady and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_decline_bench_sku.description: Lie on a decline bench and squeeze one dumbbell vertically between both hands with arms extended above the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the weight toward the sides of the head, then extend back to straight arms. Keep upper arms angled steady and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_double_dumbbell_bent_over_row.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold a dumbbell in each hand below the shoulders. Row the weights toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the torso still and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_ez_bar_reverse_grip_bent_over_row.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold the EZ bar with a reverse grip below the shoulders. Row the weights toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the torso still and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and lock one kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked. Keep the load on the same side as the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and press a light kettlebell bottoms-up overhead with the bell above the handle. Keep the load on the side opposite the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

Take a wide Cossack stance and press a light kettlebell bottoms-up overhead with the bell above the handle. Keep the load on the same side as the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one kettlebell bottoms-up in the front rack with the wrist vertical. Keep the load on the side opposite the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one kettlebell bottoms-up in the front rack with the wrist vertical. Keep the load on the same side as the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_foot.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Rack one kettlebell at the shoulder on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Rack one bottoms-up kettlebell with the wrist vertical on the side opposite the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Rack one bottoms-up kettlebell with the wrist vertical on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Press a light bottoms-up kettlebell overhead on the side opposite the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Press a light bottoms-up kettlebell overhead on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_foot_e.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Lock one kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked on the side opposite the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Lock one kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack. Elevate the long-leg foot on a low block or plate so the heel stays supported as you shift. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bent_over_row.description: Hinge at the hips with one dumbbell hanging below the working shoulder and the free hand braced if needed. Pull the elbow toward the hip, then lower the weight without standing up. Keep the shoulders square to the floor and avoid yanking with the low back.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_knee_supported_ro.description: Place one knee and hand on a bench with the other foot on the floor and hold one dumbbell below the shoulder. Row the dumbbell toward the hip, pause briefly, then lower until the arm is long. Keep the back flat and avoid twisting the torso open.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and press two light kettlebells bottoms-up overhead with wrists vertical. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

Take a wide Cossack stance and press two light kettlebells bottoms-up overhead with wrists vertical. Elevate the long-leg foot on a low block or plate so the heel stays supported as you shift. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_foot_ele.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and rack two kettlebells at the shoulders. Elevate the long-leg foot on a low block or plate so the heel stays supported as you shift. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_low_switch.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold two kettlebells locked out overhead. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_foot_eleva.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold two kettlebells locked out overhead. Elevate the long-leg foot on a low block or plate so the heel stays supported as you shift. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_pullover.description: Lie on a bench with a dumbbell held over the chest and elbows softly bent. Lower the dumbbell in an arc behind the head until the upper arms line up with the torso, then pull it back over the chest. Keep the ribs down and avoid turning the motion into a low-back arch.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one kettlebell bottoms-up in the front rack with the wrist vertical. Shift into one hip, return through center, then repeat to the opposite side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_incline_bench_pullover.description: Lie back on an incline bench with a dumbbell held over the chest and elbows softly bent. Lower the dumbbell in an arc behind the head until the upper arms line up with the torso, then pull it back over the chest. Keep the ribs down and avoid turning the motion into a low-back arch.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one kettlebell bottoms-up in the front rack with the wrist vertical. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and lock one kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked. Shift into one hip, return through center, then repeat to the opposite side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_low_sw.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and lock one kettlebell overhead with the wrist stacked. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_tire_incline_push_up.description: Place both hands on the tire edge and walk the feet back into an incline plank. Lower the chest toward the tire, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid letting the hips sag before the chest reaches the tire.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_decline_be.description: Lie on a decline bench with two dumbbells at chest height and feet secured or planted. Press the dumbbells over the chest, then lower with elbows close to the ribs. Keep the shoulder blades set and avoid letting the weight drift behind the shoulder.

ff_bodyweight_eccentric_push_up.description: Start in a high plank with hands just outside the shoulders and feet back. Lower the chest toward the floor slowly for the assigned tempo, then reset from the knees or press back up if you can keep form. Keep the body in one line and avoid dropping the hips first.

ff_double_dumbbell_bicep_curl.description: Stand tall with feet about hip width and hold a palms-forward grip. Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders with a palms-forward grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_cable_knee_hover_quadruped_fire_hydra.description: Attach a low cable cuff to one ankle and start on hands and toes with knees hovering just above the floor. Keeping the knee bent, lift the cuffed thigh out to the side, then return without touching the knee down. Keep the pelvis square and avoid leaning away from the working hip.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_bicep_c.description: Sit back on an incline bench with the arms hanging slightly behind the torso and hold a palms-forward grip. Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders with a palms-forward grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_hammer_curl.description: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and hold a neutral grip. Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders with a neutral grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_ha.description: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and hold a neutral grip. Curl one dumbbell at a time toward the shoulders with a neutral grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_hammer_.description: Sit back on an incline bench with the arms hanging slightly behind the torso and hold a neutral grip. Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders with a neutral grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_half_kneeling_win.description: Kneel with the working-side knee down and hold one dumbbell locked out overhead. Hinge and rotate the torso so the free hand tracks down the inside of the leg, then return by driving the hips under the shoulders. Keep eyes on the top weight and avoid letting the overhead arm drift forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_windmill.description: Stand with feet angled slightly away from the loaded side. Hold one dumbbell overhead and the other near the floor-side hand as a counterbalance. Hinge and rotate so the lower hand tracks down the inside of the leg. Keep eyes on the top weight and avoid letting the overhead arm drift forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Sit back on an incline bench with the arms hanging slightly behind the torso and hold a neutral grip. Curl one dumbbell at a time toward the shoulders with a neutral grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_cyclist_thruster.description: Stand with heels elevated in a narrow stance and hold two dumbbells at the shoulders. Squat down, stand hard, and use the leg drive to press both dumbbells overhead in one smooth sequence. Finish with ribs down and avoid pausing halfway unless you need to reset balance.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and press a light kettlebell bottoms-up overhead with the bell above the handle. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_cyclist_t.description: Stand with heels elevated in a narrow stance and hold two dumbbells at the shoulders. Squat down, stand hard, and use the leg drive to press one dumbbell overhead at a time in one smooth sequence. Finish with ribs down and avoid pausing halfway unless you need to reset balance.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_pistol_.description: Hold one kettlebell by the horns at chest height and stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward. Lower into a pistol squat, stand by driving through the planted foot, then switch legs on the next rep. Keep the kettlebell close and avoid dropping into the bottom faster than you can control.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_pistol_squat.description: Hold one kettlebell by the horns at chest height and balance on the working leg with the other leg extended forward. Sit into a single-leg squat as low as control allows, then press through the standing foot to rise. Keep the heel planted and avoid letting the knee dive inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_pist.description: Grip the kettlebell horns tightly at the chest and stand on one leg with the free leg forward. Perform a controlled pistol squat, return to standing, and alternate to the other leg. Keep the horns close to the sternum and avoid twisting the torso to regain balance.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bent_press.description: Stand with feet slightly wider than hips with one dumbbell racked at the shoulder. Push the hip under the weight away as you bend sideways and press yourself under the dumbbell to a locked-out arm, then stand or kneel tall. Keep the forearm vertical and avoid pressing the weight away from your center line.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_half_kneeling_ben.description: Start half kneeling with one dumbbell racked at the shoulder. Push the hip under the weight away as you bend sideways and press yourself under the dumbbell to a locked-out arm, then stand or kneel tall. Keep the forearm vertical and avoid pressing the weight away from your center line.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_zottman_curl.description: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and hold a palms-up grip on the way up. Curl both dumbbells up with palms facing up, rotate to palms down at the top, and lower slowly. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_cable_straight_bar_tricep_pushdown.description: Stand facing a high cable with a straight bar held palms down and elbows pinned near the ribs. Press the bar down until the elbows are straight, then return to about 90 degrees. Keep the wrists firm and avoid leaning your torso over the bar.

ff_cable_straight_bar_reverse_grip_trice.description: Stand facing a high cable with a straight bar held palms up and elbows pinned near the ribs. Press the bar down until the elbows are straight, then return to about 90 degrees. Keep the wrists firm and avoid leaning your torso over the bar.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_zottman_c.description: Stand tall with feet about hip width and hold a palms-up grip on the way up. Curl one dumbbell at a time up with palms facing up, rotate to palms down at the top, and lower slowly. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_zo.description: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and hold a palms-up grip on the way up. Curl one dumbbell at a time up with palms facing up, rotate to palms down at the top, and lower slowly. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_tall_kneeling_ove.description: Kneel tall with glutes squeezed and hold the implement securely overhead. Bend the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep the elbows pointing forward and avoid flaring the ribs.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_press.description: Stand tall with feet about hip width and hold one dumbbell at shoulder height. Press the weight overhead until the arm is straight, then lower to the shoulder. Keep ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_seated_overhead_p.description: Sit tall with feet planted and hold one dumbbell at shoulder height. Press the weight overhead until the arm is straight, then lower to the shoulder. Keep ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_bicep_curl.description: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and hold a palms-forward grip. Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders with a palms-forward grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_zottman.description: Sit back on an incline bench with the arms hanging slightly behind the torso and hold a palms-up grip on the way up. Curl both dumbbells up with palms facing up, rotate to palms down at the top, and lower slowly. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Sit back on an incline bench with the arms hanging slightly behind the torso and hold a palms-up grip on the way up. Curl one dumbbell at a time up with palms facing up, rotate to palms down at the top, and lower slowly. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_ov.description: Sit tall with feet planted and hold two dumbbells at shoulder height. Press one dumbbell overhead at a time until the arm is straight, then lower to the shoulder. Keep ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_kettlebell_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and two kettlebells at chest level. Press the weight straight over the chest, then lower until the upper arm touches the floor. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid bouncing the arm off the floor.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and one kettlebell at chest level. Press the weight straight over the chest, then lower until the upper arm touches the floor. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid bouncing the arm off the floor.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_floor_p.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and two kettlebells at chest level. Press one weight at a time while the other stays stacked, then lower until the upper arm touches the floor. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid bouncing the arm off the floor.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell at the shoulder. Keep the load on the same side as the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_alternat.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell locked out overhead. Shift into one hip, return through center, then repeat to the opposite side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_seated_overhead_t.description: Sit tall with feet planted and hold the implement securely overhead. Bend the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep the elbows pointing forward and avoid flaring the ribs.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bent_ov.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold a kettlebell in each hand below the shoulders. Row one side at a time while the other arm stays long. Keep the torso still and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_battle_rope_double_wave.description: Stand facing the battle-rope anchor with feet about shoulder width and a rope end in each hand. Drive both arms up and down together to send even waves to the anchor. Keep the hips back, ribs braced, and avoid turning the motion into a whole-body bounce.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_raise.description: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand in front of the thighs. Raise both arms forward to shoulder height with elbows soft, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid swinging the hips to lift the weights.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_prone_l.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with light dumbbells hanging below the shoulders. Raise the arms out to the sides until they line up with the torso, then lower slowly. Keep the neck long and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_seated_bicep_curl.description: Sit tall on a bench with feet planted and hold a one dumbbell squeezed vertically between both palms. Curl the squeezed dumbbell toward the chest, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_prone_h.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the dumbbells hanging below the shoulders and hold a neutral grip. Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders with a neutral grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_low_swit.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one dumbbell locked out overhead. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_foot_elevated_cossack.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one clubbell in order position near the shoulder. Keep the load on the same side as the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_foot_elevat.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Hold one clubbell in order position near the shoulder on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold one macebell in order position near the shoulder. Keep the load on the same side as the bent working leg. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_foot_elevat.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Hold one macebell in order position near the shoulder on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_dummbell_front_rack_foot_e.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Hold one dumbbell at the shoulder on the side opposite the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_foot_e.description: Take a wide Cossack stance with the long-leg foot elevated on a low block. Hold one dumbbell at the shoulder on the same side as the bent leg. Shift toward the working leg while the other leg stays long. Keep the heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the dumbbells hanging below the shoulders and hold a neutral grip. Curl one dumbbell at a time toward the shoulders with a neutral grip, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the upper arms quiet and avoid swinging the shoulders to start the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pre.description: Lie on an incline bench with one dumbbell at chest height and feet secured or planted. Press the dumbbell over the chest, then lower with elbows slightly below shoulder height. Keep the shoulder blades set and avoid letting the weight drift behind the shoulder.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_decline_bench_pre.description: Lie on a decline bench with one dumbbell at chest height and feet secured or planted. Press the dumbbell over the chest, then lower with elbows slightly below shoulder height. Keep the shoulder blades set and avoid letting the weight drift behind the shoulder.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_cope.description: Set up in a Copenhagen side plank with the top leg supported on a bench and the bottom forearm under the shoulder. Hold a light kettlebell bottoms-up in the top hand and lift the hips until the body forms a straight line. Keep the wrist vertical, shoulders stacked, and avoid letting the hips sag or rotate forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and two dumbbells at chest level. Press the weight straight over the chest, then lower until the upper arm touches the floor. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid bouncing the arm off the floor.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_glute_br.description: Lie on a bench or the floor in a glute bridge with two kettlebells held bottoms-up at chest level. Keep the hips lifted as you press both kettlebells over the chest and lower with control. Maintain vertical wrists and avoid letting the bells tip or the ribs flare.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_floor_pre.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and two dumbbells at chest level. Press one weight at a time while the other stays stacked, then lower until the upper arm touches the floor. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid bouncing the arm off the floor.

ff_kettlebell_seated_single_leg_tibialis.description: Sit tall with one heel on the floor and a light kettlebell handle hooked carefully over the forefoot. Pull the toes toward the shin, then lower the forefoot under control. Keep the knee still and avoid curling the toes to move the kettlebell.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_calf_raise.description: Sit tall with kettlebells resting on the thighs. Raise the heels by pressing through the balls of the feet, then lower slowly. Keep the ankles tracking straight and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_feet_elevate.description: Sit tall with kettlebells resting on the thighs and the balls of the feet on a low edge. Raise the heels by pressing through the balls of the feet, then lower slowly. Keep the ankles tracking straight and avoid bouncing through the bottom.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_thruster.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and hold two dumbbells at the shoulders. Squat down, stand hard, and use the leg drive to press one dumbbell overhead at a time in one smooth sequence. Finish with ribs down and avoid pausing halfway unless you need to reset balance.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent and one dumbbell at chest level. Press the weight straight over the chest, then lower until the upper arm touches the floor. Keep the wrist over the elbow and avoid bouncing the arm off the floor.

ff_single_arm_macebell_inside_circle.description: Stand tall with one hand on the macebell handle and the weighted end controlled near shoulder height. Guide the weighted end in an inside arc around the head and back to the start. Keep the circle close to the body and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_single_arm_macebell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall with one hand on the macebell handle and the weighted end controlled near shoulder height. Guide the weighted end in an outside arc around the head and back to the start. Keep the circle close to the body and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_single_arm_macebell_inside_circle_to_.description: Stand tall with one hand on the macebell handle and the weighted end controlled near shoulder height. Circle the tool inward around the head, return to center, then reverse into an outside circle. Keep the circle close to the body and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_macebell_inside_circle.description: Stand tall with both hands on the macebell handle and the weighted end controlled near shoulder height. Guide the weighted end in an inside arc around the head and back to the start. Keep the circle close to the body and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_macebell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall with both hands on the macebell handle and the weighted end controlled near shoulder height. Guide the weighted end in an outside arc around the head and back to the start. Keep the circle close to the body and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_macebell_inside_circle_to_outside_cir.description: Stand tall with both hands on the macebell handle and the weighted end controlled near shoulder height. Circle the tool inward around the head, return to center, then reverse into an outside circle. Keep the circle close to the body and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_battle_rope_clap.description: Stand facing the battle-rope anchor with feet about shoulder width and one rope end in each hand. Sweep the hands apart, then clap the ropes toward each other at chest height to send waves down the lines. Keep the knees soft and avoid shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.

ff_battle_rope_inward_circle.description: Stand facing the rope anchor with a rope end in each hand and elbows slightly bent. Circle both hands inward toward each other to make smooth rope spirals. Keep the chest quiet and avoid letting the shoulders creep up as the circles speed up.

ff_battle_rope_outward_circle.description: Stand facing the rope anchor with a rope end in each hand and knees softly bent. Circle both hands outward away from each other so the ropes spiral evenly. Brace the trunk and avoid rocking backward to make the ropes move.

ff_double_kettlebell_tall_kneeling_shrug.description: Kneel tall with one kettlebell in each hand hanging at your sides. Lift the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower them fully. Keep the glutes squeezed and arms long, avoiding shoulder rolls or leaning back.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_bent_over.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold a dumbbell in each hand below the shoulders. Row one side at a time while the other arm stays long. Keep the torso still and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_cossack_squat.description: Anchor the battle ropes and take a wide Cossack stance and shift side to side with one rope end in each hand. As the lower-body pattern continues, create alternating waves by driving one arm up as the other arm comes down. Keep the torso braced and land or step with the knee aligned over the middle toes rather than letting the ropes pull you forward.

ff_bodyweight_low_switch_cossack_squat.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and use bodyweight only with arms reaching for balance. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_cossack_s.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold the barbell in the crooks of the elbows with forearms angled up. Shift into one hip, return through center, then repeat to the opposite side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_cossack_squat.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold the barbell in the crooks of the elbows with forearms angled up. Shift toward the working leg by bending that knee while the other leg stays long. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_low_switch_cossack_sq.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold the barbell in the crooks of the elbows with forearms angled up. Sink into one hip, keep the other leg long, then stay low as you switch through center to the other side. Keep the working heel heavy and the knee tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_foot_elevated_cossack.description: Take a wide Cossack stance and hold the barbell in the crooks of the elbows with forearms angled up.
