Bend the front knee forward over the toes as you lower, then drive through the whole front foot to stand. Keep the heel planted and knee tracking with the toes instead of bouncing into the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_foot_eleva.description: Hold a kettlebell at each side in a suitcase carry position. Set a split stance with the front foot on a low plate or wedge, keeping most weight through that foot. Bend the front knee forward over the toes as you lower, then drive through the whole front foot to stand. Keep the heel planted and knee tracking with the toes instead of bouncing into the bottom.

ff_barbell_zercher_split_squat.description: Cradle the barbell in the bends of the elbows with fists close and ribs braced. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_suspension_alternating_split_squat_ju.description: Hold the suspension handles lightly with the straps even and set a split stance. Dip into the split squat, then jump just enough to switch legs in the air. Land in the opposite split stance with soft knees. Keep the torso tall and avoid yanking on the straps to pull yourself up.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Hold one dumbbell at your side opposite the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Hold one dumbbell at your side on the same side as the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_split_squa.description: Hold two dumbbells in the front rack with elbows slightly forward. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Hold one dumbbell in the front rack opposite the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Hold one dumbbell in the front rack on the same side as the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_split_squat.description: Press two dumbbells overhead and keep the arms stacked over the shoulders. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Press one dumbbell overhead opposite the working front leg and keep the wrist stacked. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Press one dumbbell overhead on the same side as the working front leg and keep the wrist stacked. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_split_squat.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically in a goblet position at the chest. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_wall_ball_feet_elevated_russian_twist.description: Sit with heels hovering and hold the wall ball at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the ribs to carry the ball from hip to hip. Keep the feet quiet and avoid swinging the ball with loose arms.

ff_wall_ball_russian_twist.description: Sit with knees bent and hold the wall ball at chest height. Lean back until the trunk is braced, then rotate the ribs to move the ball from side to side. Keep the chest tall and avoid yanking the ball past your hips.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_split_squat.description: Set the Bulgarian bag across the upper back and hold the handles so it cannot roll. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_double_clubbell_order_split_squat.description: Hold two clubbells in the order position with the handles vertical and elbows close. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_clubbell_order_split_squat.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at shoulder height with the handle vertical. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Hold one clubbell in the order position opposite the working front leg with the handle vertical. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Hold one clubbell in the order position on the same side as the working front leg with the handle vertical. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_macebell_order_split_squat.description: Hold the macebell in an order position with the head vertical and the hands steady near the chest. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Hold the macebell in an order position opposite the working front leg, keeping the head vertical and close. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Hold the macebell in an order position on the same side as the working front leg, keeping the head vertical and close. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_plate_overhead_split_squat.description: Hold a weight plate overhead with straight arms and ribs down. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_split_squat.description: Bear-hug the heavy sandbag against the chest with forearms wrapped around it. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_contralateral_.description: Shoulder the heavy sandbag opposite the working front leg and keep the torso tall. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_ipsilateral_sp.description: Shoulder the heavy sandbag on the same side as the working front leg and keep the torso tall. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_split_squat.description: Place the sandbag across the upper back and keep both hands on the bag. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_split_squat.description: Hug the sandbag high in the front rack with elbows under the load. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_tall_knee.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders, palms facing you and glutes squeezed. Rotate and press one dumbbell overhead, lower it back to the shoulder, then alternate sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the weight passes the face.

ff_landmine_goblet_split_squat.description: Cup the end of the landmine sleeve at chest height with both hands. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_landmine_front_rack_contra.description: Hold the end of the landmine sleeve in a front-rack position opposite the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_landmine_front_rack_ipsila.description: Hold the end of the landmine sleeve in a front-rack position on the same side as the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_single_arm_landmine_suitcase_contrala.description: Stand beside the landmine and hold the sleeve low opposite the working front leg like a suitcase. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_double_kettlebell_gunslinger.description: Stand tall with a kettlebell in each hand hanging just outside the hips. Hinge slightly, then pull the bells upward close to the body before guiding them back to the start. Keep the wrists neutral and avoid flinging the bells away from your sides.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Hold one kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the working front leg with the wrist straight. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_split_squa.description: Hold a kettlebell at each side in a suitcase carry position. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side opposite the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_ipsila.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side on the same side as the working front leg. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. ff_kettlebell_goblet_split_squat.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns in a goblet position at the chest. Set a long split stance with the back heel lifted and hips square. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee in line with the toes and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_march.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height and stand tall. March in place by lifting one knee to hip height, setting it down quietly, then alternating. Keep the ribs stacked over the hips and avoid leaning away from the bell.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_raise.description: Stand tall with one dumbbell in front of the thigh, palm facing back or inward. Raise the straight arm to shoulder height, then lower under control. Keep the shoulder down and avoid swinging the torso to start the lift.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_cross_body_hammer.description: Stand with one dumbbell at your side using a neutral grip. Curl the weight diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder, then lower it slowly. Keep the elbow close to the ribs and avoid turning the curl into a shoulder raise.

ff_suspension_hamstring_curl.description: Lie on your back with both heels in the suspension straps and hips lifted. Bend the knees to pull the heels toward you, then extend the legs with control. Keep the hips level and avoid dropping the pelvis as the legs straighten.

ff_suspension_single_leg_hamstring_curl.description: Lie on your back with one heel in a suspension strap and the other leg held clear. Lift the hips, curl the strapped heel toward you, then extend the knee slowly. Keep the pelvis square and avoid twisting toward the working leg.

ff_superband_prone_hamstring_curl.description: Anchor a superband low behind you and loop it around the ankles while lying face down. Curl the heels toward the glutes, then return the legs to straight with tension. Keep the thighs on the floor and avoid arching the low back.

ff_cable_prone_hamstring_curl.description: Attach a low cable cuff to the ankle and lie face down facing away from the stack. Curl the heel toward the glutes, pause briefly, then lower with control. Keep the thigh pressed down and avoid lifting the hips to finish the rep.

ff_superband_prone_bench_hamstring_curl.description: Anchor a superband low behind a bench and loop it around the ankles while lying face down on the bench. Curl the heels toward the glutes, then extend the legs slowly. Keep the thighs on the pad and avoid letting the band snap you straight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bicep_curl.description: Stand tall with one dumbbell at your side and palm facing forward. Curl the weight toward the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is straight again. Keep the upper arm still and avoid leaning back to move the weight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_hammer_curl.description: Stand with one dumbbell at your side in a thumbs-up grip. Curl the weight toward the shoulder without turning the wrist, then lower it slowly. Keep the elbow close to the ribs and avoid swinging the dumbbell forward.

ff_ring_dips.description: Set the rings high enough that your feet clear the floor and start in a straight-arm support. Bend the elbows to lower between the rings, then press back to support. Keep the rings close and shoulders down instead of letting the straps flare wide.

ff_bodyweight_single_arm_push_up.description: Set a wide-foot plank with one hand under the chest and the free hand off the floor. Lower the chest toward the working hand, then press back to plank. Brace hard and avoid letting the hips twist open as you push.

ff_bodyweight_aztec_push_up.description: Start in a strong push-up plank with hands under the shoulders. Lower the chest, then explode up so the hands and feet leave the floor and reach briefly toward the toes. Land softly back in plank and avoid collapsing through the low back.

ff_slider_barbell_front_rack_curtsy_lung.description: Set the barbell in the front rack with elbows lifted and the bar resting across the shoulders. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_front_rack_c.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack with elbows close to the ribs. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_suspension_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the suspension handles lightly for balance with the straps even. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_landmine_goblet_curtsy_lunge.description: Cup the end of the landmine sleeve at chest height with both hands. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_barbell_overhead_curtsy_lunge.description: Press the barbell overhead with hands wide enough to keep the wrists stacked over the shoulders. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_barbell_back_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Set the barbell across the upper back and pull the elbows slightly down to brace the trunk. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_barbell_zercher_curtsy_lunge.description: Cradle the barbell in the bends of the elbows with fists close and ribs braced. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_curtsy.description: Set the Bulgarian bag across the upper back and hold the handles so it cannot roll. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_suitcase_cur.description: Hold a kettlebell at each side in a suitcase carry position. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_overhead_cur.description: Lock two kettlebells overhead with biceps near the ears. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_f.description: Clean two kettlebells bottoms-up into the front rack with handles squeezed firmly. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_o.description: Lock two kettlebells overhead bottoms-up, squeezing the handles so the bells stay vertical. Stand with the working foot planted and the other foot on a slider. Slide the free foot diagonally behind and across the planted leg, then press through the planted foot to pull it back under you. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_front_ra.description: Hold one kettlebell in the front rack opposite the working standing leg with the wrist straight. ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsi.description: Hold the macebell in an order position on the same side as the working standing leg, keeping the head vertical and close. ff_kettlebell_goblet_cyclist_squat.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at the chest and stand with heels raised on a wedge or plates. Squat down with the knees traveling forward, then drive through the balls of the feet and heels to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_knee_over_toe_split.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns in a goblet position at the chest. Set a split stance with most weight through the front foot and the back heel lifted. Bend the front knee forward over the toes as you lower, then drive through the whole front foot to stand. Keep the heel planted and knee tracking with the toes instead of bouncing into the bottom.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_foot_elevated_knee_.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns in a goblet position at the chest. Set a split stance with the front foot on a low plate or wedge, keeping most weight through that foot. Bend the front knee forward over the toes as you lower, then drive through the whole front foot to stand. Keep the heel planted and knee tracking with the toes instead of bouncing into the bottom.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_walking_lunge.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge, push through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next step. Keep the chest up and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_ring_maltese.description: Start from a strong ring support or assisted Maltese progression with the straps even. Push the rings wide as the body lowers toward a straight horizontal line, then hold only the controlled range you own. Keep the elbows locked and shoulders active instead of sagging between the rings.

ff_barbell_front_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Set the barbell in the front rack with elbows lifted and the bar resting across the shoulders. Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_curtsy_l.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack with elbows close to the ribs. Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_suspension_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the suspension handles lightly for balance with the straps even. Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_barbell_overhead_curtsy_lunge.description: Press the barbell overhead with hands wide enough to keep the wrists stacked over the shoulders. Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_barbell_back_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Set the barbell across the upper back and pull the elbows slightly down to brace the trunk. Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_barbell_zercher_curtsy_lunge.description: Cradle the barbell in the bends of the elbows with fists close and ribs braced. Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

Stand tall with feet under the hips. Step one foot diagonally behind and across the standing leg, then press through the front foot to return to standing. Keep the hips square and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of letting the crossover twist you.

ff_ring_archer_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with arms straight and shoulders set down. Pull toward one ring while the opposite arm reaches out straighter to the side, then lower with control and alternate. Keep the body quiet and avoid swinging to reach the top.

ff_barbell_single_leg_bent_knee_bicep_cu.description: Stand on one slightly bent leg while holding a barbell with an underhand grip. Curl the bar toward the shoulders, then lower it without losing the single-leg stance. Keep the elbows near the ribs and avoid using balance wobbles to heave the bar.

ff_bodyweight_freestanding_handstand.description: Place the hands shoulder width on the floor and kick or press up to a freestanding handstand. Push tall through straight arms while keeping ribs tucked and weight balanced over the knuckles. Step or cartwheel down with control, and avoid banana-backing or letting the shoulders sink.

ff_bodyweight_wall_eccentric_straddle_pr.description: Face away from a wall in a handstand, open the legs into a straddle, and lightly use the wall for balance. Slowly lean the shoulders forward and lower the feet toward the floor under control. Keep elbows straight and hips piked, stopping before the low back arches or the feet drop quickly.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_carry.description: Hold one kettlebell by the horns at chest height with elbows close to the ribs. Walk with short, quiet steps while keeping the bell centered and the ribs stacked over the pelvis. Do not let the wrists bend back or the bell pull you into a forward lean.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_carry.description: Cup the kettlebell in a goblet hold against the upper chest and stand tall before moving. Walk forward with controlled steps, keeping shoulders down and elbows tucked. Keep the load close instead of letting it drift away from the torso.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_carry.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically at chest height with both palms around the top end. Walk with a tall spine and even steps while keeping the dumbbell close to the sternum. Avoid shrugging or leaning back to support the weight.

ff_single_arm_ring_feet_elevated_row.description: Set one ring low and place both feet on a bench so your body starts in a straight elevated plank. Row with one arm by pulling the ring toward the ribs, then lower until the arm is long. Keep hips square to the floor and avoid twisting open to finish the pull.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_snatch.description: Place two kettlebells on the floor between the feet and hinge to grip both handles. Drive through the legs, pull the bells close, and punch both hands overhead in one powerful snatch. Catch with wrists straight and avoid letting the bells swing wide away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_half_snatc.description: Start with one kettlebell on the floor between the feet and hinge over it with a flat back. Snatch the bell overhead, then lower it to the rack before returning it to the floor for the next rep. Keep the bell close on the pull and avoid letting it crash onto the forearm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_dea.description: Set one kettlebell on the floor and alternate hands each rep from the dead start. Pull from the hinge, punch to a stable overhead lockout, then lower through the rack before switching sides. Re-square the feet and hips before each pull instead of twisting off the floor.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Begin each rep with the kettlebell parked on the floor slightly in front of you. Hike it back, drive the hips, and snatch to overhead, then park it before changing hands. Keep the shoulder packed at lockout and do not bounce through continuous reps.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_snat.description: Park the kettlebell in front of the feet, hike it once, and snatch it overhead with one arm. Return the bell to the floor and let it settle before starting the next rep. Use the hip snap to move the bell and avoid pulling it up with a straight, stiff arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_snatch.description: Set two kettlebells on the floor just ahead of the toes and hinge to grip them. Hike both bells back, drive the hips, and punch to a double overhead lockout before parking them again. Keep the bells close and reset your back position between reps.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_swin.description: Park one kettlebell in front of you and grip it with one hand from a hip hinge. Hike it back, snap the hips to swing to chest height, then guide it back to the floor and pause. Keep the free arm quiet and avoid squatting the bell up.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Start with the kettlebell parked ahead of the feet and swing it with one hand at a time. Let the bell float near chest height, switch hands only when it is weightless, and park it before the next rep. Keep the spine long and do not reach for the switch while the bell is falling.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_swing.description: Place two kettlebells just in front of a wide stance and hinge to grip both handles. Hike them back, snap the hips, and swing both bells to about chest height before parking them on the floor. Keep lats tight so the bells do not drift wider than your stance.

ff_ring_l_sit_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with palms facing each other and legs held straight in an L-sit. Pull the chest toward the rings while keeping the thighs lifted, then lower until the arms are long. Avoid dropping the legs or craning the neck to reach the top.

ff_suspension_skater_jump.description: Hold the suspension handles lightly and stand on one leg with the other leg crossing behind. Jump laterally to the opposite foot, using the straps only for balance. Land softly with the knee tracking over the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_clean.description: Park two kettlebells in front of the feet and hinge to grip both handles. Hike them back, drive the hips, and guide both bells into a double front rack before returning them to the floor. Catch with elbows close and wrists straight rather than letting the bells slap the arms.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_clea.description: Set one kettlebell slightly ahead of the feet and clean it from a parked start. Drive with the hips, keep the bell close, and receive it softly in the front rack before parking it again. Pause between reps so the setup stays crisp and the back does not round.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Clean one kettlebell from a dead parked start, return it to the floor, and switch hands each rep. Use the legs and hips to float the bell into the rack with the elbow close to the ribs. Reset your hinge before each side and avoid yanking the bell off the floor.

ff_clubbell_staggered_stance_rotational_.description: Stand staggered with the clubbell in both hands and the heavy end angled across the body. Rotate through the hips as you swing the club in a diagonal arc, then guide it back across the centerline. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid letting the low back do all the twisting.

Begin in a staggered stance with the clubbell hanging across the lead side. Drive from the hips, rotate, and clean the club into an order position near the shoulder. Keep the handle close on the catch and avoid muscling it up with a bent wrist.

Clean the clubbell rotationally from a staggered stance into the order position. Step into a forward lunge after the catch, lower both knees, then push back to the start. Keep the club close to the shoulder and avoid letting the front knee cave as the load rotates.

ff_ring_bulgarian_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with a wider, externally rotated Bulgarian-style grip. Pull the chest between the rings while keeping the shoulders down, then lower under control. Do not let the rings drift unevenly or shrug the shoulders into the ears.

ff_ring_bulgarian_l_sit_pull_up.description: Hold an L-sit under the rings with a wider Bulgarian pull-up grip. Pull the chest upward without letting the legs drop, then return to a full hang. Keep the rings even and avoid turning the rep into a swinging knee raise.

ff_single_arm_landmine_split_snatch.description: Stand over the free end of a landmine bar and grip the sleeve with one hand. Drive from the legs, punch the bar up the landmine arc, and split the feet to receive it overhead. Keep the bar path close and recover the feet before lowering.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_power_clean.description: Hold two dumbbells at thigh level and hinge into a hang position. Extend the hips quickly and guide the dumbbells to the shoulders in a partial squat catch. Keep the weights close to the body and avoid curling them slowly with the arms.

ff_single_arm_barbell_bicep_curl.description: Hold the center of a light barbell in one hand with the palm facing up. Curl the bar toward the shoulder while keeping the upper arm close to the side, then lower with control. Keep the bar level and avoid leaning back to overcome the uneven balance.

ff_ring_false_grip_pull_up.description: Set a false grip by wrapping the wrists over the rings before hanging. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the wrists high on the rings, then lower without losing the grip. Avoid letting the wrists slip into a regular hang during the rep.

ff_slider_kettlebell_goblet_lateral_lung.description: Hold a kettlebell in a goblet position and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out to the side as the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep the kettlebell close to the chest and avoid letting the planted knee collapse inward.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Put the feet on a box and grip low rings under the shoulders, then walk the hips high into a pike. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the rings and press back to straight arms. Keep the straps quiet and avoid turning the rep into a flat push-up.

ff_single_arm_landmine_split_clean.description: Grip the sleeve of the landmine bar with one hand from a hinged start. Pull the bar up its arc and split the feet as you receive it near the shoulder. Keep the elbow under the sleeve and avoid catching with the torso twisted open.

ff_double_dumbbell_squat_clean.description: Start with two dumbbells on the floor or hanging low between the feet. Extend the hips, pull the dumbbells to the shoulders, and receive them in a front squat before standing. Keep elbows high in the catch and avoid letting the weights drift outside the legs.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_squat_clean.description: Begin with dumbbells at the thighs, hinge to the hang, and jump them upward. Drop into a squat as the dumbbells land at the shoulders, then stand tall. Keep the pull close and avoid catching with rounded shoulders.

ff_single_arm_landmine_split_jerk.description: Hold the landmine sleeve at one shoulder with a braced stance. Dip straight down, drive the bar up, and split the feet to receive the arm locked out on the landmine path. Keep the ribs down and avoid pressing early before the leg drive.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_ipsilateral_for.description: Snatch one kettlebell overhead and keep it locked out on the same side as the stepping leg. Step forward into a lunge, bend both knees, then drive back to the start. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid letting the front knee drift inward.

ff_clubbell_hammer_swing.description: Hold the clubbell with both hands in a hammer grip and stand in an athletic stance. Swing the heavy end down and forward in a compact arc, using the hips and trunk to guide it. Keep the shoulders packed and avoid letting the club pull the chest forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_hammer_swing.description: Grip one clubbell in a hammer position at your side with the feet set wide enough to hinge. Swing the club in front of the body with one arm while the free arm balances the torso. Keep the wrist firm and avoid letting the heavy end yank the shoulder forward.

ff_double_clubbell_hammer_swing.description: Hold one clubbell in each hand with hammer grips and stand tall. Hinge slightly, swing both clubs forward together, and stop them under control near chest height. Keep the arcs even and avoid shrugging as the heavy ends rise.

ff_ring_tuck_back_lever_with_alternating.description: Start inverted on the rings, lower into a tucked back lever, and keep the straps quiet. Extend one leg at a time from the tuck, return it, then alternate sides without changing shoulder position. Press the rings down and avoid sagging the hips as the leg reaches out.

ff_ring_tuck_front_lever_with_alternatin.description: Hang from the rings, pull the shoulders down, and lift into a tuck front lever. Extend one leg at a time until it is straight, then tuck it back before switching sides. Keep the hips level and avoid bending the elbows to hold the shape.

ff_barbell_split_clean.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull close from the floor, then split the feet as you receive the bar on the shoulders. Stand to recover the split and avoid jumping the bar forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_split_clean.description: Hold two dumbbells at the hang with palms facing the thighs. Drive the hips, pull the dumbbells to the shoulders, and split the feet to receive them. Recover to standing before lowering, and keep the dumbbells close instead of swinging them outward.

ff_ring_archer_push_up.description: Set the rings low and begin in a straight plank with hands under the shoulders. Lower toward one ring while the opposite arm reaches straighter to the side, then press back and switch sides. Keep hips square and avoid letting the reaching ring drift out of control.

ff_wall_ball_half_kneeling_lateral_toss.description: Kneel side-on to a wall with the inside knee up and hold the wall ball at the outside hip. Rotate through the ribs and hips to toss the ball laterally into the wall, then catch and reset. Keep the pelvis stable and avoid throwing only with the arms.

ff_wall_ball_isometric_split_squat_later.description: Hold a split squat halfway down while standing side-on to a wall with a wall ball at the outside hip. Rotate the torso to toss the ball laterally, catch it, and keep the lunge height steady. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid bobbing up between throws.

ff_superband_assisted_bar_muscle_up.description: Loop a superband over the pull-up bar and place a foot or knee in it. Pull high to the bar, roll the chest over, and press to straight arms on top. Use the band for assistance without bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_parallette_push_up_to_l_sit.description: Grip the parallettes and start in a straight push-up plank. Perform a push-up, then step or hop the feet through and press down into an L-sit. Keep shoulders depressed in the sit and avoid clipping the feet on the handles.

ff_single_arm_barbell_bent_press.description: Hold a light barbell in one hand at the shoulder with the elbow under the bar. Hinge and side-bend away as you press the bar to lockout, then stand tall under it. Keep eyes on the bar and avoid forcing depth if the wrist or shoulder loses its stack.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sots.description: Clean one kettlebell bottoms-up to the rack, then descend into a deep squat. From the bottom of the squat, press the bell overhead and lower it back to the rack before standing or repeating. Keep the torso upright and avoid letting the bell tip as you press.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sots_pre.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the rack and sit into the bottom of a squat. Press both bells overhead from the squat, then lower them with wrists vertical. Keep knees tracking with the toes and avoid leaning back to clear the bells.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Sit in a deep squat with two kettlebells held bottoms-up in the rack. Press one bell overhead while the other stays parked, lower it, then press the other side. Keep the nonworking bell steady and avoid rotating the torso as you alternate.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_s.description: Settle into the bottom of a squat with both kettlebells bottoms-up at the shoulders. Press one bell up as the other lowers, moving in a seesaw rhythm while staying in the squat. Keep both wrists stacked and stop if the bells wobble faster than you can control.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_h.description: Set up half kneeling with the kettlebell in front of the working-side hip. Snatch the bell overhead from the floor or a short hike, then lower it to the rack before repeating. Keep the down-knee glute tight and avoid arching through the low back.

ff_ring_alternating_reach_push_up.description: Set the rings low and start in a plank with one hand on each ring. As you lower, reach one ring slightly forward, then press back and alternate the reach on the next rep. Keep the straps quiet and avoid letting the hips rotate toward the reaching arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_outside_snatch.description: Stand between two kettlebells placed just outside the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and snatch both bells up along the outside of the body to overhead. Keep the bells close to the shoulders and avoid casting them in wide arcs.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_half_sn.description: Place the kettlebell outside one foot and hinge toward it with a flat back. Snatch the bell to overhead, lower it to the rack, then return it outside the foot. Keep the torso square and avoid twisting the low back during the outside pull.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_snatch.description: Start with one kettlebell outside the working-side foot. Drive from the hinge and guide the bell close to the body into an overhead snatch. Keep the shoulder packed at the top and avoid swinging the bell away from your midline.

ff_ring_l_sit_dips.description: Support yourself on the rings with arms straight and legs held forward in an L-sit. Bend the elbows to dip while keeping the rings close, then press back to support. Keep the legs lifted and avoid letting the shoulders roll forward at the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_single_l.description: Stand on one leg with the free knee bent and two kettlebells held bottoms-up at the shoulders. Press both bells overhead while keeping the standing foot rooted. Keep wrists vertical and avoid leaning back or setting the free foot down mid-rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sing.description: Stand on one leg and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell at the shoulder opposite the standing leg. Press overhead while the free knee stays bent in front of you. Keep the ribs down and avoid tipping toward the kettlebell side.

ff_single_ring_chin_up.description: Hang from one ring with a supinated or rotating chin-up grip and the free hand off the strap. Pull the chest toward the ring, then lower until the working arm is long. Keep the body from spinning and avoid kicking to finish the rep.

ff_single_arm_suspension_single_leg_stan.description: Hold one suspension handle with the arm opposite the standing leg and lift the free knee. Lean back, row the handle to the ribs, then lower with control. Keep hips square and avoid rotating toward the pulling arm.

Stand on one leg and hold the suspension handle on the same side as the stance leg. Lean back in a straight line, pull the elbow toward the ribs, and return slowly. Keep the lifted knee quiet and avoid shrugging the shoulder at the top.

Stand on one leg with the suspension handle in the opposite hand and palm facing up. Lean back, curl the handle toward the forehead, then straighten the arm with control. Keep the elbow high and avoid twisting the torso to shorten the curl.

Balance on one leg and hold the same-side suspension handle with a supinated grip. Curl your body toward the handle while keeping the elbow lifted, then lower slowly. Keep the lifted knee steady and avoid dropping the elbow as fatigue builds.

ff_ring_l_sit_chin_up.description: Hang from the rings with palms turned toward you and legs straight in an L-sit. Pull the chest up between the rings, then lower until the elbows are straight again. Keep the thighs lifted and avoid swinging the legs for momentum.

ff_single_ring_l_sit_chin_up.description: Hang from one ring in a chin-up grip and raise the legs into an L-sit. Pull toward the ring without letting the body spin, then lower under control. Keep the toes high and avoid bending the knees to make the pull easier.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sing.description: Stand on one leg with a bottoms-up kettlebell racked on the same side as the stance leg. Press the bell overhead while the free knee remains bent and lifted. Keep the bell vertical and avoid shifting the hip out to balance the load.

ff_ring_pelican_curl.description: Set the rings near chest height and stand between them with palms up. Lean forward as the arms extend behind the torso, then curl yourself back by bending the elbows. Keep the body rigid and avoid bouncing out of the stretched bottom position.

ff_ring_two_finger_chin_up.description: Hang from the rings using a two-finger chin-up grip on each hand. Pull smoothly until the chest approaches the hands, then lower to straight arms. Keep shoulders active and stop before the fingers open or the body swings.

ff_bodyweight_pseudo_planche_push_up.description: Place the hands near the hips or with fingers turned slightly out, then lean the shoulders well past the wrists. Lower the body as one piece and press back to the same forward lean. Keep the hips from sagging and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

ff_parallette_pseudo_planche_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes beside the ribs and lean the shoulders forward of the hands. Lower under control, then press back while maintaining the planche lean. Keep wrists stacked on the handles and avoid letting the feet push you backward.

ff_parallette_single_leg_close_grip_push.description: Set two parallettes close under the chest and lift one leg behind you. Lower between the handles with elbows near the ribs, then press back to a straight plank. Keep hips level and avoid dropping the lifted leg as you press.

ff_single_arm_macebell_single_leg_standi.description: Stand on one leg and hold the mace in the hand opposite the stance leg. Guide the mace through a 360 around the body while the free knee stays bent and lifted. Pause in the vertical stack and avoid rushing the circle if balance shifts.

ff_macebell_inside_circle_to_360.description: Hold the mace vertically in front, then draw an inside circle to guide the head behind the body. Continue the path into a full 360 and return to the vertical stack before the next rep. Keep elbows close through the back side and avoid letting the mace pull the ribs open.

ff_single_arm_landmine_single_leg_standi.description: Stand on one leg with the landmine sleeve in the same-side hand at shoulder height. Press along the landmine arc while the free knee stays bent, then lower to the shoulder. Keep the stance hip level and avoid leaning into the bar.

Balance on one leg and hold the landmine sleeve in the opposite hand at the shoulder. Press the bar up and forward on its arc, then lower with control. Keep the torso square to the anchor and avoid rotating as the bar moves.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_singl.description: Stand on one leg in front of the landmine and press the sleeve with one hand at a time. Lower to the shoulder, switch hands, and keep the free knee bent throughout the set. Brace before each press and avoid wobbling the hips side to side.

ff_macebell_alternating_360_to_pivot_spl.description: Perform a macebell 360, return to the vertical stack, then pivot into a split squat as the mace travels into an uppercut path. Alternate directions each rep and stand tall before the next swing. Keep the front knee aligned and avoid letting the mace pull you out of the lunge.

ff_double_clubbell_single_leg_standing_b.description: Stand on one leg with a clubbell in each hand in side order positions. Cast the clubs beside the shoulders, then press them outward into side flag positions while the free knee stays bent. Keep lats engaged and avoid leaning back to counter the clubs.

ff_macebell_alternating_360_to_front_fla.description: Swing the mace through a 360 and catch it in the vertical stack. Alternate direction, then press the mace forward into a front flag with both hands before drawing it back. Keep the press slow and avoid letting the head of the mace drop below chest height.

ff_double_indian_club_crescent_swing.description: Hold one light Indian club in each hand at the sides. Swing both clubs through matching crescent arcs with smooth wrist turns, then return to the start without pausing. Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid forcing speed before both paths match.

ff_single_arm_macebell_inside_circle_to_.description: Hold the mace with one hand near the end of the handle and start in a vertical stack. Trace an inside circle into a full 360 around the body, then pull back to vertical. Keep the path close and avoid death-gripping as the mace passes behind you.

ff_bulgarian_bag_spin.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag by the main handles with feet about shoulder width. Swing it across the body, whip one arm around the head to pass the bag behind you, then cast it down into the next circle. Shift weight with the bag and avoid rushing before the arc is smooth.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_spin.description: Grip the Bulgarian bag main handles and start with a lateral swing. Spin the bag around the shoulders in one direction, reset through the front, then alternate to the other direction. Keep the bag close to the body and avoid losing posture as the direction changes.

ff_double_kettlebell_feet_elevated_reneg.description: Place two kettlebells under the shoulders and elevate the feet on a box or bench. Hold a firm plank, row one bell toward the ribs, lower it quietly, and repeat on the other side. Keep feet wide enough to control rotation and avoid twisting the hips open.

Set up in a feet-elevated plank with hands gripping two kettlebell handles. Row each kettlebell in turn, then perform a push-up between the bells before the next cycle. Keep the handles stable and avoid sagging the hips during the push-up.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_snatch_to_overh.description: Snatch one kettlebell to a locked-out overhead position. With the bell stacked over the shoulder, squat down and stand back up under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid letting the kettlebell drift forward as depth increases.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_reverse_l.description: Hold the barbell in the crooks of the elbows with hands clasped and chest tall. Step one leg back into a reverse lunge, return to standing, then alternate sides. Keep the elbows tight to the ribs and avoid folding forward over the bar.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_forward_l.description: Cradle the bar in the elbow bends and stand with feet under the hips. Step forward into a lunge, push back to the start, then alternate legs. Keep the bar close to the torso and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_walking_lunge.description: Hold the barbell Zercher-style in the elbow creases and step into a walking lunge. Lower both knees, drive through the front foot, and bring the back leg through into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid bouncing the bar on the arms.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_lateral_l.description: Hold the bar in the crooks of the elbows and stand tall. Step out to one side, sit into that hip while the other leg stays long, then push back and alternate. Keep the bar close and avoid turning the toes or torso excessively.

ff_slider_barbell_zercher_lateral_lunge.description: Cradle the barbell in a Zercher hold and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep elbows tucked and avoid letting the bar drag the chest downward.

ff_slider_barbell_zercher_reverse_lunge.description: Hold the bar Zercher-style and place one foot on a slider behind you. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid rounding around the bar.

ff_suspension_pelican_curl.description: Set suspension handles near chest height and stand between them with palms up. Lean forward as the arms extend behind the body, then curl yourself back by bending the elbows. Keep the body stiff and avoid using a hip snap to return.

ff_bar_archer_pull_up.description: Hang from a pull-up bar with a wide overhand grip. Pull mostly toward one hand while the other arm stays straighter, then lower and repeat to the other side. Keep the chest facing the bar and avoid kicking the legs for height.

ff_bar_false_grip_pull_up.description: Wrap the wrists high over the pull-up bar in a false grip before hanging. Pull the chest toward the bar while keeping the wrists set, then lower slowly. Avoid letting the grip roll back into the fingers during the descent.

ff_bar_l_sit_chin_up.description: Hang from the bar with palms facing you and raise straight legs into an L-sit. Pull the chin over the bar, then lower until the arms are straight while the legs stay lifted. Keep the movement strict and avoid swinging the heels.

ff_bar_l_sit_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with an overhand grip and legs straight out in an L-sit. Pull the chest toward the bar, then lower to a full hang without dropping the legs. Keep ribs tucked and avoid using a kip to finish.

ff_bar_tuck_back_lever_with_alternating_.description: Hang from the bar, invert, and lower into a tucked back lever with straight arms. Extend one leg at a time, return to the tuck, then alternate sides. Push the bar down and avoid letting the hips sag as the leg reaches.

ff_bar_tuck_front_lever_with_alternating.description: Hang from the bar and pull into a tucked front lever with shoulders depressed. Extend one leg at a time while the other stays tucked, then switch under control. Keep elbows straight and avoid turning the drill into a hanging leg raise.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_push_press.description: Clean the bar from the floor and receive it in a full front squat. Stand, dip through the knees, and drive the bar overhead into a push press. Keep elbows high in the squat and avoid pressing before the legs finish driving.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_overhead_press.description: Perform a squat clean from the floor and stand with the bar in the front rack. After you are steady, press the bar overhead without using a leg dip. Brace the ribs down and avoid turning the strict press into a push press.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_front_rack_squ.description: Power clean the bar to the shoulders with a shallow catch. Reset the feet, keep elbows high, and perform a front rack squat before standing tall. Keep the bar close in the clean and avoid collapsing the chest in the squat.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_front_rac.description: Start from the hang with the bar at the thighs. Power clean to the front rack, then descend into a front squat and stand. Keep the pull close from the hang and avoid dropping the elbows at the bottom of the squat.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_split_jer.description: Clean the bar from the hang to the shoulders with a power catch. Dip straight down, drive the bar up, and split the feet to receive it overhead. Recover front foot then back foot, and avoid jumping the bar forward.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Power clean the bar from the hang and stand in the front rack. Dip and drive, then re-bend the knees to catch the bar overhead in a push jerk. Keep the bar over midfoot and avoid pressing it out slowly.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_push_pres.description: From the hang, power clean the bar to the shoulders and reset the brace. Dip through the knees and drive the bar overhead with a push press. Keep the torso vertical in the dip and avoid letting the elbows drop before the drive.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_overhead_.description: Power clean the bar from the hang to the front rack. Stand still, tighten the midsection, and strict press the bar overhead. Keep glutes tight and avoid sneaking in a knee dip during the press.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_split_jer.description: Start from the hang, pull the bar close, and receive it in a full front squat. Stand, then dip and split jerk the bar overhead. Keep elbows high out of the clean and recover the split before lowering.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Hang squat clean the bar into a full front squat and stand tall. Dip, drive, and catch the bar overhead with bent knees in a push jerk. Keep the bar path vertical and avoid pressing from an unstable rack.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_push_pres.description: Clean the bar from the hang into a full squat, then stand in the front rack. Dip once and push press the bar overhead to straight arms. Keep the dip shallow and vertical instead of rolling onto the toes early.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_overhead_.description: Receive the hang clean in a full front squat and stand with the bar racked. After a full stop, strict press overhead and lower under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using rebound from the clean to start the press.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_front_rack.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack with elbows close to the ribs. Set the feet, squat between the heels, and stand while keeping the bells racked. Avoid letting the bells pull the chest down at the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_push_press.description: Clean two kettlebells into the front rack and pause to brace. Dip the knees, drive up, and press both bells overhead with help from the legs. Keep wrists straight and avoid leaning back as the bells pass the face.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_front_rack.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack and stand tall. Step forward into a lunge on one side, return, then alternate legs while keeping both bells racked. Keep elbows close and avoid losing torso height as the legs switch.

ff_double_kettlebell_glute_bridge_isomet.description: Lie on the floor or bench with two kettlebells at chest level and hips lifted in a glute bridge. Hold the bridge as you press both bells over the chest, then lower with control. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the hips sag during the press.

ff_slider_archer_push_up.description: Start in a push-up position with one hand on a slider. Slide that hand out to the side as the other elbow bends, then pull it back while pressing up. Keep the body in one line and avoid letting the sliding shoulder shrug.

ff_suspension_archer_push_up.description: Set suspension handles low and begin in a plank with hands in the handles. Lower toward one side as the opposite arm reaches out, then press back and alternate. Keep straps even and avoid rotating the hips toward the reaching arm.

ff_superband_chaos_push_up.description: Loop a thick superband between two stable anchors and place the hands on the band under the shoulders. Perform a push-up while the band shifts under the hands. Brace hard and avoid rushing, since wobble can make the elbows flare.

ff_bodyweight_pivot_split_squat.description: Start in a split stance with the rear heel lifted and hands free for balance. Pivot the hips and feet slightly as you lower into the split squat, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid twisting the knee without the foot moving.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_thrust.description: Clean one kettlebell to the front rack on one side. Squat with the elbow close, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth motion. Keep the bell close in the clean and avoid pausing with the ribs flared before the press.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_push_p.description: Clean one kettlebell into the front rack and stand steady. Dip through the knees and drive the bell overhead, finishing with the wrist stacked over the shoulder. Keep the dip vertical and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_push_j.description: Clean one kettlebell to the rack, then dip and drive it upward. Re-bend the knees to catch the bell overhead before standing fully. Keep the bell close to the midline and avoid pressing it out after a shallow catch.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_front_.description: Clean one kettlebell to the rack opposite the leg that will step forward. Step into a forward lunge, push back to standing, and keep the bell at the shoulder. Stay tall through the torso and avoid letting the load rotate you.

Clean one kettlebell to a front rack on one side. Set the feet about shoulder width, squat down, and stand while keeping the elbow close to the ribs. Keep the torso upright and avoid shifting away from the loaded side.

ff_landmine_pivot_split_squat.description: Hold the landmine sleeve at chest height and set a split stance. Pivot the feet and hips slightly as you lower into the split squat, then drive back to standing. Keep the bar close to the sternum and avoid twisting through the front knee.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_pivot_spli.description: Lock two kettlebells overhead and step into a split stance. Pivot the stance as needed, lower both knees, and stand while keeping the bells stacked. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the arms drift forward during the squat.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_snatch_to_overh.description: Snatch one kettlebell overhead and keep it on the same side as the leg that steps back. Step into a reverse lunge, return to standing, and maintain the overhead lockout. Keep the shoulder stacked and avoid leaning away from the bell.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_snatch_.description: Snatch the kettlebell from outside the foot to an overhead lockout. Step the opposite-side leg back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing under the bell. Keep hips square and avoid letting the outside pull twist the torso.

Pull the kettlebell from outside the working-side foot into an overhead snatch. Step the same-side leg back into a reverse lunge, then drive forward to stand. Keep the bell over the shoulder and avoid stepping so far back that the ribs flare.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_out.description: Place the kettlebell outside one foot and hinge toward it with one hand. Swing it across the front of the body, switch hands at the float, and let it travel outside the other foot. Keep the back long and avoid rotating faster than the hips can follow.

ff_kettlebell_half_kneeling_swing.description: Set up half kneeling with the kettlebell in front of the working-side hip. Hike the bell back beside the front leg and snap the hips to swing it forward. Keep the down-knee glute tight and avoid rocking through the low back.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_march.description: Clean the heavy sandbag onto one shoulder and stand tall. March in place or forward with controlled knee lifts while keeping the bag pinned to the shoulder. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the loaded side.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_march.description: Wrap both arms around the heavy sandbag in a bear hug and brace before moving. March with slow, deliberate steps while keeping the bag high on the torso. Avoid letting the bag slide low or rounding the upper back around it.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_roll.description: Bear-hug the heavy sandbag on the floor or against the torso and set a strong stance. Roll the bag across the body or over the shoulder as the drill requires, then re-grip before the next roll. Keep the load close and avoid twisting with a loose spine.

ff_heavy_sandbag_alternating_hang_should.description: Hold the heavy sandbag from the hang with hips hinged and arms wrapped around it. Extend the hips to clean it to one shoulder, lower to the hang, then alternate shoulders. Keep the bag close and avoid curling it with the arms alone.

ff_heavy_sandbag_hang_shoulder_clean.description: Start with the heavy sandbag hanging in front of the thighs. Drive the hips and guide the bag to one shoulder, then stand tall before lowering back to the hang. Keep the torso braced and avoid letting the bag drift away during the pull.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_horn_grip_al.description: Dead clean the kettlebell from the floor and catch it by the horns at chest height. Step back into a reverse lunge, return to standing, then alternate legs while holding the horn grip. Keep the bell close and avoid letting the front knee cave.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor between the feet. Pull from a braced hinge, guide the bell close, and catch it softly in the rack. Keep the wrist straight and avoid letting the bell swing wide before the catch.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_overhead_pre.description: Clean the kettlebell from the floor to the rack and stand still. Press it overhead to a stacked lockout, then lower to the rack before returning to the floor if needed. Keep the ribs down and avoid using a back lean to finish the press.

ff_clubbell_barbarian_alternating_forwar.description: Hold the clubbell in a barbarian-style position with the heavy end controlled near the shoulder line. Step forward into a lunge, return, then alternate legs while keeping the club path steady. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the club pull you into rotation.

ff_clubbell_barbarian_forward_lunge.description: Set the clubbell in the barbarian hold and brace the midsection. Step forward into a lunge on the working leg, lower both knees, and push back to the start. Keep the club close and avoid collapsing the chest toward the handle.

ff_macebell_barbarian_alternating_forwar.description: Hold the macebell in a barbarian rack with the head controlled and the handle secure. Alternate forward lunges while keeping the mace balanced through each step. Keep the front knee aligned and avoid letting the mace swing during the leg drive.

ff_macebell_barbarian_forward_lunge.description: Brace with the macebell in a barbarian-style hold before stepping forward. Lower into the lunge, drive back to standing, and keep the mace close to the body. Avoid reaching the head of the mace away from you as the front knee bends.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells locked out overhead and face a sturdy box. Place the whole foot on the box, drive up to stand, then step down with control. Keep the dumbbells stacked over the shoulders and avoid pushing off the floor leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Hold one dumbbell in the front rack opposite the stepping leg. Put the full working foot on the box, stand tall, then step down under control. Keep the loaded elbow high and avoid leaning toward the dumbbell.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Rack one dumbbell on the same side as the leg stepping onto the box. Drive through the whole foot to stand, then lower with control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid bouncing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Hold the macebell in the order position opposite the stepping leg. Step onto the box with the full foot, stand tall, and control the descent. Keep the mace vertical and avoid letting the handle pull the torso across.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Hold the macebell in order on the same side as the working step-up leg. Drive through the box foot to stand and keep the loaded shoulder packed. Avoid letting the mace head drift as you step down.

ff_macebell_order_step_up.description: Secure the macebell in an order hold at the shoulder and face the box. Step up with one leg, stand fully, then return to the floor with control. Keep the mace close and avoid turning the torso to balance it.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_step_up.description: Hold the macebell in the order position and alternate which foot steps onto the box. Stand tall at the top of each rep before stepping down. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid rushing the foot change.

ff_barbell_zercher_step_up.description: Cradle the barbell in the crooks of the elbows and face a box. Place the whole foot on the box, drive up to standing, then step down. Keep elbows tight and avoid folding over the bar as you rise.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_step_up.description: Hold the bar Zercher-style with chest tall and alternate step-ups on a box. Stand fully on the box before returning each foot to the floor. Keep the bar pinned to the torso and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_step_up.description: Set the Bulgarian bag across the upper back and hold the handles securely. Step onto the box with the full foot, stand tall, then lower under control. Keep the chest lifted and avoid letting the bag roll up the neck.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_s.description: Hold the Bulgarian bag on the back rack and alternate legs on the box. Drive through the top foot each rep and stand fully before stepping down. Keep the bag centered and avoid wobbling the knees inward.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_step_up.description: Place the sandbag across the upper back and face a sturdy step. Step up with the whole foot, stand tall, and lower with control. Keep the torso upright and avoid bouncing the sandbag on the shoulders.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_alternating_step_up.description: Hold the sandbag in a back rack and alternate step-ups on the box. Stand fully at the top of each rep while keeping the bag balanced across the shoulders. Avoid pushing off the floor leg to make the step easier.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_step_up.description: Bear-hug or front-rack the sandbag high on the chest and face the box. Step up through the full foot, stand tall, then step down slowly. Keep the bag close and avoid rounding forward around it.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_step_u.description: Hold the sandbag in the front rack and alternate which leg steps onto the box. Drive through the top foot and keep the torso tall at the top. Avoid letting the bag slide down as fatigue builds.

ff_plate_overhead_step_up.description: Hold a weight plate locked out overhead with both hands and face the box. Step up to full standing, then lower with control before repeating. Keep the plate over the shoulders and avoid flaring the ribs to hold it up.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_step_up.description: Press a plate overhead and alternate step-ups on a sturdy box. Stand tall on each rep while the arms remain straight, then step down softly. Keep the plate stacked and avoid letting it drift forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack and face the box. Alternate step-ups while keeping elbows close to the ribs and bells parked at the shoulders. Keep the working knee over the toes and avoid rounding under the rack.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and alternate step-ups. Move slowly enough to keep each bell vertical through the ascent and descent. Keep wrists stacked and avoid using a high box that makes the bells tip.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Lock two kettlebells overhead and face the box. Alternate step-ups with controlled foot placement and a full stand at the top. Keep arms stacked over shoulders and avoid leaning back as the stepping leg drives.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and alternate step-ups on a low, stable box. Step and lower slowly so the bells stay vertical. Keep the ribs down and avoid chasing height at the expense of wrist control.

ff_suspension_alternating_hawaiian_squat.description: Hold the suspension handles and stand on one leg with the other leg wrapped behind the stance calf. Sit into the Hawaiian squat, use the straps only as needed, then stand and alternate sides. Keep the stance knee tracking over the toes and avoid pulling yourself up with the arms.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Hold a kettlebell at each side in a suitcase carry and face the box. Alternate step-ups, standing tall before each controlled descent. Keep shoulders level and avoid letting the bells swing as the feet switch.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_step_up.description: Hold one kettlebell in a goblet position at the chest and face the box. Step up through the full foot, stand tall, then lower with control. Keep the elbows tucked and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_step_up.description: Keep a kettlebell in a goblet hold while alternating legs on the box. Drive through the top foot and pause briefly at full standing. Keep the bell close to the sternum and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_step_up.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically in a goblet hold at chest height. Place the full foot on the box, stand tall, and step down under control. Keep the dumbbell close and avoid leaning forward to start the rep.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_step_up.description: Hold a dumbbell goblet-style and alternate step-ups on a sturdy box. Stand fully on the box before switching legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid using the floor foot as a spring.

ff_tire_russian_step_up.description: Stand beside or in front of a tire and place one full foot on the top surface. Drive up to stand on the tire, then step down with control. Keep the knee over the toes and avoid landing on the unstable tire edge.

ff_tire_alternating_russian_step_up.description: Use the tire as the step and alternate which foot leads each rep. Plant the whole foot on the tire, stand tall, and step down softly. Keep your eyes forward and avoid bouncing off the tire's rebound.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_seated_good_morni.description: Sit tall on a bench and crush one dumbbell between both palms at the chest. Hinge forward from the hips, then return to upright without losing the grip. Keep the spine long and avoid rounding to reach lower.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_z_press.description: Sit on the floor inside a trap bar with legs straight and the bar balanced in a bottoms-up orientation if your setup allows it. Press from shoulder height to overhead, then lower under control. Keep the torso vertical and avoid leaning back to turn it into an incline press.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_step_up.description: Hold one kettlebell by the horns at chest height and face the box. Step up through the full foot, stand tall, then lower under control. Keep wrists neutral and avoid letting the bell pull the chest forward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_step.description: Keep the kettlebell in a horn grip at the chest while alternating step-ups. Stand fully on the box before changing legs and step down softly. Keep elbows close and avoid twisting around the bell.

ff_superband_assisted_reverse_nordic.description: Anchor a superband in front of you and hold it while kneeling tall on a pad. Lean backward from the knees with hips extended, then use the quads and band assistance to return upright. Keep a straight line from knees to shoulders and avoid bending at the hips.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_mill_to_reverse_m.description: Hold one clubbell in an order position and perform a mill by casting it across the body and around the shoulder. Reverse the elbow circle into a reverse mill before returning to order. Keep the movement driven by the elbow path and avoid flicking the club with the wrist.

ff_single_arm_macebell_mill_to_reverse_m.description: Grip the mace with one hand and guide it through a mill path around the shoulder and behind the body. Change direction into a reverse mill and bring it back to the start with control. Keep the mace close and avoid letting the head pull you off balance.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_mill.description: Perform a single-arm macebell mill on one side, return to a controlled front position, then switch hands or sides. Let the elbow guide the circle rather than the wrist. Keep the torso braced and avoid speeding up before the hand change is clean.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_hang_power_clean.description: Hold one dumbbell at the thigh and hinge into a hang position. Extend the hips quickly and guide the dumbbell to the shoulder in a shallow catch. Keep the dumbbell close and avoid curling it slowly with the arm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_hang_.description: Start with one dumbbell at the hang and clean it to the shoulder on one side. Lower to the hang, switch hands or sides, and repeat with the same hip drive. Reset the brace before each side and avoid twisting through the catch.
