lateral_plyo_push_up.description: From a push-up position, lower the chest and explosively push up so the hands leave the floor and the body travels laterally. Land softly with the hands under the shoulders and reset. Keep the body in one line through the jump.

lateral_pogo_jumps.description: Hop laterally on stiff ankles with minimal knee bend, spending little time on the ground. Keep the jumps quick and the torso tall over the feet. Use the arms for rhythm and avoid drifting off line.

lateral_power_shuffle.description: Stay low in an athletic stance and shuffle laterally with powerful, short steps for the prescribed distance. Push the ground away with the outside foot on each step. Keep the chest over the toes and eyes up.

lateral_push_offs.description: Stand sideways to a wall or post and push off laterally with one foot, driving the body away explosively. Absorb the landing on the opposite foot with a soft knee. Reset and repeat, keeping the push leg fully extended at takeoff.

lateral_raise_2.description: Stand with dumbbells at the sides and raise the arms out to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend. Lead with the elbows and pause briefly at the top before lowering. Avoid shrugging or swinging the weights up.

lateral_short_shuttle.description: Sprint a short lateral shuttle between cones, touching the line and changing direction sharply each rep. Stay low on the turns and push off the outside leg. Keep the feet under the hips on every stop.

lateral_shuffle.description: Athletic stance, shuffle laterally without crossing the feet for the prescribed distance or time. Stay on the balls of the feet with the hips low and chest up. Avoid bouncing upright between steps.

lateral_shuffle_mirror_drill.description: Mirror a partner or coach with lateral shuffles, matching their direction changes on cue. Keep the hips low and the feet wide on every shuffle. React without crossing the feet or stutter stepping.

lateral_shuffle_to_ball_drop.description: Shuffle laterally and react to a dropped ball by sprinting to catch or retrieve it. Stay low in the shuffle and explode on the reaction step. Keep the chest over the feet when changing to a linear sprint.

lateral_shuffle_to_react_sprint.description: Perform lateral shuffles and on a visual cue break into a linear sprint in the prescribed direction. Stay athletic through the transition without rising upright first. Push off the outside foot on the shuffle and drive the arms on the sprint.

lateral_shuffle_to_sprint.description: Shuffle laterally for a set distance, then open into a forward sprint through a cone or line. Keep the transition sharp with one aggressive crossover step. Avoid drifting upright before the sprint phase.

lateral_shuffle_with_ball_react_facing_o.description: Shuffle laterally facing or with your back to a wall while reacting to a thrown or bounced ball. Stay low and catch or deflect the ball without crossing the feet. Reset quickly between reps.

lateral_sled_march.description: Attach a strap to a sled and march laterally with short, deliberate steps while keeping tension on the strap. Stay low in the hips and push the ground away with the outside leg. Keep the torso square and avoid leaning away from the sled.

lateral_squat_jump.description: Sit into a quarter squat and jump laterally, landing softly on both feet with knees bent. Reset or immediately bound back as prescribed. Keep the chest up and avoid collapsing inward on landing.

lateral_wall_drill.description: Lean into a wall at a sprint angle with one knee up and drive the legs in a fast marching rhythm. Keep the ankle stiff and the hips level under the shoulders. Avoid leaning back or letting the foot land behind the hip.

leg_lifts.description: Lie on your back with legs straight and hands under the hips for support if needed. Lift both legs toward the ceiling while keeping the low back pressed down, then lower slowly. Stop before the back arches off the floor.

levator_stretch.description: Sit tall and turn the head 45° toward one armpit, then gently nod the chin down to stretch the back of the neck. Hold a mild tension and breathe steadily. Avoid forcing the head with the hand.

line_hops.description: Hop forward and back or side to side over a line with quick, springy foot contacts. Land softly on the balls of the feet with knees slightly bent. Keep the torso stable and avoid letting the feet drift wide.

linear.description: Sprint or run linearly for the prescribed distance with tall posture and aggressive arm drive. Strike under the hips on the balls of the feet and stay relaxed through the shoulders. Build speed gradually unless the session calls for maximal intent.

linear_alternating_pogos.description: Alternate single-leg pogo hops forward with stiff ankles and minimal knee bend. Spend little time on the ground and keep the torso tall. Use the arms for rhythm and stay on line.

linear_pogo_jumps.description: Hop forward on two feet with quick, stiff-ankle contacts and minimal knee bend. Keep the jumps low and fast rather than high and slow. Stay tall through the chest and land under the hips.

log_press.description: Clean a log to the shoulders with a neutral grip inside the handles. Dip the knees slightly and drive the log overhead to lockout, then lower to the shoulders under control. Keep the elbows high in the rack and avoid leaning back.

low_back_pedal_walks.description: Lie on your back and alternate slow bicycle-style leg extensions while keeping the low back gently pressed down. Move only as far as you can without the back arching off the floor. Breathe steadily through each pedal.

low_band_single_arm_row.description: Anchor a band low and hinge with one handle in hand. Row the elbow toward the hip while keeping the shoulder down, then extend the arm long. Brace the core and avoid rotating the torso toward the band.

low_box_hip_switches.description: Stand beside a low box and switch the lead foot on and off at a fast cadence aimed at top-speed leg turnover. Stay relaxed through the shoulders and strike under the hips. Keep contacts quick and light.

low_hurdle_lateral_run.description: Run laterally over low hurdles with quick feet and a low hip height. Stay on the balls of the feet and avoid knocking hurdles with the trailing leg. Keep the chest over the toes through the pattern.

low_hurdle_lateral_to_linear.description: Run laterally over low hurdles, then transition into a linear sprint through the finish. Stay low on the lateral section and drive the arms on the sprint phase. Avoid rising upright before the direction change.

low_lateral_hurdle_hop.description: Hop laterally over a low hurdle on one or two feet with quick ground contacts. Land softly and reset or continue rhythmically as prescribed. Keep the knees aligned and avoid clipping the hurdle.

low_squat_jump.description: Sit into a low squat and explode upward, landing back in the squat with soft knees. Reset or repeat rhythmically without pausing upright. Keep the chest up and avoid collapsing forward on landing.

low_squat_to_split_lunge.description: Drop into a low squat and on rising split the feet into a lunge landing with both knees bent. Drive back to standing or repeat the combo as prescribed. Keep the front knee over the mid-foot on the split.

low_squat_to_vertical.description: Sit into a low squat, then explode vertically into a jump with arms driving overhead. Land softly back into the squat or on two feet as prescribed. Keep the heels down at the bottom of the squat phase.

lower.description: Work through the prescribed lower-body corrective—band walks, isometric holds, or targeted activation from the session. Move slowly with ribs down and knees tracking over the toes. Stop if you feel joint pinching rather than muscle work.

lower_limb.description: Work through the prescribed lower-limb strength pattern—squat, lunge, hinge, or step—using bodyweight or loaded setup from the session. Move through a full controlled range and pause briefly at the hardest point. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and the torso braced.

lumbar_windshields.description: Lie on your back with arms out to the sides and legs raised toward the ceiling. Slowly sweep the legs side to side like windshield wipers while keeping the shoulders down. Stop before the low back lifts off the floor.

lunge_jump.description: Start in a lunge and explode upward, switching legs in the air to land in the opposite lunge. Absorb the landing with soft knees and a tall torso. Keep the front knee aligned over the foot on every landing.

lunge_with_twist.description: Step into a forward or reverse lunge and rotate the torso over the front leg with a medicine ball or arms extended. Return to center and push back to standing. Keep the front knee stable and rotate from the mid-back.

lying_pass_through.description: Lie on your back with a stick or band held wide overhead and pass it toward the hips as far as shoulder mobility allows. Return overhead with control and keep the low back gently flat. Reduce grip width if the back arches.

max_velo_alternating_bounds.description: Alternate single-leg bounds for distance with maximal horizontal velocity intent. Push the ground away aggressively and spend minimal time on contact. Stay tall and avoid overstriding on each bound.

mb_toss_starts_2_point.description: Start in a two-point stance holding a medicine ball, then drive into a sprint or shuffle on cue while executing the prescribed toss. Stay low through the first steps and follow through with the throw. Keep the chest over the toes on takeoff.

mckenzie_press_up.description: Lie face-down with hands under the shoulders and press the upper body up while keeping the hips on the floor. Extend only as far as symptoms allow and lower with control. Keep the glutes relaxed and breathe steadily.

med_ball_box_jump_various_loads_and_plac.description: Hold a medicine ball at the chest or overhead as prescribed and jump onto a box with both feet. Land softly with the whole foot on the box and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the ball tight to the body on takeoff.

med_ball_broad_jumps.description: Hold a medicine ball at the chest, dip through the hips, and bound forward for distance. Release or keep the ball as prescribed while staying tall through the landing. Absorb with bent knees and avoid leaning back on takeoff.

med_ball_half_kneeling_vertical_toss.description: Half-kneel with a medicine ball at the chest and throw it vertically to a target or partner. Drive from the hips and extend through the arms without leaning back. Catch or reset the ball between reps.

med_ball_slam.description: Stand with feet hip-width holding a medicine ball overhead. Slam the ball into the floor by hinging at the hips and driving the arms down sharply. Pick up or catch the rebound and keep the core braced on every rep.

med_ball_underhand_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball at the hips and toss it underhand to a partner or wall using hip and leg drive. Follow through with the arms and stay tall through the chest. Avoid leaning back before release.

med_ball_vertical_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball at the chest, dip through the legs, and throw it vertically overhead to a target. Extend fully without arching the low back and absorb the catch at the chest. Reset the feet between reps if balance drifts.

medium_level_alternating_bound.description: Alternate lateral or linear bounds at a medium box or hurdle height with controlled landings. Stick each landing briefly before the next takeoff. Keep the chest up and knees aligned over the toes.

mid_split_sweep.description: Sit in a middle split or wide straddle and sweep the torso side to side over each leg with a long spine. Move slowly into a mild stretch and breathe at end range. Avoid forcing the hips or rounding the back.

mini_hurdle_hops.description: Hop forward over mini hurdles with quick, springy contacts and minimal knee bend. Stay on the balls of the feet and keep the torso tall. Avoid clipping hurdles or drifting off line.

mountain_climber_starts_2.description: Begin in a mountain-climber or sprinter start and explode into the first acceleration steps on cue. Drive the knees and arms aggressively while staying low. Keep the shin angle aggressive without stumbling forward.

mountain_climbers.description: Start in a high plank and drive one knee toward the chest, then quickly switch legs in a running rhythm. Keep the shoulders over the hands and the hips level. Avoid bouncing the feet or letting the hips rise.

neck_cars.description: Sit or stand tall and trace slow controlled neck circles through flexion, side bend, extension, and rotation. Stay in a mild range with smooth tension and steady breathing. Stop if you feel dizziness or pinching.

neck_rotation.description: Sit tall and slowly turn the head to look over one shoulder, then return to center and repeat to the other side. Keep the shoulders relaxed and the chin level. Move only through a comfortable range.

neutral_grip_pull_up.description: Hang from parallel handles with a neutral grip and arms fully extended. Pull the chest toward the hands by driving the elbows down, then lower until the arms are long. Avoid kipping or shrugging at the top.

one_arm_row.description: Hinge with one hand on a bench and a dumbbell or kettlebell in the other hand hanging straight down. Row the weight toward the hip while keeping the elbow close, then lower until the arm is long. Keep the back flat and avoid rotating the torso.

openhagen_plank_bent_knee_or_straight_le.description: Side-plank with the top leg on a bench and the bottom leg bent or straight as prescribed. Lift the hips to align the body and hold while squeezing the inner-thigh of the top leg into the bench. Keep the shoulders stacked and avoid sagging at the waist.

oscillatory_low_squat_jumps.description: Stay in a low squat stance and perform small rapid jumps without fully standing between reps. Keep the chest up and land softly with knees tracking over the toes. Avoid drifting forward onto the toes.

oscillatory_split_squat.description: Hold the bottom of a split squat and perform small pulsing up-and-down reps through a short range. Keep the front knee over the mid-foot and the torso tall. Stop if the front heel lifts off the floor.

overcoming_iso_bb_split_squat.description: Set a barbell in a rack at the bottom of your split squat and push upward into the pins without moving the bar. Drive through the front foot and keep the torso tall during the hold. Breathe steadily and avoid letting the front knee cave.

overcoming_iso_bench_press.description: Set the bar on pins at the bottom of your bench press range and push upward into the pins without moving the bar. Keep the shoulder blades pinched and the feet driving into the floor. Hold steady tension without flaring the elbows.

overcoming_iso_trap_bar_split_lunge.description: Stand inside the trap bar in a split stance with the bar pinned at knee height and push upward into the fixed bar. Keep the front shin near vertical and the chest tall through the hold. Drive evenly through the front foot.

overcoming_isometric_z_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended and press a barbell or dumbbells overhead into fixed pins without moving the weight. Keep the torso vertical and the ribs down during the hold. Avoid leaning back to cheat tension.

overhead_band_lat_stretch.description: Anchor a band overhead and grab it with one arm, then sit the hips to the side to stretch the lat. Keep the ribs down and the arm long without shrugging. Breathe into the stretch and reduce tension if the shoulder pinches.

overhead_cable_extension.description: Face away from a high cable with a rope behind the head and elbows bent. Extend the elbows to straighten the arms overhead, then return with control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid arching the low back.

overhead_db_extension.description: Stand or sit with one or two dumbbells held overhead and elbows pointing forward. Lower the weight behind the head by bending the elbows, then extend back to lockout. Keep the ribs down and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

overhead_lunge.description: Press a barbell or dumbbells overhead and step into a forward or reverse lunge while keeping the arms locked out. Push back to standing without losing stack over the shoulders. Keep the front knee aligned over the foot.

overhead_pin_press.description: Set the bar on pins at forehead or chin height in a standing or seated position. Press from the pins to lockout, then lower back to the pins under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back off the pins.

overhead_press.description: Stand with a barbell or dumbbells at shoulder height and feet hip-width. Brace the core and press overhead to lockout without leaning back, then lower under control. Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows at the start.

overhead_squats.description: Hold a barbell overhead with a wide grip and arms locked while squatting between the feet. Keep the bar stacked over the mid-foot and the chest upright at depth. Drive through the whole foot to stand without letting the elbows bend.

pad_cue.description: React to a pad or target cue in an athletic stance—touch, sprint, shuffle, or change direction as prescribed. Stay on the balls of the feet with the hips low through the first step. Keep the chest over the toes on every break.

pallof_press.description: Stand sideways to a cable or band anchor with the handle at the sternum. Press the handle straight out until the arms are long, hold briefly, then return without rotating. Keep the hips square and ribs down throughout.

pallof_press_walkouts.description: Hold a Pallof press with arms extended and take small steps away from and back toward the anchor. Resist rotation through the torso on every step. Keep the handle at sternum height and the core braced.

pallof_press_with_twist.description: Press the handle out from a Pallof setup, then rotate the torso slightly away from the anchor and return. Keep the hips facing forward and move through the mid-back. Do not let the band pull the shoulders out of alignment.

partner_lean_start.description: Lean forward into a partner's hands from a two-point start and on release drive into a sprint. Stay low through the first steps with aggressive arm action. Keep the shin angle forward without stumbling on release.

pec_minor_stretch.description: Stand in a doorway with the forearm on the frame and the elbow at shoulder height. Gently rotate the torso away until you feel a stretch across the chest and front shoulder. Keep the ribs down and avoid shrugging.

pendlay_row.description: Hinge with a flat back over a barbell on the floor and row it to the lower ribs, then lower it back to the floor each rep. Keep the torso parallel to the floor and the bar path straight. Avoid jerking with the hips.

pigeon_pose.description: From a push-up position, bring one knee forward behind the wrist and extend the other leg back. Sink the hips toward the floor while keeping the front shin angled comfortably. Breathe into the hip and avoid forcing the knee.

pike_crunches.description: Start in a pike position with hips high and hands on the floor or bench. Pull the knees toward the chest by rounding the spine, then extend back to the pike. Keep the shoulders stable and avoid collapsing in the wrists.

pike_push_up_feet_elevated.description: Place the feet on a box and the hands on the floor in a pike position. Lower the head toward the floor between the hands and press back up with control. Keep the hips stacked over the shoulders as much as mobility allows.

piston_run.description: Run in place or forward with short, piston-like strides emphasizing rapid leg turnover. Stay tall through the chest and strike under the hips. Keep the arms compact and synced with the legs.

plank_bird_dogs.description: From a high plank, extend the opposite arm and leg while keeping the hips level. Return under control and alternate sides. Avoid rotating or letting the lower back sag.

plank_kettlebell_drag_through.description: Hold a high plank with a kettlebell outside one hand. Reach under the body with the opposite hand to drag the bell through to the other side, then switch. Keep the hips square and minimize rocking.

plank_pressups.description: Start in a forearm plank and press up to a high plank one hand at a time, then return to the forearms. Keep the hips stable and avoid swaying side to side. Alternate the leading hand each rep if prescribed.

plank_shoulder_taps.description: Hold a high plank and tap one shoulder with the opposite hand, then alternate with minimal hip shift. Keep the feet wide enough for stability and the core braced. Avoid rocking the pelvis on each tap.

plank_walkouts.description: From standing, hinge to place the hands on the floor and walk out to a plank, then walk back and stand. Keep the legs straight as mobility allows and the core braced in the plank. Avoid sagging at the farthest point.

plantar_dorsi_flexion.description: Sit or stand and alternately point the toes down into plantar flexion and pull them up into dorsiflexion through a full comfortable range. Move slowly and pause briefly at each end. Keep the ankle aligned without rolling inward.

pogo_jump.description: Hop on two feet with stiff ankles and minimal knee bend, spending little time on the ground. Keep jumps low and quick with the torso tall. Use the arms for rhythm and land under the hips.

posterior_chain.description: Work the prescribed posterior-chain pattern—hinge, bridge, or hip extension—with bodyweight or loaded setup from the session. Hinge at the hips with a long spine or drive through the heels to extend the hips. Keep the ribs down and avoid rounding the back on the way down.

power_carioca.description: Perform the carioca drill with quick, powerful crossover steps facing sideways down the lane. Stay on the balls of the feet with the hips low and chest up. Keep the steps crisp without crossing too far behind.

power_clean.description: Start with the bar at hang height and drive the hips to pull the bar to the shoulders in one motion, catching in a front rack. Keep the bar close and the elbows high at the catch. Reset each rep if doing hang cleans from the floor.

power_jerk.description: Clean or rack the bar at the shoulders, dip the knees, and drive the bar overhead while splitting or power-jumping the feet. Lock out with the bar stacked over the shoulders and hips. Recover the feet under the bar before lowering.

power_shuffle_to_sprint.description: Begin with a lateral power shuffle, then open into a forward sprint through a cone or line. Stay low through the shuffle and drive the arms on the sprint transition. Avoid rising upright before changing direction.

preacher_curl.description: Sit at a preacher bench with the upper arms on the pad and curl the weight toward the shoulders. Lower until the elbows are nearly straight without locking aggressively. Keep the shoulders down and avoid lifting off the pad.

pro_shuttle.description: Run the pro-agility shuttle: sprint 5 yards to one line, cut 10 yards to the opposite line, then finish 5 yards back through the start. Touch each line with the hand and stay low on every cut. Push off the outside foot on direction changes.

prone_arm_circles.description: Lie face-down with arms extended to the sides and trace small slow circles with the thumbs up. Keep the forehead down and the shoulder blades engaged. Reduce circle size if the lower back arches.

prone_cobra.description: Lie face-down with arms at the sides and lift the chest slightly by extending the spine and squeezing the shoulder blades. Keep the gaze down and the glutes lightly engaged. Avoid cranking the neck to look forward.

prone_cobra_holds.description: Set the prone cobra position with the chest lifted and arms back, then hold isometrically while breathing steadily. Keep the ribs on the floor and the neck neutral. Stop if you feel pinching in the low back.

prone_external_rotations.description: Lie face-down with elbows bent to 90° and arms out to the sides. Rotate the forearms upward toward the ceiling, then lower with control. Keep the forehead down and avoid shrugging.

prone_itys.description: Lie face-down and lift the arms into I, T, or Y shapes as prescribed, squeezing the upper back at the top. Lower slowly without shrugging toward the ears. Keep the forehead down and the ribs on the floor.

prone_thoracic_rotation_with_dowel.description: Lie face-down with a dowel on the upper back and perform slow dynamic thoracic rotations side to side through a controlled range. Keep the hips pinned and the neck neutral. Reduce range if the low back picks up the motion.

prowler.description: Grip the high or low handles of a loaded sled and drive the legs in short powerful steps for the prescribed distance. Lean into the handles with a braced core and keep the back flat. Avoid rounding the shoulders or short choppy steps that stall the sled.

pull.description: Perform the prescribed vertical or horizontal pull—pull-up, pulldown, or row variant from the session. Drive the elbows down and back to bring the load toward the torso, then return with control. Keep the chest up and avoid shrugging at the top.

pull_ups_2.description: Hang from the bar with an overhand grip slightly outside shoulder width. Pull the chest toward the bar by driving the elbows down, then lower until the arms are fully extended. Keep the shoulders active at the bottom and avoid kipping.

pulsing_hurdle_hops.description: Hop rhythmically over a hurdle or marker with quick springy contacts, pulsing through repeated reps without pausing. Stay on the balls of the feet and keep the torso tall. Avoid clipping the hurdle or slowing between hops.

pulsing_pogos.description: Perform continuous low pogo hops with stiff ankles and rapid ground contacts. Keep the jumps small and fast rather than high. Stay tall through the chest and land under the hips.

push.description: Perform the prescribed upper-body push—press, push-up, or dip variant from the session. Lower under control and press or push to lockout while keeping the core braced. Avoid flaring the elbows or losing stack over the shoulders.

push_up_plus.description: Start in a high plank with hands under the shoulders and the body in one line. At the top, protract the shoulder blades by pushing the floor away without bending the elbows. Hold briefly, then allow the shoulder blades to retract before the next rep.

push_up_start.description: Begin in a push-up start position with hands under the shoulders and hips low. On the cue, drive one knee forward and sprint out of the position with aggressive arm action. Keep the back flat at the start and avoid popping the hips up first.

push_ups.description: Place the hands slightly outside shoulder width and walk the feet back into a straight line from head to heels. Lower the chest toward the floor with elbows at about 45°, then press back up while keeping the core braced. Avoid sagging hips or flaring the elbows straight out to the sides.

quadruped_arm_circles.description: Start on hands and knees with a neutral spine and one arm reaching forward. Trace slow controlled circles with the arm while keeping the trunk still and the shoulder blade engaged. Reduce circle size if the lower back rotates or the ribs flare.

quadruped_extension_rotation.description: From hands and knees, flow through quadruped extension and thoracic rotation side to side at a smooth tempo. Reach long through the arm and leg before threading the arm under and opening up. Keep the movement from the mid-back and avoid rocking the pelvis.

quadruped_single_arm_db_drop_catch.description: Hold a quadruped position with one hand on a dumbbell and the opposite arm reaching forward. Release the bell briefly under control and catch it before it rolls, keeping the hips level throughout. Brace the core so the trunk does not rotate when the hand leaves the weight.

quadruped_t_spine_pull_through.description: On hands and knees, thread one arm under the body and reach through while rotating the thoracic spine. Follow by opening that arm to the ceiling in a pull-through arc. Keep the hips square and move from the mid-back rather than the lumbar spine.

quadruped_thoracic_band_row.description: Loop a band around a post at floor height and hold it from a quadruped position. Row the band toward the ribs while rotating the thoracic spine open, then return with control. Keep the supporting arm stacked and avoid shifting weight onto the knees unevenly.

quarter_arc.description: Start in an athletic stance and sprint through a quarter-circle arc around a cone or marker. Lean into the curve, drive the outside leg, and stay on the balls of the feet. Keep the chest up and avoid cutting the turn too tight and losing speed.

rack_pulls.description: Set a barbell on rack pins just below the knee and grip it with a flat back and braced core. Drive through the floor to stand tall, squeezing the glutes at lockout without leaning back. Keep the bar close to the legs and avoid rounding the upper back off the pins.

reaction_skater_jumps.description: Begin in an athletic stance facing a partner or visual cue. On the signal, bound laterally onto one leg, absorb softly, and explode back the other direction. Stay low through the hips and avoid letting the knee collapse inward on landing.

reactive_side_toss.description: Stand sideways a few feet from a wall with a medicine ball at the hip. Rotate through the hips and chest to throw the ball into the wall, then catch the rebound and repeat rhythmically. Drive from the back foot and keep the front shoulder closed until the throw.

reactive_single_arm_broad_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball in one hand at the chest in an athletic stance. Step and throw the ball forward for distance with a full hip and shoulder rotation, then reset for the next rep. Keep the ribs down and avoid throwing with arm-only effort.

reactive_trap_bar_jumps.description: Stand inside a lightly loaded trap bar with feet hip-width and a tall posture. Dip quickly and jump vertically, extending the hips and ankles while keeping the core tight. Land softly with bent knees and reset before the next jump.

rear_delt_raises.description: Hinge forward at the hips with a flat back and let dumbbells hang beneath the shoulders. Raise the arms out to the sides with a slight elbow bend until they align with the torso. Lead with the elbows and avoid shrugging the weights up with the traps.

rebound_box_jump.description: Stand facing a plyo box and drop into a quarter squat before jumping onto the box. Step or jump down and immediately rebound into the next jump with minimal ground contact. Land softly on the box with the whole foot and avoid scraping the shins.

rebound_broad_jumps.description: Start in an athletic stance and swing the arms to jump forward for distance. Absorb the landing with bent knees and hips back, then immediately spring into the next broad jump. Stay tall through the chest and avoid collapsing forward on contact.

rebound_broad_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball at the chest and perform a broad jump while releasing the ball forward at takeoff. Chase the throw, reset quickly, and repeat with continuous rhythm. Time the release with hip extension rather than throwing late with the arms alone.

rebound_chest_pass.description: Stand facing a wall or partner with a medicine ball at the chest. Pass the ball explosively from the chest, catch the rebound, and immediately fire the next pass. Keep the feet grounded and use a short hip dip to generate power.

rebound_chin_up.description: Hang from a bar with an underhand grip and pull until the chin clears the bar. Lower under control and immediately pull again with minimal pause at the bottom. Keep the shoulders engaged and avoid kipping or swinging the legs.

rebound_lateral_lunge.description: Step wide to one side and sit the hips back into a lateral lunge with the trailing leg straight. Drive off the bent leg to return to center and flow directly into the opposite side. Keep the chest up and the bent knee tracking over the foot.

rebound_mb_chest_pass.description: Hold a medicine ball at chest height facing a wall or partner. Pass the ball forcefully, absorb the rebound, and chain reps with quick transitions. Stay in an athletic stance and avoid leaning back as you catch.

rebound_mb_side_toss.description: Stand sideways to a wall with a medicine ball at the hip. Rotate through the trunk and throw the ball into the wall, catch the rebound, and repeat in rhythm. Pivot on the back foot and keep the arms long through the release.

rebound_mb_soccer_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball and throw it underhand against a wall or to a partner like a soccer throw-in. Follow through with hip rotation and keep both feet grounded until release. Catch cleanly and flow into the next toss without pausing.

rebound_underhand_toss.description: Stand in an athletic stance with a medicine ball at waist height. Toss the ball underhand to a wall or partner using leg drive and a scooping arm path. Absorb the return and rebound quickly into the next rep.

rebound_underhand_vertical_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball at the hips and toss it vertically underhand to a target overhead. Extend the ankles, knees, and hips together and catch or receive the ball on the way down. Keep the toss in a straight line and avoid arching the lower back.

rebound_vertical_jump.description: Start in an athletic stance and dip quickly before jumping straight up as high as possible. Land softly on both feet with hips back and immediately rebound into the next jump. Use a quick arm swing and avoid pausing in a deep squat between reps.

renegade_rows.description: Start in a high plank with a dumbbell under each hand and feet wide for balance. Row one bell toward the hip while fighting rotation, then alternate sides. Keep the hips level and avoid twisting the torso or sagging between reps.

resisted_and_assisted_lunge_jump.description: Attach a band for resistance or assistance and start in a split stance. Drop into a lunge and explode upward, switching legs in the air if prescribed. Land softly in the next lunge with the front knee over the foot.

resisted_and_assisted_pogo_jump.description: Loop a band for assistance or resistance and perform rapid pogo hops with stiff ankles. Keep jumps low and contacts quick, landing under the hips. Stay tall through the chest and avoid letting the knees buckle inward.

resisted_and_assisted_squat_jump.description: With a band providing assistance or resistance, sit into a squat and jump vertically. Extend fully at takeoff and land softly with hips back before the next rep. Keep the chest up and avoid collapsing forward on landing.

resisted_broad_jumps.description: Attach a resistance band around the hips or waist and perform consecutive broad jumps forward. Drive the knees and arms aggressively against the band tension. Finish each landing balanced before launching into the next jump.

resisted_crossover_run.description: Anchor a band or use a sled for resistance and sprint laterally with crossover steps. Stay low, push off the outside foot, and drive the knees through the band tension. Keep the shoulders square to the direction of travel.

resisted_ground_starts.description: Begin prone or in a sprinter start with resistance attached at the waist or shoulders. On the cue, drive out of the start into a short acceleration. Keep the body low for the first few steps and avoid rising upright too early.

resisted_lateral_shuffle.description: Attach a band for resistance and shuffle laterally while staying in an athletic stance. Push off the trailing leg and keep the feet from crossing. Stay low through the hips and avoid bouncing upright between steps.

resisted_single_leg_bounds.description: With a band around the waist for resistance, bound forward on one leg for the prescribed distance. Stick each landing briefly on the same leg before pushing into the next bound. Keep the knee stable and the torso slightly forward.

resisted_skater_hops.description: Loop a band for resistance and perform lateral skater hops from leg to leg. Absorb each landing softly and explode back the other direction against the band. Stay low and avoid letting the free foot touch down between hops if continuous reps are prescribed.

reverse_cable_woodchops.description: Set a cable high and stand sideways with both hands on the handle at the shoulder. Pull the handle down and across the body by rotating through the hips and thoracic spine. Control the return and keep the arms long without shrugging.

reverse_crunch.description: Lie on your back with knees bent and curl the hips off the floor toward the ribs. Lower slowly until the low back touches down before the next rep. Keep the movement from the pelvis and avoid pulling with momentum from the legs.

reverse_hyperextension.description: Lie face-down on a reverse-hyper bench with hips at the pad edge and legs hanging free. Raise the legs to hip height by squeezing the glutes and hamstrings, then lower with control. Avoid hyperextending the lumbar spine at the top.

reverse_lunge.description: Stand tall and step one foot back, lowering until both knees bend to about 90°. Drive through the front heel to return to standing and alternate or repeat on one side. Keep the torso upright and the front knee over the mid-foot.

reverse_nordics.description: Kneel with ankles secured and a straight line from knees to shoulders. Tip backward slowly as far as hamstring strength allows, then pull back to vertical using the quads and hips. Use hands on the floor lightly if needed and avoid bending at the hips.

reverse_sled_walks.description: Attach straps or hold sled handles and walk backward with short controlled steps. Stay upright, drive through the full foot, and keep tension on the sled throughout. Avoid rounding the shoulders or taking steps that are too long to control.

reverse_throws.description: Stand facing away from a wall or partner with a medicine ball at the chest or overhead. Throw the ball backward by extending the hips and arms, then turn to receive or reset. Keep the core braced and avoid arching the lower back on overhead throws.

rolling_planks.description: Start in a forearm plank and roll onto one side into a side plank without dropping the hips. Roll through front plank to the other side with control. Keep the body in one line and avoid collapsing at the shoulder of the bottom arm.

rolling_tricep_extension.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells or an EZ bar with arms extended over the chest. Bend the elbows to lower the weight toward the forehead, then extend back up. Keep the upper arms still and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

rollover_reach.description: Lie on your back and roll to one side, reaching the top arm overhead along the floor. Follow the reach with thoracic rotation while keeping the knees stacked or bent for support. Move slowly and avoid forcing range from the lower back.

romanian_deadlift.description: Stand with feet hip-width holding a barbell or dumbbells at the thighs. Push the hips back and lower the weight along the legs until you feel a hamstring stretch, keeping a flat back. Drive the hips forward to stand tall without squatting the weight down.

rope_inverted_row.description: Hold a climbing rope or thick ropes and lean back with the body straight and heels planted. Pull the chest toward the hands by driving the elbows back, then lower with control. Keep the hips aligned and avoid sagging at the bottom.

rope_pull_ups.description: Hang from a thick rope with a firm grip and pull until the chin passes hand level. Lower under control and avoid excessive swinging. Squeeze the rope hard and keep the shoulders down at the start of each rep.

rotation.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width and rotate the torso side to side through the prescribed range. Keep the hips facing forward and initiate from the thoracic spine. Move with control and avoid bouncing at end range.

rotational_low_cable_rows.description: Set a low cable and grab the handle with one or both hands in an athletic stance. Row toward the ribs while rotating slightly through the torso, then extend back with control. Keep the core braced and avoid rounding the lower back at the finish.

russian_lunges.description: Step one foot far back and across behind the front leg into a curtsy-style lunge. Drop the back knee toward the floor while keeping the front knee tracking over the foot. Drive through the front leg to return upright and alternate sides.

russian_twist.description: Sit with knees bent and lean back slightly while holding a weight or medicine ball at the chest. Rotate the torso to tap the load side to side, keeping the feet light or elevated as prescribed. Move from the ribs and avoid pulling solely with the arms.

sandbag_carry.description: Load a sandbag to bear-hug, shoulder, or Zercher position and walk the prescribed distance with tall posture. Brace the core each step and keep the shoulders level. Avoid leaning away from the load or letting the bag pull the spine into extension.

sandbag_loads.description: Start with a sandbag on the floor and lift it to the shoulder or platform using a clean or scoop technique. Keep the back flat during the pick and drive through the legs. Reset the bag under control between reps if the session calls for repeated loading.

scarecrows.description: Stand with elbows bent to 90° and upper arms parallel to the floor. Externally rotate the forearms upward, then reverse into internal rotation under control. Keep the shoulders down and avoid arching the lower back.

scorpion.description: Lie face-down with arms extended and lift one leg up and across the body toward the opposite hand. Keep the chest on the floor and rotate through the thoracic spine. Move slowly and avoid forcing the foot to touch if the hips lift off the mat.

seal_walks.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended and walk forward on the hands while keeping the hips low. Drag or lift the legs minimally and keep the chest proud. Avoid collapsing through the shoulders or letting the lower back round excessively.

seated_box_jump.description: Sit on a box or bench with feet flat and arms ready to swing. Jump up onto a taller box without using a countermovement from standing. Land softly with bent knees and step down to reset each rep.

seated_broad_toss.description: Sit on the floor or a bench with a medicine ball at the chest and feet anchored lightly. Throw the ball forward for distance by leaning back slightly and exploding through the hips and arms. Follow through without falling backward off the seat.

seated_calf_raise.description: Sit with the balls of the feet on a step and a weight across the knees if used. Raise the heels as high as possible, pause briefly, then lower until you feel a calf stretch. Keep the movement smooth and avoid bouncing at the bottom.

seated_dead_bugs.description: Sit tall with a slight posterior pelvic tilt and extend the opposite arm and leg slowly. Return to center and alternate sides while keeping the ribs down. Stop if the lower back rounds or the torso wobbles.

seated_external_rotations.description: Sit or stand with the working elbow bent to 90° and pinned to the side. Rotate the forearm outward against band or cable resistance through a controlled arc. Keep the shoulder blade set and avoid leaning away from the band.

seated_mb_overhead_toss.description: Sit tall holding a medicine ball overhead with arms extended. Brace the trunk isometrically for the prescribed hold or slow partial throws. Keep the ribs down and avoid arching the lower back as the arms fatigue.

seated_mb_twists.description: Sit with knees bent holding a medicine ball at the chest and rotate the torso side to side. Tap the ball lightly beside each hip while keeping the feet grounded. Lead with the chest and avoid swinging the arms without trunk rotation.

seated_med_ball_toss.description: Sit facing a wall or partner with a medicine ball at the chest. Toss the ball forward using a chest-pass motion and catch the return. Stay tall through the spine and use a short hip drive for power.

seated_shot_toss.description: Sit with a heavy medicine ball at one hip like a shot-put load. Rotate and push the ball forward or upward for distance without foot lift if prescribed. Drive from the back foot through the hips and keep the path smooth.

seated_shoulder_external_rotation.description: Sit with the working elbow at 90° against the side and forearm parallel to the floor. Rotate the hand upward against light resistance, then lower slowly. Keep the elbow fixed and avoid shrugging the shoulder.

seated_soccer_toss.description: Sit with a medicine ball and perform an underhand throw to a wall or partner like a soccer throw-in. Lean back slightly and follow through with both hands. Keep the feet anchored and avoid twisting only through the arms.

seated_twists.description: Sit tall with feet flat and rotate the torso side to side through a controlled range. Keep the hips facing forward and hands lightly at the chest or beside the ribs. Move smoothly and avoid jerking at end range.

short_shuttle.description: Mark a short distance of about 5–10 yards and sprint out, touch the line, and return. Stay low on the turn and drive the outside foot on the cut. Keep transitions sharp and avoid decelerating too far past the marker.

shoulders.description: Work through the prescribed shoulder mobility or activation series—band pull-aparts, wall slides, or scapular drills—from the session plan. Move slowly through comfortable range and keep the ribs down. Stop if you feel pinching rather than muscle work.

side_bends.description: Stand tall with a dumbbell or kettlebell in one hand or both hands overhead. Bend sideways toward the load side through the obliques, then return to vertical. Keep the hips facing forward and avoid rotating the chest.

side_lying_clamshells.description: Lie on one side with hips stacked and knees bent at about 45°. Keeping the feet together, lift the top knee without rolling the pelvis backward. Squeeze the glute at the top and lower slowly.

side_plank.description: Stack the feet or stagger them and support the body on one forearm with the elbow under the shoulder. Lift the hips to form a straight line from head to heels and hold. Avoid letting the hips sag or rotating the chest toward the floor.

side_plank_band_row.description: Hold a side plank with the top hand gripping a band anchored in front. Row the band toward the ribs while keeping the hips elevated and stable. Avoid twisting the trunk or dropping the hips during the pull.

side_plank_db_raise.description: Hold a side plank with a light dumbbell in the top hand at the hip. Raise the arm toward the ceiling in a controlled arc without losing hip height. Keep the neck neutral and the shoulder blade engaged.

side_plank_hip_lifts.description: Set up in a side plank and lower the hips toward the floor without touching down. Drive the bottom elbow and top foot together to lift the hips back to line. Keep the body facing sideways and avoid rolling forward.

side_slam.description: Stand sideways to a wall or open space with a medicine ball overhead. Slam the ball down and across the body by hinging at the hips and driving the arms. Catch or reset the ball and repeat on the same side before switching.

side_to_side_pull_up.description: Hang from a bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder width. Pull up while shifting slightly toward one hand at the top, then lower and alternate sides. Keep the shoulders engaged and avoid kipping.

side_toss.description: Stand sideways to a wall with a medicine ball at the hip. Rotate through the hips and chest to throw the ball into the wall, then catch and reset. Pivot on the back foot and keep the front knee soft.

single_arm_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench with one dumbbell at shoulder height and the free arm out for balance. Press to lockout over the mid-chest while keeping the shoulder blade pinned. Lower under control and avoid twisting the torso toward the working side.

single_arm_broad_toss.description: Hold a medicine ball in one hand at the chest in an athletic stance. Step and throw the ball forward for distance with full hip rotation, then reset. Keep the ribs down and drive from the back leg.

single_arm_cable_row.description: Stand or half-kneel facing a cable set at mid height and grab the handle with one hand. Pull the elbow back toward the ribs while keeping the torso still, then extend the arm long. Squeeze the shoulder blade at the finish and avoid rotating the trunk.

single_arm_db_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with one dumbbell at shoulder height and knees bent for stability. Press the bell to lockout over the chest, then lower until the upper arm lightly touches the floor. Keep the wrist stacked and avoid flaring the elbow.

single_arm_landmine_jerk.description: Stand facing the landmine with the bar at shoulder height in one hand. Dip the knees and drive the bar overhead in a push-jerk path, catching with a soft elbow lockout. Keep the core braced and avoid leaning away from the bar.

single_arm_landmine_row.description: Straddle the landmine bar and hinge forward with one hand on the end of the bar. Row the bar toward the hip by driving the elbow back, then lower with control. Keep the back flat and avoid twisting the shoulders.

single_arm_larsen_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench with legs elevated or extended off the floor and one dumbbell at shoulder height. Press to lockout while keeping the hips and shoulders pinned to the bench. Lower slowly and avoid arching the lower back to compensate.

single_arm_overhead_carry.description: Clean or press a dumbbell or kettlebell to a locked-out overhead position on one side. Walk the prescribed distance with ribs down and the biceps near the ear. Avoid leaning away from the load or letting the lower back arch.

single_arm_pendlay_row.description: Hinge forward with a flat back and rest a dumbbell on the floor between reps. Explosively row the bell to the hip, then lower it back to the floor under control. Keep the torso parallel to the floor and avoid rotating the shoulders.

single_arm_rear_delt_flyes.description: Hinge forward with a flat back and let a dumbbell hang under the shoulder. Raise the arm out to the side with a slight elbow bend until it aligns with the torso. Lead with the elbow and keep the shoulder blade stable.

single_arm_sled_push.description: Grip one sled handle with a single arm and drive forward with short powerful steps. Stay low with a braced core and keep the path straight. Avoid rotating the trunk open toward the pushing side.

single_arm_sled_row.description: Face the sled and pull one handle toward the ribs with a strong hip hinge or athletic stance. Extend the arm back out under control and keep the shoulder down. Avoid jerking the sled with a rounded upper back.

single_leg_approach_box_jump.description: Take a short approach on one leg and jump onto a box, landing softly on the same foot or both feet as prescribed. Stick the landing with the knee aligned over the foot. Reset fully before the next approach.

single_leg_approach_hurdle.description: Build speed with a few steps and hurdle over the barrier on one leg or after a single-leg takeoff as prescribed. Drive the knee up and snap the trail leg through without clipping the hurdle. Land balanced and reset for the next rep.

single_leg_bounds.description: Bound forward on one leg with long powerful contacts, covering distance with each hop. Absorb landing forces through the hip and knee on the same leg. Keep the torso slightly forward and the arms driving in opposition.

single_leg_box_jump.description: Stand on one leg facing a box and swing the arms to jump up onto it. Land softly on the same foot or both feet as prescribed and stand tall on top. Step down and reset rather than rebounding if fatigue affects landing quality.

single_leg_broad_jump.description: Balance on one leg and swing the arms to jump forward for distance. Land on the same leg with a soft knee and hip, holding balance briefly. Keep the knee tracking over the foot and avoid collapsing inward.

single_leg_broad_to_90.description: Jump forward on one leg, then immediately open the hips to land or redirect at roughly 90° as prescribed. Absorb each landing with a stable knee and upright chest. Reset between reps if direction changes become sloppy.

single_leg_broad_to_90_outside_and_insid.description: Perform a single-leg broad jump and cut outside or inside to a 90° landing on the same leg. Stay low through the cut and stick the second landing before resetting. Avoid drifting upright before changing direction.

single_leg_glute_bridge_with_leg_whip.description: Hold a single-leg hip bridge at the top and whip the free leg through a controlled arc without dropping the hips. Keep the supporting glute engaged and the ribs down. Reduce range if the pelvis tilts or the lower back arches.

single_leg_glute_bridge_with_opposite_tu.description: Bridge on one leg with the opposite knee tucked toward the chest. Hold or pulse at the top while keeping the hips level and the planted foot flat. Avoid rotating open on the supporting side.

single_leg_high_knee_mini_hurdles.description: Sprint through a line of mini hurdles on one leg with aggressive high-knee action. Stay tall and strike under the hips on each contact. Keep the ankle stiff and avoid reaching with the swing leg.

single_leg_high_knees.description: Drive one knee up rapidly while hopping on the opposite leg for the prescribed time or distance. Pump the arms in opposition and land on the ball of the foot. Stay upright and avoid leaning back.

single_leg_hurdle_hops_for_distance.description: Hop on one leg over consecutive hurdles spaced for distance, covering ground with each contact. Absorb lightly and rebound quickly while keeping the knee stable. Reset the course if landings become noisy or unbalanced.

single_leg_hurdle_hops_for_height.description: Hop on one leg vertically over hurdles set for height rather than forward spacing. Drive the knee up and snap the ankle at takeoff. Land softly on the same leg and pause briefly if needed before the next hurdle.

single_leg_hurdle_to_box_jump.description: Clear a hurdle on one leg and immediately jump onto a box with the same leg or both feet as prescribed. Stick the box landing with hips back and chest up. Step down to reset between reps.

single_leg_lateral_bounds.description: Bound laterally from one leg to the other over a distance or marker line. Absorb each landing with a soft knee and immediately push back the other direction. Stay low through the hips and keep the chest up.

single_leg_lateral_box_jump.description: Stand beside a box on one leg and jump up sideways onto the platform. Land softly and stand tall before stepping down to reset. Keep the knee from collapsing inward on takeoff and landing.

single_leg_lateral_broad.description: Balance on one leg and jump laterally for distance, landing on the same leg. Stick the landing with the hip back and knee aligned. Reset fully before the next lateral broad jump.

single_leg_lateral_double_broad_jump.description: Perform two consecutive lateral broad jumps on the same leg without an extra step between them. Stay low through the hips and drive the arms for distance. Focus on quiet landings and stable knees.

single_leg_lateral_line_hops.description: Hop on one leg back and forth over a line with quick lateral contacts. Stay on the ball of the foot and keep the hops low and fast. Avoid letting the free foot touch down between hops if continuous reps are prescribed.

single_leg_lateral_pogos.description: On one leg, perform rapid lateral pogo hops side to side over a short distance or line. Stay low through the hips and push off the inside edge of the foot. Keep contacts quick and avoid crossing the feet.

single_leg_line_hops.description: Hop on one leg forward and backward over a line with stiff ankle contacts. Keep the torso tall and land under the hip. Reduce speed if the knee wobbles on landing.

single_leg_linear_hops.description: Hop forward on one leg in a straight line for the prescribed distance or rep count. Absorb each landing briefly and push off quickly for the next hop. Keep the arms driving and the chest up.

single_leg_linear_pogos.description: Perform rapid pogo hops forward on one leg with minimal ground contact time. Keep jumps small and ankle-dominant rather than high knee lifts. Stay tall and land under the center of mass.

single_leg_low_box_push_distance.description: Place one foot on a low box and drive off it horizontally for distance, landing on the same leg. Use a powerful arm swing and keep the torso slightly forward. Reset the box foot fully between reps.

single_leg_low_box_push_off_vertical.description: Stand with one foot on a low box and explode vertically off that leg. Land softly on the same foot or as prescribed and reset on the box. Avoid pushing off with a long countermovement step.

single_leg_push_off.description: Start in a split or step stance and drive off the front leg into a jump or bound. Extend the hip, knee, and ankle together and land under control. Keep the torso tall and avoid leaning back at takeoff.

single_leg_push_off_broad_toss.description: Push off one leg into a broad jump while releasing a medicine ball forward at takeoff. Time the throw with hip extension and land balanced on the jumping leg. Reset ball and body position between reps.

single_leg_rdl.description: Stand on one leg with a soft knee and hinge forward at the hip while the free leg extends behind for balance. Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch, then drive the hip forward to stand tall. Keep the back flat and hips square.

single_leg_reach_jumps.description: Balance on one leg and jump while reaching the opposite hand toward a target or marker. Land softly on the same leg and stick balance briefly. Keep the knee stable and avoid collapsing inward on landing.

single_leg_snap_downs.description: Start tall on one leg, drop quickly into an athletic landing position, and hold briefly. Keep the chest up, hips back, and knee aligned over the foot. Reset to standing before the next snap down.

single_leg_stationary_pogos.description: Hop in place on one leg with quick stiff-ankle contacts and minimal knee bend. Stay tall through the chest and keep landings quiet. Reduce height if the knee drifts inward.

single_leg_step_over.description: Drive one knee up and step over a hurdle or cone without knocking it, landing softly on the same leg. Keep the foot dorsiflexed and the torso tall. Reset behind the obstacle between reps if needed.

single_leg_step_over_mini_hurdles.description: Sprint or march through mini hurdles on one leg, clearing each bar with a high knee step-over. Stay on the ball of the foot and keep contacts quick. Avoid swinging the leg wide around the hurdles.

single_leg_swiss_ball_hamstring_curl.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a stability ball and the other leg extended or in the air. Curl the ball toward the hips by bending the knee, then extend slowly. Keep the hips lifted and avoid dropping the pelvis on each rep.

single_leg_swiss_ball_hamstring_iso.description: Set up a single-leg hamstring curl on a stability ball and hold the midpoint isometrically with the hips up. Keep the heel pulled toward the glute and the core braced. Breathe steadily and avoid cramping by reducing angle if needed.

single_leg_trap_bar_deadlifts.description: Stand inside a trap bar on one leg with the other leg extended slightly behind for balance. Hinge and grip the handles, then drive through the planted foot to stand tall. Keep the back flat and avoid rotating the hips open.

single_leg_tuck_jump.description: Jump vertically on one leg and tuck the free knee toward the chest at the peak. Land softly on the same leg with hips back and knee aligned. Reset balance before the next tuck jump.

single_leg_vertical.description: Jump vertically as high as possible from one leg and land under control on the same foot. Use a strong arm swing and full ankle-knee-hip extension at takeoff. Stick the landing briefly before resetting.

single_leg_wall_calf_raise.description: Stand on one leg with hands on a wall for balance and the ball of the foot on a step if available. Rise onto the toes as high as possible, pause, then lower until you feel a calf stretch. Keep the knee straight and avoid bouncing.

sissy_squats.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width and rise onto the toes while leaning the torso back slightly. Bend the knees forward over the toes and lower into a deep quad stretch, then drive back up. Hold support lightly if needed and avoid collapsing at the ankles.

sit_up_soccer_toss.description: Lie on your back holding a medicine ball overhead and sit up while throwing the ball underhand to a wall or partner. Return under control to the floor between reps. Keep the feet anchored and avoid pulling with momentum from the hip flexors only.

sit_ups.description: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet anchored or free as prescribed. Curl the torso up until the shoulders leave the floor, then lower with control. Keep the chin tucked and avoid yanking the neck with the hands.

skater_jumps.description: Bound laterally from leg to leg, landing softly on each outside edge with the hip back. Reach the opposite foot behind for balance without letting it touch down if continuous reps are prescribed. Stay low and drive off the inside edge of the landing foot.

skater_jumps_with_cones_45_degree_stabil.description: Skater jump to a cone placed at a 45° angle and stick the landing for a brief stability hold. Reset and jump to the next cone with control rather than speed. Keep the knee aligned and the chest up on each stick.

skater_jumps_with_cones_45_to_90_reactiv.description: Skater jump at a 45° angle to a cone, then react to a cue and cut to 90° on the next jump. Stay low through transitions and land quietly. Avoid drifting upright before the reactive cut.

skater_jumps_with_cones_single_leg_broad.description: Perform continuous single-leg broad jumps at a 45° angle around or between cones without pausing. Absorb each landing quickly and drive into the next bound. Keep the torso slightly forward and the knee stable.

Chain single-leg broad jumps with 90° changes of direction around cones in continuous fashion. Stick each landing only long enough to redirect, then explode to the next cone. Stay low and avoid extra stutter steps.

skull_crushers.description: Lie on a bench with an EZ bar or dumbbells extended over the chest. Bend only at the elbows to lower the weight toward the forehead, then extend back up. Keep the upper arms vertical and avoid flaring the elbows wide.

sl_hip_thrust.description: Rest the upper back on a bench with one foot planted and the other leg extended. Drive through the planted heel to lift the hips to a straight line and squeeze the glute at the top. Keep the pelvis level and avoid hyperextending the lower back.

sled_march.description: Push or drag a heavier sled with slow marching steps emphasizing full hip extension on each stride. Keep the torso braced and drive through the whole foot for strength work. Avoid shortening range to move the sled faster.

sled_push.description: Grip the sled handles at hip or shoulder height and drive forward with short powerful steps. Lean into the load with a flat back and keep constant tension on the sled. Avoid rising upright too early or taking choppy steps that stall momentum.

sled_resisted_sprint.description: Attach a harness or belt to a loaded sled and sprint for the prescribed distance with aggressive knee drive. Stay low for the first steps and gradually rise while maintaining forward lean. Keep the chest up and avoid leaning back into the straps.

sleeper_internal_external_stretch.description: Lie on your side with the bottom arm at 90° and the shoulder and elbow stacked. Gently rotate the forearm down then up to stretch internal and external rotation. Move slowly and avoid forcing the wrist to the floor.

snap_downs.description: Start standing tall, drop quickly into an athletic quarter-squat landing, and hold for a beat. Keep the chest up, hips back, and knees aligned over the toes. Reset to standing tall before the next snap down.

snatch_grip_deadlift.description: Grip the bar with a wide snatch-width grip and feet hip-width. Hinge to the bar with a flat back, then drive through the floor to stand tall without shrugging early. Keep the bar close and the lats engaged throughout.

snatch_grip_rdl.description: Hold a barbell with a wide snatch grip at hip height and push the hips back to lower the bar along the legs. Stop when you feel a hamstring stretch while keeping the back flat. Drive the hips forward to return upright.

soccer_toss.description: Stand facing a wall or partner and throw a medicine ball underhand with both hands like a soccer throw-in. Lean back slightly and follow through with hip rotation. Keep both feet grounded until release.

spanish_squat_iso.description: Loop a band behind the knees and lean back into it while sitting into a squat against the band tension. Hold the isometric with the shins vertical and the chest up. Breathe steadily and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

spanish_squats.description: Anchor a band behind the knees and squat while leaning back into the band to reduce knee shear. Descend until the thighs are parallel or as deep as comfort allows, then drive up through the mid-foot. Keep the torso upright and shins near vertical.

speed_russian_lunges.description: Perform curtsy or Russian-style lunges with a quick but controlled tempo, alternating legs each rep. Keep the front knee tracking over the foot and the torso tall. Avoid bouncing off the back knee at the bottom.

spider_man_push_up.description: Start in a push-up and lower the chest while bringing one knee toward the same-side elbow. Press back up and alternate legs each rep. Keep the hips low and avoid piking up as the knee comes forward.

split_stance_medball_side_toss.description: Stand in a split stance sideways to a wall with a medicine ball at the hip. Rotate through the hips and throw the ball into the wall, then catch and reload. Keep most of the weight on the back foot until the throw.

sprinter_sit_ups.description: Lie on your back and sit up while driving one knee toward the chest and the opposite arm forward in a sprint motion. Lower under control and alternate sides. Keep the movement crisp without pulling on the neck.

sprinter_step_up_launches.description: Face a box in a sprinter stance and drive explosively off the front foot to launch onto the platform. Drive the opposite knee up aggressively at takeoff. Land softly on the box and step down to reset.

sprinter_step_up_med_ball_toss.description: Perform a sprinter-style step-up onto a box while releasing a medicine ball to a target overhead or forward. Time the throw with the drive leg extension. Land balanced on the box before stepping down.

sprinter_step_ups.description: Stand facing a box in a staggered sprinter stance with one foot on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the chest up and avoid pushing off the back toes excessively.

squat.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width or as prescribed and sit the hips back and down while keeping the chest up. Descend until the thighs are at least parallel or to comfortable depth with heels down. Drive through the mid-foot to stand without letting the knees collapse inward.

squat_chops.description: Hold a medicine ball or cable handle at one hip in an athletic stance. Squat slightly and chop the load diagonally across the body by rotating through the hips and thoracic spine. Return with control and alternate sides if prescribed.

squat_jump.description: Sit into a squat and explode upward into a maximum vertical jump. Land softly with hips back and knees aligned, then either reset or flow into the next rep as prescribed. Keep the chest up and avoid collapsing forward on landing.

stagger_trap_bar_rdl.description: Stand inside a trap bar in a staggered stance and hinge forward with a flat back to grip the handles. Push the hips back until you feel a hamstring stretch, then drive forward to stand tall. Keep most of the load on the front foot and the bar centered.

staggered_box_jumps.description: Start in a staggered stance facing a box and jump onto it, switching or keeping the lead leg as prescribed. Land softly with both feet on the box and stand tall. Step down and reset the stagger before the next jump.

staggered_broad_jumps.description: Begin in a staggered stance and jump forward for distance, landing in an athletic position. Reset the feet to the stagger and repeat. Drive with both legs but emphasize the front leg push at takeoff.

staggered_pull_ups.description: Hang from the bar with hands staggered—one higher than the other—and pull the chin toward the top hand. Lower under control and switch hand positions each set or rep as prescribed. Keep the shoulders down at the start and avoid excessive swinging.

staggered_push_ups.description: Set up in a push-up with one hand forward and one hand back. Lower the chest between the hands and press back up while keeping the body in one line. Switch hand positions each set and avoid rotating the hips.

staggered_stance_rdl.description: Stand in a staggered stance holding dumbbells or a bar at the thighs. Hinge at the hips with a flat back until you feel a stretch in the front-leg hamstring, then return upright. Keep about 80% of the weight on the front foot.

staggered_stance_vertical_toss.description: Stand in a staggered stance holding a medicine ball at the chest. Dip and toss the ball vertically overhead using leg drive from the front foot. Catch or receive the ball and reset the stagger between reps.

stance_to_wall_drill.description: Start in an athletic stance a short distance from a wall and fall forward into the first sprint steps without breaking posture. Drive the knees and arms aggressively for the prescribed time or contacts. Keep the body angle consistent and avoid standing upright too soon.

standing_calf_raise.description: Stand on the balls of the feet on flat ground or a step with optional weight. Rise onto the toes as high as possible, pause briefly, then lower until you feel a calf stretch. Keep the knees straight and movement smooth without bouncing.

standing_calf_raise_on_wall.description: Face a wall for balance and perform calf raises on the balls of the feet through full range. Press all the way up onto the toes and lower under control. Keep the body tall and avoid leaning heavily on the wall.

static.description: Hold the prescribed isometric strength position—plank, split squat, bridge, or wall sit—from the session plan. Breathe steadily and keep joints stacked without sagging or locking aggressively. Reduce hold time if form breaks.

stationary_alternating_pogos.description: Hop in place alternating feet with quick stiff-ankle contacts and minimal ground time. Stay tall through the chest and keep jumps low and fast. Avoid letting the knees collapse inward on each landing.

step_ups.description: Stand facing a box or bench with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box without pushing off the back leg excessively. Step down under control and alternate legs.

stir_the_pot.description: Kneel or plank with forearms on a stability ball and roll the ball in small circles by moving the shoulders. Keep the core braced and hips steady so the lower back does not arch. Make circles slow and controlled in both directions.

stop_and_go.description: Sprint for a short distance, stick a hard deceleration on a line or cone, then immediately accelerate again. Stay low on the stop and push off the outside foot on the restart. Keep transitions crisp without drifting past the marker.

straight_arm_pulldowns.description: Stand facing a high cable with arms extended and a slight elbow bend fixed throughout. Pull the bar or rope down toward the thighs by driving the shoulders down and back. Control the return and avoid bending the elbows into a pressdown.

straight_leg_bounds.description: Bound forward with minimal knee bend, emphasizing long hamstring and glute contacts like a sprint drill. Stay tall and paw the ground under the hips. Keep the torso slightly forward and avoid sitting back on each landing.

straight_leg_raise.description: Lie on your back with legs extended and raise one or both legs toward the ceiling while keeping the low back pressed down. Lower slowly until you feel control challenge without the back arching. Keep the knees straight as prescribed.

suitcase_carry.description: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell in one hand like a suitcase and walk with tall posture. Brace the core to resist leaning toward the load and keep the shoulders level. Avoid shrugging the loaded side or letting the weight bang against the leg.

suitcase_lunges.description: Hold a weight in one hand at the side and step into forward or reverse lunges while staying upright. Keep the torso from tilting toward the load and the front knee over the foot. Alternate legs or complete one side before switching.

sumo_deadlift.description: Take a wide stance with toes turned out and grip the bar inside the knees. Drive through the floor while keeping the chest up and knees tracking over the toes. Stand tall without hyperextending the lower back at lockout.

sumo_squat.description: Stand with a wide stance and toes turned out, holding weight at the hips or goblet position if used. Squat between the knees while keeping the chest upright and heels down. Drive through the mid-foot and push the knees out in line with the toes.

super_man_dives.description: Lie face-down with arms extended overhead and lift the arms, chest, and legs off the floor simultaneously. Hold briefly or pulse at the top while keeping the neck neutral. Avoid cranking the head up and keep the movement from the upper back and glutes.

superman_holds.description: Lie face-down and raise the arms, chest, and legs to hold a superman position isometrically. Breathe steadily and keep the glutes lightly engaged. Stop if you feel pinching in the lower back.

supermans.description: Lie face-down with arms extended and lift the chest and legs slightly off the floor by squeezing the glutes and upper back. Lower with control and repeat without jerking. Keep the gaze down and neck neutral.

supine_chest_pass.description: Lie on your back with knees bent holding a medicine ball at the chest. Pass the ball explosively upward or toward a partner and absorb the return. Keep the low back neutral and avoid arching off the floor.

supine_scorpion.description: Lie on your back with arms out and lift one leg up and across the body toward the opposite hand. Keep both shoulders on the floor and rotate through the thoracic spine. Move slowly and avoid forcing the foot to the ground.

swiss_ball_hamstring_curl.description: Lie on your back with heels on a stability ball and hips lifted. Curl the ball toward the glutes by bending the knees, then extend the legs with control. Keep the hips high and avoid dropping the pelvis between reps.

tempo_run.description: Run at a steady controlled pace—often roughly half-marathon effort—for the prescribed duration or distance. Stay relaxed through the shoulders and land under the hips with quick cadence. Avoid sprinting early and keep breathing rhythmic.

thigh_pop_run.description: Sprint with an exaggerated thigh drive, popping each knee up aggressively while staying tall. Strike under the hips with stiff quick ground contacts. Keep the torso stable and avoid leaning back.

thoracic_open_books.description: Lie on your side with knees bent and both arms extended in front of you. Open the top arm toward the floor behind you, following with the eyes while keeping the knees stacked. Return slowly and breathe into the upper back rotation.

tibialis_raise.description: Stand with heels on the ground and toes on a wall or lean back against a support if needed. Dorsiflex the ankles to lift the toes toward the shins, then lower with control. Keep the legs straight and avoid rocking the hips back.

toe_walks.description: Walk forward on the toes with tall posture for the prescribed distance or time. Keep the ankles stiff and steps controlled without letting the heels touch down. Avoid excessive knee bend or leaning forward from the waist.

trap_bar_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar with feet hip-width and grip the handles at the sides. Hinge to the handles with a flat back, then drive through the floor to stand tall. Keep the lats engaged and the bar path vertical without shrugging at lockout.

trap_bar_deadlift_off_blocks.description: Set the trap bar on blocks or pins around mid-shin height and stand inside with a braced torso. Pull the handles to lockout by driving through the floor and squeezing the glutes. Keep the back flat and avoid jerking the bar off the blocks.

trap_bar_jumps.description: Stand inside a lightly loaded trap bar and dip quickly before jumping vertically with the hands fixed on the handles. Land softly with bent knees and reset between reps. Keep the core braced and avoid pulling with the arms.

trap_bar_rdl.description: Stand inside the trap bar with handles at the sides and push the hips back to hinge forward. Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch while keeping the back flat. Drive the hips forward to stand tall without rounding the upper back.

tricep_extensions.description: Attach a rope or bar to a high or mid cable and stand facing the stack with elbows pinned at the sides. Extend the forearms until the arms are straight, then bend back to about 90° under control. Keep the shoulders down and avoid swinging the torso.

trunk.description: Work through the prescribed trunk mobility or core activation series—dead bugs, pallof holds, rotations, or carries—from the session plan. Move with control and keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis. Stop if the lower back compensates for limited thoracic motion.

tuck_jump.description: Dip quickly and jump vertically, tucking both knees toward the chest at the peak. Land softly with hips back and knees aligned over the toes. Reset fully between reps if height or landing quality drops.

underhand_chin_ups.description: Hang from the bar with a shoulder-width underhand grip and pull until the chin clears the bar. Lower until the arms are fully extended with shoulders still engaged. Avoid kipping or swinging the legs for momentum.

unilateral_front_rack_kb_march_carry.description: Clean a kettlebell to a single-arm front rack and march forward with tall posture for the prescribed distance. Keep the elbow close and the ribs down while resisting side bend. Avoid leaning away from the bell or letting the wrist collapse.

unilateral_front_rack_kb_march_stationar.description: Hold a kettlebell in a single-arm front rack and march in place with high controlled knee lifts. Stay tall through the crown of the head and brace the core each step. Keep the elbow tight and avoid rotating the torso open.

unilateral_strength.description: Perform the prescribed single-leg or single-arm strength drill—split squat, RDL, press, or row variant—from the session plan.
